topic.label: 時態與時態一致
topic.scope: 完成進行式(has been V-ing)、敘述時態一致、未來完成式、時間/條件副詞子句用現在式表未來(When he comes...)、過去完成與過去簡單先後、by the time+時態

note.title: 時態與時態一致
note.summary: 英文的時態不只是「動作發生在何時」，更牽涉到「動作之間的先後」與「整句時態要彼此呼應」。本單元帶你掌握學測必考的六大重點：現在／過去完成進行式(has/had been V-ing)、敘述時態一致(sequence of tenses)、未來完成式(will have p.p.)、時間與條件副詞子句用『現在式』代替未來、過去完成與過去簡單的先後關係、以及 by the time 引導的時態搭配。只要抓住『主要子句決定基準時間，附屬子句配合它前後移動』這個核心觀念，時態就不再是死背，而是有邏輯可循。

sections.h: 完成進行式:has / had been V-ing(強調『持續』)
sections.body: 現在完成進行式 has/have been V-ing 用來強調一個動作『從過去某點持續到現在(且常常還在進行或剛結束)』，重點在『持續的過程或它造成的現在影響』。過去完成進行式 had been V-ing 則把這種持續往前推,表示『在過去某時間點之前,一直持續著的動作』。和單純完成式(has done)相比,完成進行式更凸顯『時間長度』與『持續性』。
examples: I have been preparing for the exam since last month, so I feel exhausted. 我從上個月就一直在準備考試,所以覺得筋疲力盡。
She had been waiting for the bus for half an hour when it finally came. 公車終於來時,她已經等了半個小時。
My eyes hurt because I have been staring at the screen all day. 我的眼睛好痛,因為我一整天都盯著螢幕。

sections.h: 敘述時態一致(Sequence of Tenses)
sections.body: 當主要子句是過去式(said, told, thought, knew...)時,後面 that 子句的時態通常要『往過去挪一格』以保持一致:現在→過去(is→was)、現在完成/過去→過去完成(has done/did→had done)、will→would。但有一個重要例外:如果子句陳述的是『不變的真理、科學事實或習慣性事實』,則保留現在式。
examples: He said that he was busy that day. 他說他那天很忙。(is→was)
The teacher told us that water boils at 100 degrees Celsius. 老師告訴我們水在攝氏一百度沸騰。(真理保留現在式)
She explained that she had already finished the project. 她解釋說她已經完成了那個專案。(過去完成表更早)

sections.h: 未來完成式:will have + p.p.
sections.body: 未來完成式 will have + 過去分詞,表示『到未來某個時間點之前,某動作已經完成』。常與 by + 未來時間、by the time + 現在式子句連用。若要強調到未來某點為止『持續了多久』,可用未來完成進行式 will have been V-ing。
examples: By the time you arrive, I will have finished cooking dinner. 等你到的時候,我已經把晚餐煮好了。
By 2030, engineers will have built the new high-speed rail. 到二○三○年,工程師將已建好新的高速鐵路。
Next month, I will have been learning English for ten years. 到下個月,我學英文就滿十年了。

sections.h: 時間/條件副詞子句:用現在式表未來
sections.body: 在 when, before, after, until, as soon as, once(時間)以及 if, unless(條件)所引導的副詞子句中,即使指的是未來,也要用『現在簡單式』代替 will,用『現在完成式』代替 will have done。主要子句才用 will。這是台灣學生最常錯的地方——千萬別在這些子句裡放 will。
examples: When he comes back, I will give him the message. 等他回來,我會把留言轉告他。(comes 不是 will come)
We won't start the meeting until everyone arrives. 大家都到之前,我們不會開始開會。
As soon as I have saved enough money, I will travel around the world. 一旦我存夠了錢,我就要環遊世界。

sections.h: 過去完成 vs 過去簡單:標記先後
sections.body: 當過去發生了兩件事,『先發生』的用過去完成式(had + p.p.),『後發生』的用過去簡單式,藉此清楚標記時間先後。常見連接詞:before, after, when, by the time。若用 before/after 已經明確點出先後,有時可省略過去完成而全用過去式,但『先發生的動作若要強調在前』仍以過去完成最保險。
examples: When I got to the platform, the train had already left. 我到月台時,火車已經開走了。(火車先走)
She had studied French before she moved to Paris. 她搬到巴黎之前就學過法文了。
After the guests had left, we cleaned up the living room. 客人離開後,我們清理了客廳。

sections.h: by the time 的時態搭配
sections.body: by the time(到...的時候)引導的是時間副詞子句,規則要看主要子句指向過去還是未來:(1)談未來:by the time 子句用『現在式』,主要子句用『未來完成式 will have done』。(2)談過去:by the time 子句用『過去簡單式』,主要子句用『過去完成式 had done』。掌握『子句時態較單純,主要子句用完成式表更早完成』即可。
examples: By the time the movie starts, we will have bought the popcorn. 電影開演前,我們就會買好爆米花。(未來)
By the time the firefighters arrived, the house had burned down. 消防員抵達時,房子已經燒毀了。(過去)
By the time you finish high school, you will have taken dozens of exams. 你高中畢業前,將已經考過幾十次試。

mistakes: 誤:When he will come, I will tell him. → 正:When he comes, I will tell him.(時間副詞子句用現在式代替未來)
誤:By the time we arrived, the concert already started. → 正:By the time we arrived, the concert had already started.(過去兩動作,先發生的用過去完成式)
誤:He said that the sun rose in the east. → 正:He said that the sun rises in the east.(不變真理保留現在式,不隨主句變過去)
誤:I have waited for two hours and I am still tired. → 正:I have been waiting for two hours.(強調持續過程用完成進行式)
誤:By next year I will finish this course. → 正:By next year I will have finished this course.(by + 未來時間點,表已完成用未來完成式)
note.tip: 口訣:『主句作主,子句配合;when/if 裡不放 will,先發生的加 had。』——主要子句定基準,附屬子句往前後靠。

d1.stem: When he ___ home tonight, please tell him to call me back.
d1.explanation: 這是 when 引導的時間副詞子句,即使指未來也要用現在簡單式代替 will,故選 comes。主要子句(please tell...)才是未來語氣。

d1.stem: By the time you read this note, I ___ already left for the airport.
d1.explanation: by the time 談未來時,主要子句用未來完成式 will have + p.p.(will have already left),表示到你讀信時我已經離開。

d1.stem: She ___ English for three hours; that's why she looks so tired.
d1.explanation: 從過去持續到現在、且強調『持續了三小時』並造成現在疲累的結果,用現在完成進行式 has been studying。

d1.stem: If it ___ tomorrow, we will cancel the outdoor picnic.
d1.explanation: if 引導的條件副詞子句用現在式代替未來,故用 rains;主要子句 will cancel 才用未來式。

d1.stem: By 2035, scientists ___ a cure for many rare diseases.
d1.explanation: by + 未來時間點(2035)表示『到那時已完成』,用未來完成式 will have found。

d1.stem: My brother said that he ___ tired after the basketball game.
d1.explanation: 主要子句是過去式 said,that 子句要時態一致往過去挪一格,is→was。

d1.stem: I ___ waiting at the bus stop for twenty minutes when the bus finally showed up.
d1.explanation: 描述過去某時點(公車來時)之前一直持續的動作,用過去完成進行式 had been waiting。

d1.stem: As soon as the bell ___, all the students rushed out of the classroom.
d1.explanation: 整句是過去的敘述(rushed out 為過去式),as soon as 子句也用過去簡單式 rang 保持時態一致。

d1.stem: Don't worry. We ___ dinner by the time you get home.
d1.explanation: by the time 談未來,主要子句用未來完成式 will have finished,表示你到家前我們已煮好。

d1.stem: The Lin family ___ in this town since 2010.
d1.explanation: since + 過去時間點,表示從過去持續到現在,用現在完成式 have lived。

d1.stem: Before I moved to Taipei, I ___ in Tainan for twelve years.
d1.explanation: 過去有兩件事:『住台南』先於『搬台北(moved)』,先發生的用過去完成式 had lived。

d1.stem: Please wait here until the teacher ___ back.
d1.explanation: until 引導時間副詞子句,指未來也要用現在式代替 will,故用 comes。

d2.stem: By the time the firefighters arrived, the old warehouse ___ completely.
d2.explanation: by the time 談過去,子句用過去式(arrived),主要子句用過去完成式 had burned down,表示消防員到之前倉庫已燒毀。

d2.stem: Our chemistry teacher reminded us that water ___ at zero degrees Celsius.
d2.explanation: 雖然主要子句是過去式 reminded,但『水在零度結冰』是不變的科學事實,例外保留現在式 freezes。

d2.stem: My hand really hurts because I ___ essays all morning.
d2.explanation: 強調『整個早上持續書寫』的過程並造成現在手痛的影響,用現在完成進行式 have been writing 最貼切。

d2.stem: She ___ the science report by the time the teacher collects the assignments tomorrow.
d2.explanation: by the time 談未來,子句用現在式(collects),主要子句用未來完成式 will have finished。

d2.stem: During the interview, he asked me whether I ___ abroad before.
d2.explanation: 主要子句 asked 是過去式,間接問句中『之前的經驗』要比過去更早,用過去完成式 had traveled。

d2.stem: Look at those dark clouds gathering! I'm sure it ___ any minute.
d2.explanation: 根據眼前的徵兆(烏雲)推測即將發生的事,用 be going to,故選 is going to rain;rains 為習慣性事實不合語境。

d2.stem: When I finally reached the platform, the train ___ already ___.
d2.explanation: 到月台(reached, 過去)時火車『更早』就走了,先發生用過去完成式 had already left。

d2.stem: Next April, my grandparents ___ married for exactly fifty years.
d2.explanation: 表示『到未來某時點為止已持續多久』,用未來完成式 will have been married for fifty years。

d2.stem: The manager won't sign the contract until she ___ every detail carefully.
d2.explanation: until 引導的時間副詞子句用現在式代替未來,故選 reviews;主要子句 won't sign 才是未來否定。

d2.stem: The concert ___ by the time we managed to find our seats.
d2.explanation: 我們找到座位(過去)時,音樂會『更早』就開始了,用過去完成式 had already begun。

d2.stem: He looks worn out; he ___ for the marathon every morning this week.
d2.explanation: this week 這段期間持續反覆訓練且與現在狀態相關,強調持續性用現在完成進行式 has been training。

d2.stem: I promise I ___ you as soon as I ___ any news about the results.
d2.explanation: 主要子句表未來用 will call;as soon as 時間子句用現在式代替未來,故用 hear。第二選項在子句誤放 will,錯誤。

d3.stem: Hardly ___ the last question when the bell rang to end the exam.
d3.explanation: Hardly...when 句型表『一...就...』,否定副詞置句首須倒裝,且『先發生』用過去完成式,故為 Hardly had she answered。

d3.stem: It is the first time in my life that I ___ such a breathtaking sunrise.
d3.explanation: 『It is the first time (that)...』固定接現在完成式,表示到目前為止的第一次經驗,用 have seen。

d3.stem: Not until he failed the mock exam ___ how little he had actually prepared.
d3.explanation: 『Not until...』置於句首,主要子句須部分倒裝(助動詞提前),故為 did he realize;had he realized 語意時序不符。

d3.stem: By the end of this semester, our class ___ five full-length practice tests for the exam.
d3.explanation: by the end of...(未來時間)表『到那時已完成』,用未來完成式 will have completed。

d3.stem: ___ in Kaohsiung for over a decade, she still speaks with a slight northern accent.
d3.explanation: 分詞構句中,動作(住)先於主句(仍帶口音)且為主動,用完成分詞 Having lived 表較早發生且持續。

d3.stem: No sooner ___ the classroom than the fire alarm suddenly went off.
d3.explanation: No sooner...than 表『一...就...』,否定詞置句首須倒裝且先發生用過去完成式,故為 No sooner had we entered。

d3.stem: By this time next year, I ___ at this cram school for exactly three years.
d3.explanation: 『到未來某時點為止持續了多久』並強調持續性,用未來完成進行式 will have been studying。

d3.stem: If I ___ that you were coming to visit, I would have cleaned up my room in advance.
d3.explanation: 與過去事實相反的假設(第三類條件句),if 子句用過去完成式 had known,主句用 would have + p.p.。

d3.stem: Little ___ that a single decision that morning would change the course of his life forever.
d3.explanation: 否定副詞 Little 置句首,主要子句須倒裝(助動詞提前),故為 Little did he know。

d3.stem: Only after the typhoon had passed ___ to assess the full extent of the damage.
d3.explanation: 『Only after...』置句首,主要子句須倒裝,主詞 we 為複數配 were,故為 were we able。

d3.stem: She told the reporters that by the time the new stadium opened, the city ___ hosting international games for years.
d3.explanation: 主句 told 為過去,時態一致下未來完成(will have been)須改為 would have been,呼應過去的敘述視角。

topic.label: 助動詞與語氣
topic.scope: must/may/might/can't be 表推測、should/ought to/must have p.p.表過去推測與後悔責備、would rather(+過去式)、had better、used to vs be/get used to、need/dare

note.title: 助動詞與語氣
note.summary: 助動詞不只表能力或義務，更是英語中「說話者態度」的核心工具。本單元聚焦兩大主軸：一是用 must / may / might / can't 對現在與過去做「推測」，其中過去推測要用「助動詞 + have + p.p.」；二是用 should have / must have / needn't have 表達過去的「後悔、責備與其實不必」。此外整理易混淆的 would rather（+過去式假設）、had better（勸告警告）、used to vs be/get used to（過去習慣 vs 習慣於），以及 need 與 dare 的助動詞用法。掌握這些，就能精準表達確定程度與情緒語氣，是學測文法與閱讀的高頻考點。

sections.h: 一、對「現在」的推測：must / may / might / can't be
sections.body: 依把握程度由高到低：must be（一定是，肯定推測）＞may/might be（可能是，不確定）＞can't be（不可能是，否定推測）。注意否定的肯定推測用 can't，而非 mustn't；mustn't 是「禁止」，不是推測。這些後面都接原形動詞或 be。
examples: The office is dark; everyone must be gone. 辦公室黑漆漆的，大家一定都走了。
She isn't answering — she may be in a meeting. 她沒接電話，可能正在開會。
That can't be his real name; it sounds fake. 那不可能是他的真名，聽起來很假。

sections.h: 二、對「過去」的推測：助動詞 + have + p.p.
sections.body: 要推測「過去發生的事」，就把 be 換成 have + 過去分詞：must have p.p.（過去一定…）、may/might have p.p.（過去可能…）、can't have p.p.（過去不可能…）。這是學測最愛考的結構，關鍵在於句中通常有過去的線索（如已完成的動作、殘留的證據）。
examples: The ground is wet; it must have rained last night. 地面是濕的，昨晚一定下過雨。
He might have missed the bus; that's why he's late. 他可能沒趕上公車，所以才遲到。
She can't have finished the whole book in one hour. 她不可能一小時就讀完整本書。

sections.h: 三、後悔與責備：should have / needn't have / must have p.p.
sections.body: should have + p.p. 表「過去該做卻沒做」（懊悔或責備）；shouldn't have + p.p. 表「過去不該做卻做了」；needn't have + p.p. 表「過去做了其實不必做的事」。三者都在檢討已經發生、無法改變的過去，情緒色彩濃厚。
examples: You should have studied harder for the test. 你考試前該更用功的。
I shouldn't have said those hurtful words. 我不該說那些傷人的話。
You needn't have bought so much; we already had food. 你不必買那麼多，我們本來就有食物了。

sections.h: 四、選擇與勸告：would rather、had better
sections.body: would rather + 原形＝寧願（做某事）；表達對「別人」的期望時用 would rather + 主詞 + 過去式（假設語氣），如 I'd rather you didn't。had better + 原形＝最好（帶勸告與警告），否定為 had better not；常搭配 or 表「否則」。兩者都是助動詞片語，後接原形。
examples: I would rather walk than wait for the late bus. 我寧願走路也不要等誤點的公車。
I'd rather you didn't tell anyone about this. 我寧願你別把這件事告訴任何人。
You'd better hurry, or you'll be late for class. 你最好快一點，否則上課要遲到了。

sections.h: 五、過去習慣 vs 習慣於：used to、be/get used to
sections.body: used to + 原形＝過去經常（現在已不然），否定 didn't use to；be used to + V-ing/名詞＝已經習慣於某狀態；get/become used to + V-ing＝逐漸習慣。關鍵差異：used to 後接原形講「過去的習慣」，而 be used to 的 to 是介系詞，後接動名詞講「適應、習慣」。
examples: I used to play basketball every day after school. 我以前放學後每天打籃球。
She is used to studying late at night. 她已經習慣熬夜念書。
It takes time to get used to a new environment. 適應新環境需要時間。

sections.h: 六、need 與 dare 的助動詞用法
sections.body: need 與 dare 可作助動詞（主要用於否定與疑問）：needn't + 原形＝不必；needn't have + p.p.＝過去其實不必；dare not / daren't + 原形＝不敢。它們也能當一般動詞（need to、dare to），但作助動詞時後面直接接原形、不加 to。
examples: You needn't worry; everything is under control. 你不必擔心，一切都在掌握中。
He dared not look his father in the eye. 他不敢正視父親的眼睛。
We needn't have rushed; the train was delayed anyway. 我們其實不必趕，火車反正誤點了。

mistakes: 誤：It must rained last night. → 正：It must have rained last night.（推測過去要用 must have + p.p.，不能只加過去式）
誤：He can't be at school yesterday. → 正：He can't have been at school yesterday.（過去的否定推測用 can't have p.p.）
誤：I'm used to get up early.／She used to getting up early. → 正：I'm used to getting up early.（習慣於接 V-ing）／She used to get up early.（過去習慣接原形）
誤：You mustn't be tired after the long trip. → 正：You must be tired after the long trip.（肯定推測用 must be；mustn't 是禁止，不是推測）
誤：I would rather to stay home.／I'd rather you don't go. → 正：I would rather stay home.（接原形）／I'd rather you didn't go.（對他人期望用過去式假設）
note.tip: 推測看時間軸：講「現在」用「助動詞 + be」，講「過去」一律「助動詞 + have + p.p.」；把握程度 must ＞ may/might ＞ can't。

d1.stem: Look at the dark clouds! It ___ rain soon, so take an umbrella.
d1.explanation: 「may + 原形」表示對現在或未來的推測「可能會」。看到烏雲推測「可能下雨」，故選 may。

d1.stem: The lights in the classroom are all off. Nobody ___ be inside now.
d1.explanation: 燈全關表示裡面不可能有人。主詞 Nobody 已含否定意味，須搭配 can：「Nobody can be inside」＝不可能有人在裡面。若再用 can't 會形成雙重否定，不合標準英語；must be 為肯定推測、need 用法不合，皆不選。

d1.stem: Amy studies English for six hours every day, so she ___ be very hardworking.
d1.explanation: 根據充分證據做肯定推測「一定是」，用 must be。can't 與 might not 為否定，語意不合。

d1.stem: I ___ go to bed early tonight because I have an important exam tomorrow.
d1.explanation: had better + 原形，表「最好」帶有勸告與警告。此句建議早睡以備考，故選 had better。

d1.stem: There ___ a small bookstore on this corner, but it closed last year and is gone now.
d1.explanation: used to be 表「過去曾存在、現在已不在」。已於去年歇業，故 There used to be。be used to 意為「習慣於」，語意不合。

d1.stem: When he was a child, he ___ eat vegetables, but now he loves them.
d1.explanation: 「過去不習慣（過去的習慣）」用 didn't use to + 原形（否定時 used 去 d 變 use）。be used to 意為「習慣於」，語意與時態不符。

d1.stem: You ___ worry about the result; you did your best.
d1.explanation: needn't + 原形表「不必」。此句安慰對方「不必擔心」，故選 needn't。

d1.stem: I would rather ___ at home than go out in this stormy weather.
d1.explanation: would rather + 原形，表「寧願」。此句主詞自己的選擇用現在，故 would rather stay（原形）。

d1.stem: It ___ be John at the door. He said he would come around this time.
d1.explanation: 對不太確定的事做推測「可能是」，用 might be。搭配「他說會來」的線索，四選項中僅 might 語意相合；can't／must not 為否定推測，與線索矛盾。

d1.stem: We ___ to finish the project before Friday, or the teacher will be angry.
d1.explanation: ought 後須接 to（ought to + 原形），表「應該」。空格後有 to，其餘 must/should/had better 後接原形不加 to，故選 ought。

d2.stem: The floor is wet and there are footprints. Someone ___ here just a minute ago.
d2.explanation: 對過去做肯定推測「一定曾在」用 must have + p.p.。地板濕、有腳印是過去的證據，故 must have been。

d2.stem: I can't find my phone anywhere. I ___ it on the bus this morning.
d2.explanation: 對過去行為的肯定推測「一定是把…忘在」用 must have left。手機找不到，推測早上遺留在公車上。

d2.stem: You ___ told me earlier that you were sick; I would have taken you to the doctor.
d2.explanation: should have + p.p. 表「過去該做而未做」的責備或後悔。此句責備對方沒早點說，故 should have told。

d2.stem: Ken looks exhausted this morning. He ___ much sleep last night.
d2.explanation: 對過去的否定推測「不可能有」用 can't have + p.p.。看他很累，推測昨晚「不可能睡很多」。

d2.stem: I'm not used to ___ up so early; back in high school I would sleep until noon on weekends.
d2.explanation: be used to 的 to 是介系詞，後接動名詞 V-ing。「不習慣這麼早起」用 used to getting up。

d2.stem: Rather than ___ the crowded night market, they would rather ___ a quiet walk in the park.
d2.explanation: would rather + 原形（take）；rather than 與主要動詞對等，依平行結構亦接原形（visit）。故兩處皆用原形 visit / take；含 taking 者不合 would rather 用法。

d2.stem: You ___ have worked so hard last week; the deadline was actually next month.
d2.explanation: shouldn't have + p.p. 表「過去做了不該做的事」帶懊悔或責備。截止其實是下個月，所以「不該那麼拼」。

d2.stem: When I was young I ___ afraid of dogs, but now I ___ having them around.
options: used to be / am used to
was using to / am used to
d2.explanation: 「過去曾…」用 used to be（過去狀態）；「現在習慣於」用 be used to + V-ing。故 used to be / am used to having。

d2.stem: It ___ have been Maria who called; she's been abroad without any signal all week.
d2.explanation: 她整週在國外沒訊號，對過去做否定推測「不可能是」，用 can't have been。

d2.stem: We'd better ___ now, ___ we'll miss the last train home.
d2.explanation: had better + 原形（leave）；「否則」用 or。had better ... or ... 為固定警告句型。

d2.stem: She ___ not come to the party — she never replied, but I'm honestly not sure.
d2.explanation: 「我不確定」顯示是低把握推測，might not come 表「可能不會來」。must/can't 語氣太肯定。

d2.stem: You needn't ___ so much food; there were only three of us at dinner.
d2.explanation: needn't have + p.p. 表「過去做了其實不必做的事」。晚餐只有三人卻煮太多，故 needn't have cooked。

d3.stem: "Where's your brother?" "I'm not sure. He ___ still be at the gym, or he ___ have already left — I really can't tell."
d3.explanation: 說話者表明「說不準」，前後都是低把握推測：might be（現在可能）與 might have left（過去可能已離開），故兩處皆 might。

d3.stem: Had I known you were coming, I ___ the guest room; I'm sorry it's such a mess.
d3.explanation: 倒裝 Had I known 為與過去事實相反的條件（=If I had known）。主句需與過去相反的完成式，四選項中僅 should have cleaned 表「當時該整理而沒整理」，符合致歉語境；must have cleaned 為過去推測、had better clean 為現在，皆不合。

d3.stem: Not until he failed the exam ___ that he should have listened to his teacher's advice.
d3.explanation: 否定副詞 Not until 置句首須倒裝，助動詞提前：did he realize。子句 should have listened 表「過去該聽而沒聽」，全句語意一致。

d3.stem: Rather than ___ someone who lies, I would rather ___ alone for the rest of my life.
d3.explanation: would rather + 原形（be）；rather than 與之對等，亦接原形（marry）。故 marry / be，表「寧願單身也不願嫁給說謊者」。

d3.stem: The witness insisted the thief ___ tall, but the security footage shows a short man, so she ___ mistaken.
d3.explanation: 證人堅稱小偷「（過去）很高」用直述 was；但影片顯示矮個，推斷她「一定看錯了」用 must have been mistaken，肯定的過去推測。

d3.stem: So exhausted ___ that he fell asleep the moment he sat down; he ___ have slept at all the night before.
d3.explanation: So + 形容詞置句首須倒裝：So exhausted was he。後半空格後已有 have，對過去否定推測「前一晚根本沒睡」用 can't（→ can't have slept），故選 was he / can't。

d3.stem: "You ___ have told me the meeting was cancelled!" "But I did text you — you just never checked your phone."
d3.explanation: might have told 在口語中可表責備「你大可（本該）告訴我」。此語境是抱怨對方未告知，might have + p.p. 最貼切。

d3.stem: Only after moving to Tokyo ___ how much she ___ the slow pace of her hometown.
options: did she understand / was used to enjoying
did she understand / used to enjoy
d3.explanation: Only after 置句首須倒裝：did she understand。「過去（曾）喜歡」用 used to enjoy（過去習慣，接原形），故選 did she understand / used to enjoy。

d3.stem: He spoke as if he ___ the whole story himself, but honestly he ___ there — he was in another city that day.
d3.explanation: as if 表與過去事實相反用過去完成 had witnessed；後半「那天他在別的城市」，對過去否定推測「不可能在場」用 can't have been there。

d3.stem: I'd rather you ___ my parents about the accident; they ___ worry themselves sick.
d3.explanation: would rather + 主詞 + 過去式，表對他人現在或未來的期望（假設語氣），故 I'd rather you didn't tell；後半 would worry 表推測性結果「會擔心得要命」。

d3.stem: After years abroad, she still isn't quite used to ___ on the left, though she ___ to it now more than before.
d3.explanation: be used to + V-ing（習慣於）故 used to driving；後半「正逐漸習慣」用進行式 is getting used to，表適應中的動態過程。

topic.label: 進階被動語態
topic.scope: 使役與感官動詞被動(be made to)、授與動詞雙受詞被動、片語動詞被動(be looked after)、by以外介系詞(be known for/as/to, be filled with)、It is said that.../be said to、完成式被動

note.title: 進階被動語態
note.summary: 被動語態不只是「be + 過去分詞」而已。到了學測程度，你會遇到六大進階變化：使役與感官動詞被動時「消失的 to 要回來」、授與動詞的雙受詞可以有兩種被動寫法、片語動詞被動時介系詞絕不能掉、by 以外的固定介系詞搭配(known for/as/to、filled with)、傳聞句型 It is said that.../be said to，以及完成式與情態的被動(have been done、must have been done)。掌握這些規則，長難句的閱讀與寫作都會更精準。

sections.h: 使役與感官動詞被動——消失的 to 要回來
sections.body: make、see、hear、watch、feel 等在主動時後面接「原形動詞」(make sb do、see sb do)，但變成被動時，原本省略的 to 必須『還原』，變成 be made/seen/heard to + 原形。感官動詞若強調動作正在進行，被動可保留 V-ing。此外 let 沒有被動形，通常改用 be allowed to。
examples: The coach made us run ten laps. → We were made to run ten laps. 教練逼我們跑十圈。
Someone saw him leave the lab. → He was seen to leave the lab. 有人看見他離開實驗室。
My parents let me travel alone. → I am allowed to travel alone. 我父母讓我獨自旅行。

sections.h: 授與動詞雙受詞的兩種被動
sections.body: give、send、offer、teach、show、tell 等有兩個受詞(間接的人 + 直接的物)，兩者都能當被動主詞，因此有兩種被動句。以『物』當主詞時，後面接人要加介系詞 to(或 for)；以『人』當主詞時，直接接物，不需要介系詞。
examples: The teacher gave me some advice. → I was given some advice. / Some advice was given to me. 老師給我一些建議。
They offered her the job. → She was offered the job. 他們提供她這份工作。
Grandpa told us a story. → We were told a story. 爺爺講了個故事給我們聽。

sections.h: 片語動詞被動——介系詞不能掉
sections.body: look after、take care of、laugh at、listen to、deal with、look up to 等片語動詞要當成一個整體。變成被動時，後面的介系詞或副詞不可以省略，必須緊跟在過去分詞之後。這是台灣學生最常漏掉的地方。
examples: Everyone laughed at his mistake. → His mistake was laughed at. 他的錯誤被大家嘲笑。
A nurse takes care of the patients. → The patients are taken care of. 病患受到照顧。
Students look up to that professor. → That professor is looked up to. 那位教授受學生景仰。

sections.h: by 以外的介系詞——固定搭配要背熟
sections.body: 被動的動作者通常用 by，但有一群動詞是固定搭配別的介系詞：be known for(因某特色而聞名)、be known as(以某身分著稱)、be known to(為某人所知)；還有 be filled with(充滿)、be covered with(覆蓋)、be made of/from(由…製成)、be interested in、be satisfied with、be crowded with 等。這些介系詞不能隨便換成 by。
examples: Tainan is known for its delicious food. 台南以美食聞名。
Jay Chou is known as the King of Mandopop. 周杰倫以華語流行樂天王著稱。
The glass was filled with fresh orange juice. 玻璃杯裝滿新鮮柳橙汁。

sections.h: It is said that... 與 be said to——傳聞句型
sections.body: 要表達『據說、據信、據報導』有兩種寫法：一是 It is said/believed/reported that + 完整子句；二是把子句主詞提前，變成『主詞 + be said/believed/reported to + 原形』。若傳聞的內容比主要動詞更早發生，to 之後要用『have + 過去分詞』來表達較早的時間。
examples: It is said that she speaks five languages. = She is said to speak five languages. 據說她會說五種語言。
It is believed that the castle was built in 1600. = The castle is believed to have been built in 1600. 據信這座城堡建於一六〇〇年。
It is reported that the star has left the country. = The star is reported to have left the country. 據報導那位明星已離開該國。

sections.h: 完成式與情態的被動
sections.body: 完成式被動 = have/has/had been + 過去分詞；情態動詞被動 = 情態助動詞 + be + 過去分詞；情態完成被動(對過去的推測) = 情態 + have been + 過去分詞。進行式被動則用 be being + 過去分詞(is being cleaned)。關鍵是別漏掉那個 been。
examples: They have repaired the road. → The road has been repaired. 這條路已被修好。
Someone must have stolen my bike. → My bike must have been stolen. 我的腳踏車一定被偷了。
The classroom is being cleaned right now. 教室現在正被打掃。

mistakes: 誤：He was made work harder. → 正：He was made to work harder.(使役動詞變被動，被省略的 to 要還原)
誤：The baby is taken care. → 正：The baby is taken care of.(片語動詞被動時介系詞 of 不能掉)
誤：Jade Mountain is known as its sunrise. → 正：Jade Mountain is known for its sunrise.(因某特色聞名要用 for，known as 是接身分)
誤：The bridge has built since 2020. → 正：The bridge has been built since 2020.(完成式被動必須是 has been + 過去分詞)
note.tip: 口訣:主動省 to、被動 to 回來;片語別掉介系詞;傳聞比主句更早,就用 to have + 過去分詞。

d1.stem: The naughty students were made ___ the classroom after school.
d1.explanation: 使役動詞 make 主動時接原形(make sb clean)，變成被動後原本省略的 to 必須還原，成為 be made to + 原形，故選 to clean。

d1.stem: This famous poem was written ___ a well-known poet during the war.
d1.explanation: 被動語態中，動作的執行者(這裡是詩人)前面用介系詞 by，故選 by。

d1.stem: A brand-new bike was given ___ the winner of the singing contest.
d1.explanation: give 的雙受詞被動：當『物』(bike)當主詞時，後面接『人』要加介系詞 to，故選 to。

d1.stem: The lonely old lady is well ___ by her kind grandchildren.
d1.explanation: 片語動詞 look after(照顧)變被動時，介系詞 after 不可省略，且動詞用過去分詞 looked，故選 looked after。

d1.stem: Jade Mountain is known ___ its beautiful sunrise every year.
d1.explanation: be known for 表『因某特色而聞名』，日出是玉山的特色，故用 for。

d1.stem: The thief was seen ___ the shop window late last night.
d1.explanation: 感官動詞 see 主動接原形，變被動後 to 要還原，成為 be seen to + 原形，故選 to break。

d1.stem: The classroom was filled ___ excited students before the party started.
d1.explanation: be filled with 是固定搭配，表『充滿』，介系詞用 with，故選 with。

d1.stem: Notice letters have already been ___ to all the parents about the field trip.
d1.explanation: 完成式被動 have been + 過去分詞，send 的過去分詞是 sent，故選 sent。

d1.stem: Spider-Man is known ___ a superhero who protects New York City.
d1.explanation: be known as 表『以某身分著稱』，蜘蛛人是以超級英雄的身分聞名，故用 as。

d1.stem: All the homework must be ___ before this Friday afternoon.
d1.explanation: 情態動詞被動：情態助動詞(must) + be + 過去分詞，故選 finished。

d1.stem: English is spoken ___ millions of people all over the world.
d1.explanation: 被動語態的動作執行者(說英語的人)前用 by，故選 by。

d1.stem: The students were shown ___ a short film about climate change in class.
d1.explanation: show 雙受詞被動：以『人』(students)當主詞時，後面直接接『物』，不需加任何介系詞，故選空白。

d2.stem: When we were little, we were often made ___ our vegetables before we could have dessert.
d2.explanation: make 變被動時要還原 to，成為 be made to eat；選項 eat 是最常見的錯誤形式(誤把原形留著)。

d2.stem: Madame Curie is known ___ scientists everywhere as a great pioneer of her time.
d2.explanation: be known to sb 表『為某人所知』，此處『為各地科學家所知』用 to；後面另有 as a pioneer 才是接身分。

d2.stem: The shy boy was laughed ___ by his classmates because of his funny haircut.
d2.explanation: 片語動詞 laugh at(嘲笑)變被動仍保留介系詞 at，成為 be laughed at by，故選 at。

d2.stem: By last winter, the whole mountain top was covered ___ thick white snow.
d2.explanation: be covered with 是固定搭配，表『被…覆蓋』，介系詞用 with，故選 with。

d2.stem: It is widely ___ that doing regular exercise can improve our memory.
d2.explanation: 傳聞句型 It is believed that...(據信…)，be 之後用過去分詞 believed，故選 believed。

d2.stem: The ancient temple is believed ___ over three hundred years ago by local craftsmen.
d2.explanation: 傳聞內容(建造)比主句動詞更早發生且為被動，故用完成式被動不定詞 to have been built。

d2.stem: The new bridge ___ since 2021, so cars are able to cross it now.
d2.explanation: since 搭配現在完成式，且橋是『被建造』，故用完成式被動 has been built。has built 漏了 been，是常見錯誤。

d2.stem: This traditional dress is made ___ silk; you can still clearly see the original threads.
d2.explanation: be made of 用於『看得出原料』的情況；因為還看得見絲線，材質沒有質變，故用 of。made from 用於原料已改變(看不出來)時。

d2.stem: The little children are being ___ by a kind nurse while their parents are at work.
d2.explanation: 現在進行式被動 be being + 過去分詞，且 look after 的介系詞不可掉，故選 looked after。

d2.stem: The old love letter was said ___ by a young soldier a hundred years ago.
d2.explanation: be said to 的內容(書寫)比主句更早且為被動，故用完成式被動不定詞 to have been written。

d2.stem: A full scholarship was offered ___ the student who got the highest score in the exam.
d2.explanation: offer 雙受詞被動，以『物』(scholarship)當主詞時，後接『人』要加 to；選項 by 是易混淆的動作者介系詞。

d3.stem: Not only ___ to apologize in front of the class, but he was also asked to pay for the broken window.
d3.explanation: Not only 置於句首要倒裝，助動詞提前；又是使役被動 be made to，主詞是 he，故為 was he made。

d3.stem: Never before ___ our small temple been visited by so many tourists as it is this year.
d3.explanation: Never before 句首要倒裝；配合『as it is this year』用現在完成式被動 has been visited，倒裝後助動詞 has 提前，故選 has。

d3.stem: The mysterious novel, ___ to have been written in the 18th century, is now seen as a masterpiece.
d3.explanation: 此為省略關係代名詞的簡化結構，原為 which is said to...，被動的分詞用 said，故選 said。

d3.stem: By the time the firefighters finally arrived, the whole wooden house ___ by the flames.
d3.explanation: 『在消防員到達之前』房子已先被燒毀，比過去更早，用過去完成式被動 had been destroyed。

d3.stem: The old scientist is known ___ very few people today, even though his invention once changed the world.
d3.explanation: be known to sb 表『為某人所知』，此處『只為少數人所知』用 to。for(因…聞名)是誘答陷阱。

d3.stem: The prisoners were made ___ for hours under the burning sun without any rest or water.
d3.explanation: make 變被動要還原 to，成為 be made to stand。原形 stand 是最常見的誘答錯誤。

d3.stem: ___ that the ancient castle on the hill is haunted, which is why almost no one dares to enter it.
d3.explanation: 傳聞句型『據說』用 It is said that + 子句，為固定的被動結構，故選 It is said。

d3.stem: The huge stadium ___ with cheering fans long before the final match was about to begin.
d3.explanation: be filled with 表『充滿』且為被動，配合過去時間點用 was filled。filled(主動)語意與結構皆不合。

d3.stem: The missing painting is believed ___ by a professional thief several years ago at midnight.
d3.explanation: 偷竊發生在過去(比主句更早)且畫是『被偷』，用完成式被動不定詞 to have been stolen。

d3.stem: According to the safety rule, small children should never ___ in a hot car alone, even for a few minutes.
d3.explanation: 情態動詞被動 should + be + 過去分詞；孩子是『被留下』，故用 be left。leave(主動)語意相反。

d3.stem: The president ___ to have already left the country by the time the huge scandal broke out in the news.
d3.explanation: 傳聞句型 be reported to(據報導)為被動，主詞總統是『被報導』，故用 is reported；reported(主動)不對。

d3.stem: Had the warning email ___ a day earlier, the students would have prepared much better for the surprise test.
d3.explanation: 這是與過去事實相反的假設，if 省略後倒裝為 Had + 主詞 + been + 過去分詞(被動)，故選 been sent。

topic.label: 使役與感官動詞
topic.scope: make/have/let + O + 原形、get + O + to V、have/get + O + p.p.(被動)、see/hear/watch/feel + O + do vs doing、help + (to) do、感官動詞被動轉 to V

note.title: 使役與感官動詞
note.summary: 使役動詞（make/have/let/get）和感官動詞（see/hear/watch/feel/notice...）是學測與段考的高頻文法。核心在於：後面接的「受詞補語」該用『原形動詞』、『to + 原形』、『現在分詞 V-ing』還是『過去分詞 p.p.』。判斷關鍵有三個：(1) 是哪一個動詞—make/have/let 接原形、get 接 to V；(2) 受詞和後面動作是主動還是被動—主動用原形/V-ing，被動用 p.p.；(3) 動作是『完整看到全程』還是『看到進行中一瞬間』—前者用原形、後者用 V-ing。此外，改成被動語態時，原本省略的 to 要『還原』，這是最容易失分的陷阱。掌握這套流程，就能穩定拿下相關題目。

sections.h: 1. make / have / let + 受詞 + 原形動詞
sections.body: 這三個是最典型的『使役動詞』，意思分別是 make(強迫/使)、have(要求/使)、let(允許/讓)。它們後面的受詞補語一律用『原形動詞（bare infinitive）』，絕對不加 to、也不加 ing。這是本單元最基本、也是選擇題最愛考的規則。只要看到 make/have/let 當使役用，後面接『人做某事』就是原形。
examples: The teacher made us rewrite the whole essay. 老師要我們把整篇作文重寫一遍。
My mom won't let me stay up late before an exam. 考試前我媽不讓我熬夜。
I'll have my classmate check the answers for me. 我會請同學幫我核對答案。

sections.h: 2. get + 受詞 + to V；help + (to) V
sections.body: get 雖然常翻成『使、讓』，意思和 make 很像，但用法不同：get 後面必須接『to + 原形』，也就是 get + O + to V。這是最常見的誘答陷阱—很多人誤把 get 當成使役動詞而漏掉 to。另外 help 很特別，後面接原形或 to 原形『兩者皆可』（help someone do / help someone to do），美式英文更常直接省略 to。
examples: I finally got my brother to help me move the desk. 我終於說動我弟幫我搬書桌。
How did you get the teacher to change your grade? 你是怎麼讓老師改你成績的？
This app helps students (to) memorize new words faster. 這個 App 幫助學生更快記住新單字。

sections.h: 3. have / get + 受詞 + 過去分詞（被動使役：請人做／東西被處理）
sections.body: 當受詞是『物』、而且這件事是『被別人處理／被完成』時，補語要用『過去分詞 p.p.』，表示被動。have something done ＝ get something done，意思是『請人把某物…、讓某物被…』。這裡的重點是判斷主被動：車子是『被修』、頭髮是『被剪』，所以用 p.p.，不能用原形。這也常用來表達『遭遇不幸的事』（如 have my phone stolen 手機被偷）。
examples: I need to have my computer repaired before the report is due. 報告截止前我得把電腦拿去修。
She got her hair cut short for the summer. 她為了夏天把頭髮剪短了。
He had his wallet stolen on the crowded bus. 他在擁擠的公車上錢包被偷了。

sections.h: 4. 感官動詞 see / hear / watch / feel / notice + 受詞 + 原形 vs V-ing
sections.body: 感官動詞後面接『人／物 + 動作』時，補語有兩種：用『原形動詞』表示看到／聽到『動作的全程或完整事件』；用『現在分詞 V-ing』表示看到／聽到『動作正在進行中的一瞬間』，強調動態、進行感。兩者常常都對，要看語意。例如 I saw him cross the street.（看他走完整條街）vs I saw him crossing the street.（看到他正在過馬路的畫面）。切記：感官動詞後絕對不接 to V（主動時）。
examples: I heard the whole class cheer when the bell rang. 鐘聲一響，我聽到全班歡呼。
As I passed by, I saw her studying alone in the library. 我經過時，看到她獨自在圖書館唸書。
We felt the ground shake violently during the earthquake. 地震時我們感覺到地面劇烈搖晃。

sections.h: 5. 被動語態：使役與感官動詞後的『to 要還原』
sections.body: 這是最容易被扣分的進階陷阱。主動時 make/see/hear 後面接『原形（省略 to）』，但一旦改成『被動語態』，那個原本省略的 to 必須『補回來』，變成 be made to V、be seen to V、be heard to V。例如主動 They made him apologize. → 被動 He was made to apologize.。感官動詞被動也可保留 V-ing（be seen doing）。注意 let 沒有這種被動用法，通常改用 be allowed to V 代替。
examples: The students were made to clean the classroom after school. 學生被要求放學後打掃教室。（主動 made ... clean）
He was seen to leave the building at midnight. 有人看到他半夜離開大樓。（也可 seen leaving）
We were allowed to use dictionaries during the test. 考試時我們被允許使用字典。（let 的被動改用 be allowed to）

mistakes: 誤：The coach made us to run ten laps. → 正：The coach made us run ten laps.（make 後接原形，不加 to）
誤：I had my bike to fix yesterday. → 正：I had my bike fixed yesterday.（物被修理，用過去分詞 p.p.，不是 to V）
誤：She got her sister clean the room. → 正：She got her sister to clean the room.（get 使役後面要加 to）
誤：I saw him to cross the street. → 正：I saw him cross / crossing the street.（感官動詞主動時接原形或 V-ing，不接 to）
誤：He was made stay after class. → 正：He was made to stay after class.（改被動時省略的 to 要還原）
note.tip: 口訣：主動看『誰做的動作』—人主動用原形/V-ing、物被處理用 p.p.；get 一定要 to；一改被動，藏起來的 to 就要通通還回來！

d1.stem: My mother made me ___ my homework before I could watch TV.
d1.explanation: make 是使役動詞，後面接原形動詞（make + O + 原形）。故選 do，不加 to、不用 ing。

d1.stem: The teacher let the students ___ a short break after the quiz.
d1.explanation: let（讓、允許）為使役動詞，後接原形動詞，故選 take。

d1.stem: I had the mechanic ___ my scooter last weekend.
d1.explanation: have + 人（mechanic）+ 原形動詞，表示『請某人做某事』。技師是主動去修，故用原形 fix。

d1.stem: She got her brother ___ her with the science project.
d1.explanation: get 當使役用時，後面接 to + 原形（get + O + to V），故選 to help。

d1.stem: Please have your report ___ by this Friday.
d1.explanation: report（報告）是『被完成』的，屬被動，用 have + 物 + 過去分詞，故選 finished。

d1.stem: We watched the fireworks ___ over the stadium last night.
d1.explanation: watch 為感官動詞，後接原形或 V-ing。此處看煙火綻放的完整過程，選原形 explode。

d1.stem: Did you hear someone ___ your name just now?
d1.explanation: hear 是感官動詞，主動時後接原形動詞，故選 call，不接 to。

d1.stem: The funny video made everyone in class ___ out loud.
d1.explanation: make 為使役動詞，後接原形，故選 laugh。

d1.stem: I need to get my hair ___ before the graduation ceremony.
d1.explanation: 頭髮是『被剪』的，用 get + 物 + 過去分詞。cut 的過去分詞仍是 cut，故選 cut。

d1.stem: My parents won't let me ___ out on school nights.
d1.explanation: let 使役動詞後接原形動詞，故選 go。

d1.stem: The teacher helped us ___ the difficult grammar point.
d1.explanation: help 後可接原形或 to 原形。選項中 understand 為原形，符合 help + (to) V 的用法。

d1.stem: I clearly felt the ground ___ for a few seconds during the earthquake.
d1.explanation: feel 為感官動詞，後接原形（或 V-ing）。此處選原形 shake，不加 to。

d2.stem: The strict manager had all the new interns ___ overtime, which really upset them.
d2.explanation: have + 人 + 原形，表示要求某人做某事。實習生是主動加班，故用原形 work，而非 p.p.。

d2.stem: Since the school printer was broken, Jenny had her documents ___ at the copy shop.
d2.explanation: documents 是『被列印』的，屬被動，用 have + 物 + 過去分詞，故選 printed。

d2.stem: No matter what I said, I couldn't get my little brother ___ his vegetables.
d2.explanation: get 使役後接 to V（get + O + to V）。這裡弟弟是主動吃，故選 to eat，不用 p.p.。

d2.stem: The horror movie was so scary that it made my heart ___ much faster.
d2.explanation: make 使役動詞後接原形，故選 beat。

d2.stem: As I walked past the classroom, I saw my classmates ___ hard for the final exam.
d2.explanation: 感官動詞 see 後可接原形或 V-ing。『經過時看到正在唸書的畫面』強調進行中，用 V-ing studying 最恰當。

d2.stem: After the accident, the driver was made ___ for all the damage he had caused.
d2.explanation: make 主動時接原形，但改成被動語態 be made 時，省略的 to 要還原，故選 to pay。

d2.stem: Would you please let me ___ what you decide as soon as possible?
d2.explanation: let 使役動詞後接原形，故選 know（let me know 為固定用法）。

d2.stem: The nurse patiently helped the elderly patient ___ up from the wheelchair.
d2.explanation: help 後接原形或 to 原形，此處僅原形 stand 為正確形式。

d2.stem: I heard the balloon suddenly ___ with a loud bang and everyone jumped.
d2.explanation: hear 為感官動詞，此處為一瞬間完整的爆破聲，用原形 pop 最合適；被動時才需 to。

d2.stem: My father finally got the leaking faucet ___ after watching an online tutorial.
d2.explanation: 水龍頭是『被修好』的，屬被動，用 get + 物 + 過去分詞，故選 fixed。

d2.stem: The comedian was so funny that his jokes had the whole audience ___ within minutes.
d2.explanation: have + O + V-ing 表示『使某人一直處於某動作狀態』，強調持續進行的畫面，故選 laughing。

d2.stem: We must not let a single small setback ___ us from reaching our final goal.
d2.explanation: let 使役動詞後接原形，故選 stop（stop A from Ving：阻止 A 做某事）。

d3.stem: Never before had I seen such a beautiful sunset, nor had I ever felt the wind ___ so gently against my face.
d3.explanation: feel 為感官動詞，主動時後接原形。雖然句首倒裝（nor had I felt）改變語序，動詞補語規則不變，故選原形 blow。

d3.stem: Rarely does the principal let students ___ campus during lunch, so getting his permission felt like a real victory.
d3.explanation: 句首 Rarely 引導倒裝，但主要動詞 let 仍是使役動詞，後接原形，故選 leave。

d3.stem: It was not until the dentist explained the risks that she finally had her wisdom teeth ___.
d3.explanation: 智齒是『被拔除』的，屬被動，用 have + 物 + 過去分詞，故選 removed。此句為 not until 強調句型，不影響使役結構。

d3.stem: What made the whole audience ___ was not the ending itself, but the clever way the director had hidden every clue.
d3.explanation: 本句主詞為名詞子句 What made the audience...，其中 make 使役後接原形，故選 gasp。

d3.stem: Only after being made ___ the entire report did the intern realize how important proofreading was.
d3.explanation: make 在被動語態（being made）後，省略的 to 必須還原，故選 to rewrite。句首 Only after 也造成後半倒裝。

d3.stem: So absorbed was he in the video game that he never noticed his mother ___ in and stand right behind him.
d3.explanation: notice 為感官動詞，後接原形。此處與並列的 stand（原形）對稱，故選原形 come。句首 So...that 造成倒裝，不影響補語形式。

d3.stem: The company's new policy is designed to get employees ___ more responsibility for their own projects.
d3.explanation: get 使役後接 to V（get + O + to V），員工是主動承擔責任，故選 to take。

d3.stem: Legend has it that the ancient emperor had a magnificent palace ___ within a single year.
d3.explanation: 宮殿是『被建造』的，屬被動，用 had + 物 + 過去分詞，故選 built。

d3.stem: Had the teacher not let us ___ our dictionaries, very few of us would have passed that difficult translation test.
d3.explanation: let 使役動詞後接原形，故選 use。句首為假設語氣的倒裝（Had the teacher not let us = If the teacher had not let us）。

d3.stem: The suspect claimed that he had merely watched the crime ___, insisting he had never taken part in it.
d3.explanation: watch 為感官動詞，主動時後接原形，故選 happen，不加 to。

d3.stem: Seldom do parents nowadays have their children ___ household chores without offering some kind of reward.
d3.explanation: have + 人（children）+ 原形，孩子是主動做家事，故選 do。誘答的 done（p.p.）用於『事被完成』，此處主詞是人做事，不適用。句首 Seldom 引導倒裝。

d3.stem: The thief was ___ leaving the shop with unpaid goods, which left him no way to deny the theft.
d3.explanation: 此為感官動詞的被動語態 be seen + V-ing / to V，空格需填過去分詞 seen 構成被動。故選 seen（was seen leaving）。

topic.label: 不定詞與動名詞
topic.scope: remember/forget/stop/try/mean/regret + to V vs V-ing 語意差異、疑問詞+to V、too...to/...enough to、be worth V-ing、can't help V-ing、look forward to V-ing、used to 與 be used to V-ing、獨立不定詞

note.title: 不定詞與動名詞
note.summary: 「不定詞(to V)」與「動名詞(V-ing)」是學測文法的核心戰場。同一個動詞後面接 to V 或 V-ing，意思可能完全不同(如 remember、forget、stop、try、mean、regret)；有些片語則只能固定接其中一種(如 be worth V-ing、look forward to V-ing、can't help V-ing)。掌握的關鍵口訣是：『to V 多半指向未來、還沒做的事;V-ing 多半指向過去、已發生或正在進行的事』。本單元帶你逐一拆解語意差異動詞、疑問詞+to V、too...to / ...enough to、used to 與 be used to、以及獨立不定詞等高頻考點，並用貼近校園與考試情境的例句幫你建立語感。

sections.h: 語意差異動詞:to V 看未來,V-ing 看過去
sections.body: remember / forget / stop / try / mean / regret 後面接 to V 或 V-ing 意思不同。remember/forget to V=記得/忘記『要去做』(事情還沒做);remember/forget V-ing=記得/忘記『做過』(事情已發生)。stop to V=停下來『去做另一件事』(不定詞表目的);stop V-ing=停止『正在做的事』。regret to V=『遺憾地(告知)』(多用 say/tell/inform,壞消息正要說出口);regret V-ing=後悔『做過』某事。
examples: Remember to bring your student ID tomorrow, or you can't enter the exam room. 明天記得帶學生證,否則不能進考場。
I still remember winning my first English speech contest in junior high. 我仍記得國中時贏得第一次英語演講比賽。
We stopped to buy some drinks, then stopped talking and focused on the road. 我們停下來買飲料,接著停止交談、專心開車。

sections.h: try / mean 的雙面語意
sections.body: try to V=『努力、試圖』去做(強調付出努力,可能沒成功);try V-ing=『嘗試某個方法看看效果』。mean to V=『打算、故意』;mean V-ing=『意味著、需要』(相當於 involve)。判斷時先看語境:是在『盡力做』還是『試個方法』?是『存心』還是『代表某種結果』?
examples: He tried hard to finish the marathon, and he tried drinking sports drinks to keep going. 他努力想跑完馬拉松,也嘗試喝運動飲料來撐下去。
I'm sorry — I didn't mean to hurt you. 對不起,我不是故意要傷害你。
Getting into that school means studying much harder every day. 考上那所學校意味著每天要更用功。

sections.h: 疑問詞 + to V
sections.body: 『疑問詞(what/which/who/where/when/how/whether)+ to V』可當名詞片語,放在動詞後當受詞,等於一個名詞子句。常見於 know / decide / learn / tell / explain / have no idea 之後。注意 why 通常不接 to V;whether 可接 to V 但 if 不行。
examples: Could you tell me how to get to the train station? 你能告訴我怎麼去火車站嗎?
Facing so many electives, freshmen often don't know which to choose first. 面對這麼多選修課,新生常不知道該先選哪一門。
I can't decide whether to join the debate club or the drama club. 我決定不了要加辯論社還是戲劇社。

sections.h: too...to 與 ...enough to
sections.body: 『too + 形容詞/副詞 + to V』=『太…以致於不能…』(本身就含否定);『形容詞/副詞 + enough + to V』=『夠…足以…』。注意:enough 放在形容詞/副詞『之後』、名詞『之前』。若不定詞的受詞就是主詞,句尾不再重複受詞(too heavy to carry,不加 it)。可用『for + 受詞』點明對象。
examples: The problem was too difficult for me to solve on my own. 這個問題太難,我無法自己解出來。
She is old enough to apply for a driver's license now. 她現在的年紀夠大,可以申請駕照了。
The box is too heavy to carry upstairs. 這個箱子太重,搬不上樓。

sections.h: used to V 與 be / get used to V-ing
sections.body: used to + 原形V=『過去(習慣/曾經),現在不再』;be / get used to + V-ing(或名詞)=『(變得)習慣於』某事,此處 to 是介系詞,務必接動名詞。兩者長得像卻天差地遠:前者談『今昔對比』,後者談『適應與否』。
examples: My grandfather used to walk to school every day when he was young. 我爺爺年輕時每天走路上學。
After living in Taipei for years, I'm used to taking the MRT every day. 在台北住了多年後,我習慣每天搭捷運。
I wasn't used to getting up at six, but now it feels normal. 我以前不習慣六點起床,但現在覺得很正常了。

sections.h: 固定接 V-ing 的片語 & 獨立不定詞
sections.body: 以下片語後面固定接動名詞:be worth V-ing(值得)、can't help V-ing(忍不住)、look forward to V-ing(期待,to 是介系詞)。另有『獨立不定詞』當句子的插入語,與主詞無關,如 to be honest(老實說)、to tell the truth(說實話)、to begin with(首先)、to make matters worse(更糟的是)、needless to say(不用說)。
examples: This novel is really worth reading twice. 這本小說真的值得讀兩遍。
When I heard the joke, I couldn't help laughing. 聽到那個笑話,我忍不住笑了出來。
To be honest, I think we still need more practice before the contest. 老實說,我覺得比賽前我們還需要更多練習。

mistakes: 誤:I look forward to see you. → 正:I look forward to seeing you.(look forward to 的 to 是介系詞,後面接 V-ing)
誤:He used to playing basketball after school. → 正:He used to play basketball after school.(used to + 原形V=過去習慣;be used to + V-ing 才是習慣於)
誤:I can't help to laugh when I see him. → 正:I can't help laughing when I see him.(can't help + V-ing=忍不住)
誤:The soup is too hot for me to drink it. → 正:The soup is too hot for me to drink.(不定詞後不再重複與主詞相同的受詞 it)
誤:Remember bringing your umbrella tomorrow.(指未來該做的事)→ 正:Remember to bring your umbrella tomorrow.(remember to V=記得去做;remember V-ing=記得做過)
note.tip: 一句話記:『to V 的事還沒做(看未來)、V-ing 的事已做過或正在做(看過去/當下)』;遇到 stop/remember/forget/try/mean/regret 先問自己是『還沒做』還是『已做過』。

d1.stem: Remember ___ off the lights before you leave the classroom.
d1.explanation: remember to V=記得『要去做』某事(離開前該關燈,動作還沒做),故選 to turn。remember V-ing 是記得做過。

d1.stem: On our way to school, we stopped ___ some breakfast at the convenience store.
d1.explanation: stop to V=停下來『去做另一件事』,不定詞表目的(停下來『去買』早餐),故選 to buy。

d1.stem: I'm looking forward ___ my old classmates at the class reunion.
d1.explanation: look forward to 的 to 是介系詞,後面須接動名詞,故選 to seeing。

d1.stem: When I heard the funny joke, I couldn't help ___.
d1.explanation: can't help + V-ing=忍不住(做),為固定用法,故選 laughing。

d1.stem: This English novel is really worth ___ twice.
d1.explanation: be worth + V-ing=值得(做),動名詞主動形即可表被動意義,故選 reading。

d1.stem: The math problem was too difficult for me ___ on my own.
d1.explanation: too + 形容詞 + (for 人) + to V=太…以致於不能…,故選 to solve。

d1.stem: She is old ___ a driver's license now.
d1.explanation: 形容詞 + enough + to V=夠…足以…;enough 放形容詞之後,後接不定詞,故選 enough to get。

d1.stem: My grandfather ___ walk to school every day when he was young.
d1.explanation: used to + 原形V=過去的習慣(現在不再),故選 used to;後面接原形 walk。

d1.stem: After living in Taipei for years, I ___ taking the MRT every day.
d1.explanation: be used to + V-ing=習慣於某事;此處後接動名詞 taking,故選 am used to。

d1.stem: Could you tell me ___ to the train station?
d1.explanation: 疑問詞 + to V 可當名詞片語作受詞,how to get=如何到達,故選 how to get。

d1.stem: Don't forget ___ your homework tomorrow morning.
d1.explanation: forget to V=忘記『要去做』(交作業的動作還沒發生),故選 to hand in。

d1.stem: I'm really sorry. I didn't mean ___ your feelings.
d1.explanation: mean to V=打算、故意(做);此處指『不是故意要傷害』,故選 to hurt。

d2.stem: I've been feeling sleepy in class, so I tried ___ more water, but it didn't help much.
d2.explanation: try V-ing=『嘗試某方法看看效果』;喝水是想試看看能否提神(而非努力去做的難事),故選 drinking。try to V 是努力去做。

d2.stem: I clearly remember ___ this movie before — I already know how it ends.
d2.explanation: remember V-ing=記得『做過』某事;已看過所以知道結局,故選 seeing。

d2.stem: The teacher walked in and asked us to stop ___ and pay attention to the board.
d2.explanation: stop V-ing=停止『正在做的事』(停止交談),故選 talking。stop to V 則是停下來去做別的事。

d2.stem: We regret ___ you that your application has unfortunately been rejected.
d2.explanation: regret to V(常搭配 say/tell/inform)=『遺憾地(告知)』,壞消息正要說出口,故選 to inform。

d2.stem: She now regrets ___ so rude to her mother during the argument yesterday.
d2.explanation: regret V-ing=後悔『做過』某事;昨天已發生的無禮行為,故選 being。

d2.stem: If you really want to get into that school, it will mean ___ much harder from now on.
d2.explanation: mean V-ing=意味著、需要(=involve);考上等於『需要更用功』,故選 studying。mean to V 是打算。

d2.stem: I'll never forget ___ the Taipei 101 fireworks with my family for the first time.
d2.explanation: forget V-ing=忘記『做過』某事(此處為難忘的回憶),故選 watching。

d2.stem: When I first moved here, I wasn't used to ___ so early, but now it feels natural.
d2.explanation: be used to + V-ing=習慣於;此處 to 是介系詞,後接動名詞 getting up,故選 getting up。

d2.stem: Be careful — this cup of tea is too hot ___ right now.
d2.explanation: too + 形容詞 + to V=太…不能…;太燙以致於不能喝,故選 to drink(受詞與主詞相同,不加 it)。

d2.stem: After running for almost an hour, the exhausted players finally stopped ___ a short rest.
d2.explanation: stop to V=停下來『去做某事』,不定詞表目的(停下來去休息),故選 to take。

d2.stem: I really can't decide ___ to accept the offer now or wait for a better one.
d2.explanation: whether + to V=是否要…;句中 or 提示在兩種選擇間猶豫,故選 whether。

d2.stem: It's definitely worth ___ some extra time reviewing your notes before the final exam.
d2.explanation: (be/It's) worth + V-ing=值得(做);故選 spending。

d3.stem: My laptop kept freezing during the online class, so I tried ___ it, and luckily that fixed the problem.
d3.explanation: try V-ing=『嘗試某方法看效果』;後句說明這個方法奏效了,重點在『試某招』而非努力去做,故選 restarting。若用 try to restart 則強調『努力想重開機(可能沒成功)』,與語境不合。

d3.stem: "Attention, passengers. We regret ___ you that flight BR182 has been delayed due to bad weather."
d3.explanation: regret to V=『遺憾地(告知)』,壞消息正要宣布;廣播用 regret to inform,故選 to inform。regret informing 會變成『後悔已通知過』。

d3.stem: You ___ careful when you share your personal information with strangers online.
d3.explanation: can't be too + 形容詞=『再…也不為過、越…越好』的慣用語;can't be too careful=再小心也不為過,故選 can't be too。

d3.stem: ___, I don't think our team deserved to win; the other side clearly performed better.
d3.explanation: 獨立不定詞 To be honest=『老實說』,當句子的插入語,與主詞無關;其餘皆非慣用形式,故選 To be honest。

d3.stem: I grew up in a quiet countryside town, so even now I'm still not used to ___ in such a noisy city.
d3.explanation: be used to + V-ing=習慣於;此 to 為介系詞,後接動名詞 living,故選 living。若寫 used to live 則變成『過去曾住』,與語意矛盾。

d3.stem: The stand-up comedian was so hilarious that the whole audience couldn't help but ___.
d3.explanation: can't help but + 原形V=忍不住(做),此變體 but 後接原形動詞,故選 laugh。(注意:can't help + V-ing 才接動名詞。)

d3.stem: For a firefighter, the job often means ___ your own life to save complete strangers.
d3.explanation: mean V-ing=意味著、需要(=involve);這份工作『需要冒生命危險』,故選 risking。mean to V 則是『打算』,不合語境。

d3.stem: Now that the results are out, I really regret ___ so much time playing games instead of studying.
d3.explanation: regret V-ing=後悔『做過』某事;後悔已浪費掉的時間,故選 wasting。

d3.stem: I was so absorbed in the novel that I didn't stop ___ how late it was until midnight.
d3.explanation: stop to V=停下來『去做某事』;此處指『沒停下來去察覺時間』,didn't stop to notice 表沒有停下來注意時間,故選 to notice。

d3.stem: "Sure, I'd be ___ to help you with your science project — just tell me when you're free."
d3.explanation: only too + 形容詞 + to V=非常樂意…;only too happy to help=非常樂意幫忙,為固定慣用語,故選 only too happy。

d3.stem: Facing dozens of elective courses, freshmen often have no idea ___ first.
d3.explanation: 疑問詞 + to V 可作名詞片語當受詞;which to choose=該選哪一門,故選 which to choose。

d3.stem: The evidence presented in court was not strong ___ the jury, so the suspect was set free.
d3.explanation: 形容詞 + enough + to V=夠…足以…;not strong enough to convince=不夠有力到能說服,故選 enough to convince。

topic.label: 名詞子句
topic.scope: that/whether/if 引導、疑問詞引導名詞子句、間接問句語序(I don't know where he is)、同位語 that 子句、名詞子句當主詞/受詞/補語、whether...or not

note.title: 名詞子句
note.summary: 名詞子句(Noun Clause)就是「一個完整句子被降級成一個名詞」，可以在主要子句中當主詞、受詞、補語或同位語。它由連接詞 that / whether / if，或疑問詞 who、what、where、when、why、how 等引導。最重要的觀念有兩個：一是名詞子句一律使用「肯定句語序(主詞+動詞)」，就算它是由疑問詞引導也不能倒裝(這就是間接問句);二是要分清 that(不缺成分、無疑問語氣)、whether/if(是否)、以及疑問詞(帶疑問語氣)三者的差別。學測常考語序、連接詞選用、同位語 that，以及 whether 與 if 的細微差異。

sections.h: that 引導的名詞子句
sections.body: 當一個完整、無疑問語氣的句子要當作主詞、受詞或補語時,用連接詞 that 引導,that 本身沒有意義、在子句中也不當任何成分,只是「打包」的功能。當它當受詞時,that 常常可以省略。當它當主詞時,通常會用虛主詞 it 移到句尾,寫成 It is + 形容詞 + that 子句。
examples: That she passed the entrance exam surprised everyone. 她通過入學考試這件事讓大家都很驚訝。
The teacher said (that) our final report was due on Friday. 老師說我們的期末報告週五要交。
It is obvious that he has been practicing English every day. 很明顯他每天都在練英文。

sections.h: whether / if 引導「是否」的名詞子句
sections.body: 當子句帶有「是不是、有沒有」的不確定語氣時,用 whether 或 if,意思是「是否」。兩者常可互換,但有規則:當子句要當『主詞』或放在『介系詞後面』、或後面直接接 or not 時,只能用 whether,不能用 if;if 通常只用在動詞的受詞位置。
examples: I'm not sure whether/if I should join the debate club. 我不確定該不該參加辯論社。
Whether we win the contest or not doesn't really matter. 我們有沒有贏比賽其實不重要。
The question of whether to study abroad worries many students. 要不要出國念書這問題困擾許多學生。

sections.h: 疑問詞引導的名詞子句與間接問句語序
sections.body: 由 who、what、when、where、why、how 等疑問詞引導的名詞子句,最容易犯錯的就是『語序』。雖然是疑問詞開頭,但它是名詞子句(間接問句),必須用『肯定句語序:疑問詞 + 主詞 + 動詞』,不可以像直接問句那樣把 be 動詞或助動詞移到主詞前面,也不需要 do/does/did。
examples: I don't know where he is now. 我不知道他現在在哪裡。(不是 where is he)
Can you tell me why the meeting was canceled? 你能告訴我會議為什麼取消嗎?
What she really wants remains a mystery to us. 她真正想要什麼對我們仍是個謎。

sections.h: 同位語 that 子句 與 名詞子句的句中功能
sections.body: 某些抽象名詞(fact、idea、news、belief、fact、hope、possibility 等)後面可以接 that 子句來『說明其內容』,這叫同位語子句,that 不可省略也不可換成 which。此外要能判斷名詞子句在句中扮演的角色:當主詞(前面沒別的主詞)、當及物動詞或介系詞的受詞、或當 be 動詞後的補語。
examples: The fact that she never gives up inspires all of us. 她從不放棄這個事實激勵了我們所有人。
My worry is that we may not finish the project on time. 我擔心的是我們可能無法準時完成專案。(補語)
Nobody paid attention to what the speaker was saying. 沒有人注意演講者在說什麼。(介系詞受詞)

mistakes: 誤：I don't know where is the library. → 正：I don't know where the library is.(間接問句要用主詞+動詞的肯定句語序)

誤：Whether or not he comes are important. / The question is if we should go.(主詞、be 補語位置誤用 if)→ 正：Whether he comes or not is important. / The question is whether we should go.(當主詞、補語、接 or not 用 whether)
誤：The news which our team won the game excited us. → 正：The news that our team won the game excited us.(同位語用 that,不用 which,且 that 不可省)
note.tip: 看到疑問詞開頭卻不是在問問題,先在心裡默念口訣「疑問詞+主詞+動詞」,語序千萬別倒裝;要選連接詞時問自己「有沒有疑問語氣、缺不缺成分」——不缺又無疑問用 that,是不是用 whether/if,缺成分用疑問詞。

d1.stem: Everyone believes ___ she will win the English speech contest this year.
d1.explanation: 子句「she will win...」是完整句、無疑問語氣、不缺成分,故用連接詞 that 引導當 believes 的受詞。what 會使子句缺受詞,whether 帶「是否」語氣皆不合。

d1.stem: I don't know ___ the school library is.
d1.explanation: 間接問句(名詞子句)必須用「疑問詞+主詞+動詞」的肯定句語序,故用 where 接 the library is。where is 是直接問句倒裝語序,錯誤。

d1.stem: ___ he studies hard every day is obvious to his classmates.
d1.explanation: 名詞子句當主詞、內容完整無疑問語氣,用 That 引導。What 會使子句缺成分,If 不可放句首當主詞,Whether or 為破碎選項。

d1.stem: Can you tell me ___ the next bus will arrive?
d1.explanation: tell me 後接名詞子句,採肯定句語序「when + 主詞 + 動詞」,即 when the next bus will arrive。when will 為倒裝的直接問句語序,錯誤。

d1.stem: I'm not sure ___ I locked the classroom door.
d1.explanation: 「不確定是否鎖了門」帶有『是不是』的疑問語氣,用 whether(或 if)。that 無疑問語氣,語意不合。

d1.stem: The teacher asked us ___ we had finished the homework.
d1.explanation: 「是否完成作業」帶疑問語氣,在動詞 asked 的受詞位置可用 if(=whether)。that 無「是否」意思。

d1.stem: ___ she said in the meeting really impressed me.
d1.explanation: 子句「she said ___」缺少 said 的受詞,需用兼具名詞功能的 What(=the thing that)。That 不缺成分才用。

d1.stem: The problem is ___ we don't have enough time to review.
d1.explanation: 名詞子句當 be 動詞後的補語,內容完整,用 that 引導。what 會缺成分,if 不可用於補語位置。

d1.stem: Do you remember ___ we first met on campus?
d1.explanation: remember 後的名詞子句用肯定句語序 when + 主詞 + 動詞(when we first met)。when did 為直接問句倒裝,錯誤。

d1.stem: I wonder ___ he will join our study group or not.
d1.explanation: 句尾有 or not,只能用 whether,不可用 if。that 與 what 皆無「是否」語意。

d1.stem: The fact ___ she practices English daily explains her fluency.
d1.explanation: 抽象名詞 The fact 後接同位語子句說明其內容,用 that(不可用 which),且 that 不可省。

d2.stem: ___ we will hold the sports day depends on the weather next week.
d2.explanation: 名詞子句當『主詞』且表「是否」,只能用 Whether,不可用 If(If 不放句首當主詞)。That 無疑問語氣、What 會缺成分皆不合。

d2.stem: I have no idea ___ he was so upset about the exam results.
d2.explanation: 語意「不知道他為何如此不高興」,子句成分完整但需表原因,用 why。that 無疑問語氣、於此時態不自然,what 會缺成分皆不合。

d2.stem: She was worried about ___ her answer was correct.
d2.explanation: 介系詞 about 後的名詞子句表「是否」,只能用 whether,不能用 if(if 一般不接在介系詞後)。

d2.stem: ___ makes this book special is its honest description of teenage life.
d2.explanation: 子句「___ makes this book special」缺主詞,需用兼作主詞的 What(=The thing that)引導,整個子句再當全句主詞。

d2.stem: Please tell me ___ so that I can prepare in advance.
options: what time does the exam start
d2.explanation: 間接問句用肯定句語序:what time + 主詞the exam + 動詞starts,且不需助動詞 does。其餘皆保留了直接問句的倒裝或助動詞。

d2.stem: The question of ___ to trust the online news troubles many readers.
d2.explanation: 介系詞 of 後、且接不定詞 to trust,只能用 whether(whether to V 表「是否要…」),if 不可接不定詞也不宜接在介系詞後。

d2.stem: It never occurred to me ___ he had been lying all along.
d2.explanation: 此為虛主詞 It 代替後面的真主詞名詞子句,子句內容完整無疑問語氣,用 that。It...that... 為常見句型。

d2.stem: ___ the rumor is true remains to be seen.
d2.explanation: 名詞子句當主詞、表「是否為真」,句首只能用 Whether。If 不可放句首當主詞,That 無「是否」語氣。

d2.stem: I can't decide ___ to take the science course or the art course.
d2.explanation: 接不定詞 to take 且有二選一(or)語意,用 whether(whether to V)。if 不可直接接不定詞。

d2.stem: Do you have any idea ___ the missing library book might be?
d2.explanation: 名詞子句(同位於 idea)採肯定句語序 where + 主詞 + 動詞(where the book might be)。where is 為倒裝,錯誤。

d2.stem: ___ surprised the whole class was that our quiet classmate won first prize.
d2.explanation: 子句「___ surprised the whole class」缺主詞,用 What 引導當主詞;全句為 What...was that...(主詞子句 + be + 補語子句)結構。

d3.stem: ___ the new policy will actually reduce students' stress is still open to debate among teachers.
d3.explanation: 名詞子句當主詞、含「是否會真正減壓」的不確定語氣,句首只能用 Whether。That 會斷言其為事實、與 open to debate(仍有爭議)矛盾;If 不置於句首主詞位置。

d3.stem: Little did the committee realize ___ far-reaching the consequences of their decision would be.
d3.explanation: 前半 Little did...realize 是否定副詞倒裝,但受詞名詞子句本身仍須肯定句語序;空格後接形容詞 far-reaching,需用 how 修飾程度(how far-reaching...would be)。what 不能直接修飾形容詞。

d3.stem: The scientist's conclusion, ___ humans are largely responsible for climate change, sparked heated discussion.
d3.explanation: 逗號後為抽象名詞 conclusion 的同位語子句,說明結論『內容』,用 that;子句成分完整,故不能用作關係代名詞的 which(which 需在子句中當成分),what 則會缺成分。

d3.stem: ___ he apologized suggests that he has realized his mistake, even though he said little.
d3.explanation: 語意為「他道歉了這件事」(已發生的事實)當主詞,用 That;整句 That...suggests that... 兩個 that 子句功能不同(前主詞、後受詞)。用 Whether 會變成『是否道歉』與後文『已意識到錯誤』矛盾。

d3.stem: Not until she read the letter twice did she understand ___.
d3.explanation: 主句 Not until...did she 為倒裝,但 understand 後的名詞子句不受影響,須用肯定句語序且不加助動詞:what the writer meant。其餘選項含多餘助動詞、多餘 that 或語序錯誤。

d3.stem: The coach made ___ clear that whoever arrived late would sit on the bench.
d3.explanation: made 後用虛受詞 it 代替真受詞的 that 子句(make it clear that...);同時子句內 whoever(=anyone who)引導名詞子句當主詞。that 直接放此處不合句型。

d3.stem: ___ you choose to major in matters less than whether you are truly passionate about it.
d3.explanation: 子句「___ you choose to major in」中介系詞 in 缺受詞,需用兼作受詞的 What 引導;整個子句當主詞。後半 whether... 對比呼應,但空格處因缺成分不能用 Whether/That。

d3.stem: His reluctance to answer left us wondering ___.
options: if or not he was hiding something
d3.explanation: wondering 後表『是否隱瞞』,用 whether + 肯定語序。選項A語序倒裝錯誤;C『if or not』搭配錯誤(or not 須配 whether);D 用 that 無疑問語氣、與 wondering 不搭。

d3.stem: ___ remains uncertain is how the committee will allocate the limited budget among the clubs.
d3.explanation: 句首子句「___ remains uncertain」缺主詞,用 What 引導(What remains uncertain=尚不確定的事);後接 be + how 引導的補語子句,全句為主詞子句+be+補語子句。

d3.stem: So convincing was her argument ___ even the skeptics changed their minds.
d3.explanation: 此為 So + 形容詞 + be 倒裝的 so...that 結構(=Her argument was so convincing that...),空格為引導結果子句的 that,屬 so...that 句型而非名詞子句,唯 that 正確。此題測驗學生辨別 that 的不同用法。

d3.stem: The teacher emphasized ___, regardless of the outcome.
options: that what matters is how hard you try
what that matters is how hard you try
that how hard you try is what matters it
whether how hard you try matters
d3.explanation: emphasized 後接 that 子句,子句內主詞為 what matters(缺主詞用 what),補語為 how hard you try。A 結構正確;B 語序錯亂,C 多出 it,D 用 whether 語意與斷言不合。

d3.stem: It puzzled the researchers ___ the same experiment produced different results each time.
d3.explanation: 虛主詞 It 代替真主詞名詞子句,子句「the same experiment produced...」成分完整、陳述令人困惑的事實,用 that(It puzzled...that...)。whether 會變『是否』與 produced different results 的事實陳述矛盾。

topic.label: 形容詞子句(關係子句)
topic.scope: who/whom/which/that/whose、關係副詞 when/where/why、限定與非限定用法(逗號)、介系詞+關代(the man to whom)、數量詞+of+which/whom(both of which)、that 不可用的情況

note.title: 形容詞子句(關係子句)
note.summary: 形容詞子句(又稱關係子句)是用一整個句子來修飾名詞,讓表達更精簡、更有層次,是學測閱讀與寫作的關鍵句型。本單元帶你掌握關係代名詞(who/whom/which/that/whose)與關係副詞(when/where/why)的選用邏輯,分辨限定與非限定(逗號)用法,並進階學會「介系詞+關代」與「數量詞+of+which/whom」等高分句構,最後釐清 that 必用與絕對不可用的情況。

sections.h: 一、關係代名詞的基本判斷(先行詞＋在子句中的角色)
sections.body: 關係代名詞用來連接兩句話,並代替前面的名詞(先行詞)。選哪一個要看兩件事:先行詞是人還是物,以及關代在形容詞子句裡扮演的角色(主格/受格/所有格)。人的主格用 who、受格用 whom、所有格用 whose;物的主格與受格用 which 或 that,所有格用 whose。作受格的關代在限定用法中常可省略。
examples: The boy who broke the window apologized to the teacher. 打破窗戶的那個男孩向老師道歉。
I like the songs (that/which) she wrote. 我喜歡她寫的那些歌。
Look at the dog whose tail is wagging. 看那隻搖著尾巴的狗。

sections.h: 二、關係副詞 when / where / why
sections.body: 當先行詞表時間(the day、the year、the moment)、地點(the place、the city、the house)或原因(the reason)時,若後面接的是「完整句子」(不缺主詞也不缺受詞),就要用關係副詞 when / where / why,而不是關係代名詞。關係副詞相當於「介系詞+關係代名詞」(where = in/at which、when = on/in which)。判斷關鍵永遠是:子句到底完不完整。
examples: This is the café where we usually study. 這是我們常唸書的咖啡廳。(study 之後不缺受詞,子句完整)
I still remember the summer when I learned to swim. 我仍記得我學會游泳的那個夏天。
Nobody knows the reason why he quit the team. 沒人知道他退出隊伍的原因。

sections.h: 三、限定與非限定用法(逗號是關鍵)
sections.body: 沒有逗號的是「限定用法」,子句用來界定先行詞究竟是哪一個,不可省略。加了逗號的是「非限定用法」,只是補充說明,拿掉後句子主要意思仍完整。非限定用法有兩條鐵則:(1) 不可用 that;(2) 關係代名詞不可省略。此外,which 還可以代表前面「一整個句子」。
examples: My uncle who lives in Tainan is a doctor. 我那位住台南的叔叔是醫生。(暗示我不只一個叔叔)
My uncle, who lives in Tainan, is a doctor. 我叔叔是醫生(順帶補充他住台南)。
She got full marks, which surprised everyone. 她考了滿分,這件事讓大家都很驚訝。

sections.h: 四、介系詞+關代 與 數量詞+of+which/whom
sections.body: 在正式書寫中,介系詞可以移到關係代名詞前面,形成「介系詞+whom/which」:the man to whom I spoke。要注意介系詞前面只能接 whom(人)或 which(物),不可用 who 或 that,關代也不能省略。另外「數量詞+of+關代」(both of which、all of whom、none of which、some of which)常出現在非限定子句,用來表示先行詞的其中一部分。
examples: The friend to whom I lent my notes returned them today. 我借筆記給的那位朋友今天還我了。
He asked many questions, none of which I could answer. 他問了很多問題,我一題也答不出來。
I read two novels, both of which were written by the same author. 我讀了兩本小說,兩本都是同一位作者寫的。

sections.h: 五、that 的特殊規則(必用與絕對不可用)
sections.body: 有些情況「傾向」用 that:先行詞是 everything、all、the only、the very、序數或最高級,或先行詞同時包含人與物時。但有兩種情況「絕對不可用 that」:(1) 非限定用法(逗號之後);(2) 介系詞緊接在關代前面(不可寫 in that)。另外「the way how」是錯的,只能用 the way、the way that,或用介系詞搭配 which。
examples: This is the best advice that I have ever received. 這是我聽過最好的建議。
She is the only student that passed the exam. 她是唯一通過考試的學生。
That is the house in which they live. 那就是他們住的房子。(不可寫 in that)

mistakes: 誤:The city which I was born is beautiful. → 正:The city where I was born is beautiful.(was born 後子句完整,先行詞為地點,須用 where)
誤:Taipei, that is the capital, is crowded. → 正:Taipei, which is the capital, is crowded.(逗號非限定用法不可用 that)
誤:This is the man who I gave the book to him. → 正:This is the man to whom I gave the book.(關代已代替受詞,不可再加 him)
誤:I have two dogs, both of them are white. → 正:I have two dogs, both of which are white.(非限定子句要用關代連接,不可用 them)
誤:Do you know the way how she solved it? → 正:Do you know the way (that) she solved it?(the way 與 how 不可並用)
note.tip: 先看先行詞是人是物,再看子句缺不缺;缺主詞或受詞用關代,句子完整用關副,逗號後面忘掉 that。

d1.stem: The student ___ won the English speech contest is my classmate.
d1.explanation: 先行詞 the student 是人，關代在子句中作主詞(won)，用主格 who。which 用於物，whom 為受格，whose 為所有格，皆不合。

d1.stem: This is the smartphone ___ I bought last week.
d1.explanation: 先行詞 smartphone 是物，關代作 bought 的受詞，用 which(或 that)。who 指人，whose 為所有格，when 為關係副詞，皆不合。

d1.stem: The girl ___ mother is a teacher sits next to me.
d1.explanation: 要表達「女孩的媽媽」這種所有關係，用所有格關代 whose。

d1.stem: I still remember the day ___ we first met.
d1.explanation: 先行詞 the day 表時間，後接完整子句(we first met 不缺成分)，用關係副詞 when。

d1.stem: That is the classroom ___ we take English lessons.
d1.explanation: 先行詞 classroom 表地點，子句 we take English lessons 完整，用關係副詞 where。

d1.stem: Tell me the reason ___ you were late for school.
d1.explanation: 先行詞 the reason 表原因，後接完整子句，用關係副詞 why。

d1.stem: The book ___ is on the desk belongs to Amy.
d1.explanation: 先行詞 book 是物，關代作子句主詞(is on the desk)，用 which(或 that)。

d1.stem: Mr. Lin is the teacher ___ everyone respects.
d1.explanation: 先行詞 teacher 是人，關代作 respects 的受詞，用受格 whom。which 用於物，what 不可當關代引導形容詞子句。

d1.stem: Basketball is the sport ___ I like most.
d1.explanation: 先行詞 sport 是物，關代作 like 的受詞，選項中用 that 正確。who/whom 用於人，where 後須接完整子句。

d1.stem: Taipei 101 is a building ___ attracts many tourists.
d1.explanation: 先行詞 building 是物，關代作主詞(attracts)，用 which。where 後面必須是完整子句，此處子句缺主詞故不可用。

d1.stem: She is the singer ___ songs are popular among teenagers.
d1.explanation: 要表達「歌手的歌」的所有關係，用所有格 whose。

d1.stem: Summer is the season ___ students look forward to a long vacation.
d1.explanation: 先行詞 season 表時間，後接完整子句(students look forward to a long vacation 不缺成分)，用關係副詞 when。

d2.stem: The old house ___ windows are broken has been empty for years.
d2.explanation: whose 也可用來修飾「物」，表示「房子的窗戶」這種所有關係。which/that 無所有格功能，where 後須接完整子句。

d2.stem: This is almost everything ___ I know about Taiwanese history.
d2.explanation: 先行詞為 everything(不定代名詞)時，關代習慣用 that，不用 which；what 不可再接先行詞。

d2.stem: The excuse ___ he made for missing class was not convincing.
d2.explanation: made 需要受詞(make an excuse)，子句缺受詞，故用關係代名詞 that/which，而非關係副詞 why。

d2.stem: Kaohsiung is the city ___ I spent my childhood.
d2.explanation: spent my childhood 子句完整，先行詞為地點，用關係副詞 where(=in which)。若用 which/that 須補上介系詞 in。

d2.stem: I will always remember the day ___ I stood on the stage to receive the award.
d2.explanation: 先行詞 the day 表時間，後接完整子句，用關係副詞 when。

d2.stem: Tom failed the driving test again, ___ disappointed his father.
d2.explanation: 非限定子句用來代表前面一整句話「Tom 又沒考過」，用 which；that/what 不可，it 會造成兩個句子沒有連接詞。

d2.stem: Sun Moon Lake, ___ is famous for its scenery, draws many visitors.
d2.explanation: 逗號後為非限定用法不可用 that；先行詞 Sun Moon Lake 在子句中作主詞(is famous)，用 which，而非 where。

d2.stem: The professor to ___ I addressed my question smiled kindly.
d2.explanation: 介系詞 to 之後接受格關代，先行詞為人，用 whom；不可用 who、that，which 用於物。

d2.stem: The organization for ___ my sister volunteers helps stray animals.
d2.explanation: 介系詞 for 之後代表物(organization)，用 which；for that 不合語法，who/whom 用於人。

d2.stem: The friends ___ stood by me during hard times mean a lot to me.
d2.explanation: 關代作子句主詞(stood)，先行詞為人，用主格 who，不用受格 whom。

d2.stem: Is this the key ___ opens the back door?
d2.explanation: 先行詞 key 是物且作主詞(opens)，用 that(或 which)；選項中只有 that 正確。

d2.stem: The moment ___ the results were announced, everyone cheered.
d2.explanation: 先行詞 the moment 表時間，後接完整子句，用關係副詞 when(the moment when = 一……就……)。

d3.stem: She has two daughters, both of ___ became doctors.
d3.explanation: 「數量詞+of+關代」的非限定用法，先行詞為人用 whom(介系詞 of 之後接受格)；them 無法連接兩句，who 為主格皆不合。

d3.stem: The report offered several solutions, none of ___ were practical.
d3.explanation: 先行詞 solutions 為物，none of which；用 them 會使兩句缺乏連接詞，that 不可接在介系詞之後。

d3.stem: He is one of those people who ___ never satisfied with what they have.
d3.explanation: who 代表先行詞 those people(複數)，故動詞用複數 are。這是「one of + 複數名詞 + who + 複數動詞」的經典陷阱。

d3.stem: The scientist, ___ theory was once rejected, later won the Nobel Prize.
d3.explanation: 逗號後為非限定用法，且表「科學家的理論」所有關係，用 whose；that 在非限定用法中絕對不可用。

d3.stem: There are moments in life ___ words simply cannot express our feelings.
d3.explanation: 先行詞 moments 表時間，後接完整子句(words cannot express our feelings)，用關係副詞 when；where 為地點陷阱。

d3.stem: Rarely do we meet a person ___ kindness touches everyone around him.
d3.explanation: 先行詞 person 與 kindness 為所有關係(那個人的善良)，用 whose。句首 Rarely 造成的倒裝是干擾。

d3.stem: The house ___ we had lived for ten years was finally sold.
d3.explanation: had lived for ten years 子句完整，先行詞為地點，用 where(=in which)。若用 which/that 須補 in。

d3.stem: Everyone admired the way ___ she handled the difficult situation.
d3.explanation: 先行詞 the way 之後只能用 that 或直接省略，不可用 how(the way how 為錯誤)；which 需搭配介系詞 in。

d3.stem: My best friend moved to Canada, ___ made me quite sad.
d3.explanation: 非限定子句代表前面整句「好友搬去加拿大」這件事，用 which；that/what 不可，it 會缺連接詞。

d3.stem: The building the roof of ___ collapsed is under investigation.
d3.explanation: the roof of which 等於 whose roof，先行詞為物用 of which；that 不可接在介系詞之後，whom 用於人。

d3.stem: Those ___ were late for the exam were not allowed to enter.
d3.explanation: 先行詞 Those 指「人們」，關代作子句主詞(were late)，用主格 who；which 用於物，whom 為受格。

d3.stem: This is the very book for ___ I have been searching everywhere.
d3.explanation: 雖然先行詞前有 the very 常配 that，但關代緊接在介系詞 for 之後時只能用 which(物)；for that/who/what 皆不合。

topic.label: 副詞子句
topic.scope: 時間(as soon as/by the time/the moment)、條件(unless/as long as/provided that/in case)、讓步(although/even though/while/no matter)、目的(so that/in order that)、結果(so...that/such...that)

note.title: 副詞子句
note.summary: 副詞子句由從屬連接詞引導，用來修飾主要子句，說明時間、條件、讓步、目的或結果等關係。本單元聚焦五大類：時間（as soon as／by the time／the moment）、條件（unless／as long as／provided that／in case）、讓步（although／even though／while／no matter）、目的（so that／in order that）與結果（so...that／such...that）。學測常在時態呼應、so 與 such 的區分、unless 的隱含否定、以及進階倒裝與省略上設陷阱，掌握每個連接詞的語意與句型結構是拿分關鍵。

sections.h: 一、時間副詞子句與時態呼應
sections.body: as soon as（一…就）、the moment（一…就）強調兩動作幾乎同時發生；by the time（到…的時候）常搭配完成式，表主要子句動作在時間子句之前或之後完成。最重要的規則：當主要子句用未來式時，時間副詞子句要用現在式代替未來式（不可用 will）。時態必須與主要子句一致，主要子句過去式，子句也用過去式。
examples: As soon as the class ends, I will call you. 一下課我就打給你。
By the time we got to the theater, the show had already started. 我們到戲院時，表演已經開始了。
The moment she saw the results, she screamed with joy. 她一看到成績就開心地尖叫。

sections.h: 二、條件副詞子句
sections.body: unless =「除非；如果不」，本身已含否定，後面動詞不可再加 not；as long as 與 provided (that) 都表「只要」，強調條件成立才會有結果；in case 表「以防萬一」，用來說明預防措施。條件子句同樣以現在式表未來。
examples: You won't pass unless you study harder. 除非你更用功，否則不會及格。
I'll lend you the book as long as you return it next week. 只要你下週還我，我就借你這本書。
Bring some cash in case the card reader is broken. 帶點現金，以防讀卡機壞掉。

sections.h: 三、讓步副詞子句
sections.body: although／even though／though =「雖然」，引導事實；even if =「即使」，引導假設情況；while 除了「當…時」，也可表「然而」的對比語氣；no matter + wh-（how／what／who…）=「無論…」，可與 however／whatever／whoever 互換。特別注意：although 不可與 but 連用，一句只留一個連接詞。
examples: Although he was exhausted, he finished the race. 雖然他筋疲力盡，還是完成了比賽。
No matter how busy you are, remember to rest. 無論你多忙，都要記得休息。
While some students like online classes, others prefer face-to-face teaching. 有些學生喜歡線上課，然而其他人偏好面對面教學。

sections.h: 四、目的 vs 結果
sections.body: 目的：so that／in order that + 子句，子句中常含 can／could／will／would 等助動詞，表「為了…、以便…」。結果：so + 形容詞／副詞 + that，或 such + (a/an) + 形容詞 + 名詞 + that，表「如此…以致於…」。判斷關鍵在 so 後面接形容詞或副詞，such 後面接名詞（片語）。若名詞前無冠詞（不可數或複數），用 such nice weather；用 so 修飾形容詞時語序為 so + adj + a + 名詞。
examples: She spoke slowly so that everyone could follow. 她說得很慢，好讓大家都跟得上。（目的）
The box was so heavy that I couldn't lift it. 箱子太重了，我抬不起來。（結果）
It was such a moving film that everyone cried. 那是部如此感人的電影，大家都哭了。（結果）

mistakes: 誤：Although it was raining, but we went out. → 正：Although it was raining, we went out.（although 不與 but 並用，一句只留一個連接詞）
誤：It was so a nice day that we went hiking. → 正：It was such a nice day that we went hiking.（so 接形容詞／副詞，含名詞的片語要用 such）
誤：Unless you don't hurry, you will be late. → 正：Unless you hurry, you will be late.（unless 已含否定，不可再加 not）
誤：As soon as I will arrive, I will text you. → 正：As soon as I arrive, I will text you.（時間副詞子句以現在式代替未來式）
note.tip: so 配形容詞副詞、such 配名詞；目的加 that 帶助動詞，結果 so／such…that 講後果；時間條件子句「現在式代未來」。

d1.stem: ___ the bell rang, the students rushed out of the classroom.
d1.explanation: the moment 意為「一…就」，引導時間副詞子句，符合「鐘一響學生就衝出去」的語意。unless（除非）、although（雖然）、so that（以便）皆不合。

d1.stem: You can borrow my bike ___ you return it before dark.
d1.explanation: as long as 表「只要」，引導條件子句，符合「只要天黑前歸還就可以借」的意思。其餘連接詞語意不通。

d1.stem: ___ it was raining heavily, we still went hiking.
d1.explanation: 前後為讓步關係「雖然下大雨，我們仍去健行」，用 although。because 表原因、so that 表目的、as soon as 表時間，皆不符。

d1.stem: I saved some money ___ I could buy a new phone.
d1.explanation: 表目的「為了能買新手機」用 so that + 子句（常含 could）。such that 與 so...that 為結果句型，需搭配形容詞或名詞，句中沒有。

d1.stem: Take an umbrella ___ it rains this afternoon.
d1.explanation: in case 表「以防萬一」，說明帶傘的預防目的。unless（除非）、as long as（只要）、although（雖然）語意皆不通。

d1.stem: The soup was ___ hot that I couldn't drink it.
d1.explanation: hot 是形容詞，結果句型用 so + 形容詞 + that。such 需接名詞；very、too 後面不接 that 引導結果子句。

d1.stem: It was ___ a boring lecture that half the class fell asleep.
d1.explanation: a boring lecture 為「冠詞+形容詞+名詞」的名詞片語，結果句型用 such + a + adj + n + that。so 只能直接接形容詞或副詞。

d1.stem: ___ you hurry, you will miss the school bus.
d1.explanation: unless =「如果不、除非」，「如果你不快點，就會錯過校車」。as long as／provided that 表「只要」語意相反，the moment 表時間不合。

d1.stem: ___ hard the test is, I will do my best.
d1.explanation: 修飾形容詞 hard 表程度「無論多難」，用 no matter how。no matter what 接名詞子句；although／even though 後不可直接接形容詞這樣的倒裝結構。

d1.stem: As soon as she ___ home, she started doing her homework.
d1.explanation: 主要子句用過去式 started，時間子句須時態一致用過去式 got。will get（未來）、gets（現在）皆與主句時態不呼應。

d1.stem: He studied really hard ___ he could pass the exam.
d1.explanation: 空格後為完整子句 he could pass，表目的用 in order that + 子句。in order to／so as to 後接原形動詞，不能接子句；such that 為結果句型。

d1.stem: The teacher spoke slowly ___ that everyone could understand.
d1.explanation: 表目的「以便大家聽懂」用 so that，故空格填 so。such that 需先有名詞或屬結果用法，very、too 不與 that 構成目的子句。

d2.stem: It was ___ nice weather that we decided to have a picnic.
d2.explanation: weather 為不可數名詞，nice weather 是名詞片語且不加冠詞，用 such + adj + 不可數名詞。so 只接形容詞／副詞；such a 錯在不可數名詞前不可加 a。

d2.stem: There were ___ many people at the concert that we couldn't find seats.
d2.explanation: many 是形容詞（修飾 people），so many + 複數名詞 + that 為結果句型。such 後直接接名詞不搭 many 這種用法；such a 用於單數可數名詞。

d2.stem: By the time we ___ to the station, the train had already left.
d2.explanation: 主要子句用過去完成式 had left，時間子句 by the time 用簡單過去式 got 表較晚發生的動作。get／will get 時態不合；子句不需再用過去完成。

d2.stem: You may play video games ___ you have finished all your homework.
d2.explanation: 「只要你寫完所有功課就可以玩」為條件成立，用 as long as。unless（除非）會使語意變成「除非寫完否則可以玩」相反；in case、although 不合。

d2.stem: ___ some students prefer online classes, others like face-to-face teaching.
d2.explanation: 前後為對比「有些人…，另一些人…」，while 可表「然而、儘管」的對比語氣。unless、as long as、so that 皆無法表達這種對比。

d2.stem: No matter ___ you decide, I will support you.
d2.explanation: decide 後缺受詞，需用 no matter what（無論做什麼決定）當名詞子句。how／how much 修飾程度、whether 表是否，皆與此處結構不合。

d2.stem: You can join the field trip ___ your parents sign the consent form.
d2.explanation: provided that 表「條件是、只要」，說明參加校外教學的前提。in case（以防）、no matter（無論，需接 wh-）、so that（以便）語意皆不符。

d2.stem: ___ I studied all night, I still didn't do well on the exam.
d2.explanation: 讀了整晚是既成事實，「雖然…卻…」用 even though。even if 表假設「即使（可能沒發生）」；as long as、so that 語意不合。

d2.stem: Remember to bring a jacket ___ it gets cold at night.
d2.explanation: in case 表「以防萬一晚上變冷」的預防用意。unless 會變成「除非變冷否則帶外套」相反語意；as long as、even though 不通。

d2.stem: She raised her voice ___ everyone in the back could hear her.
d2.explanation: 空格後為完整子句且表目的「好讓後排的人聽見」，用 so that。so...that／such that 為結果句需搭形容詞或名詞；in order to 後接原形動詞。

d2.stem: The moment the teacher ___ the room, the students stopped talking.
d2.explanation: 主要子句用過去式 stopped，時間子句須用過去式 entered 時態一致。enters／will enter／has entered 皆與過去主句不呼應。

d2.stem: It rained so heavily ___ the whole match was canceled.
d2.explanation: so + 副詞 heavily 之後接 that 引導結果子句。多一個 so 變成 so that（目的）不合；such that 需接名詞；because 表原因方向相反。

d3.stem: ___ was the noise from the party that the neighbors called the police.
d3.explanation: 這是 such...that 的倒裝：Such + be 動詞 + 主詞 + that（原為 The noise was such that...）。noise 為名詞，故用 Such 而非接形容詞的 So。

d3.stem: ___ carefully did he plan the surprise party that nothing went wrong.
d3.explanation: so + 副詞置句首時，主要子句須倒裝：So carefully did he plan...。carefully 是副詞，用 so 不用接名詞的 such；very、too 不引導 that 結果子句。

d3.stem: ___ we lose this game, we will still be proud of our team.
d3.explanation: 比賽尚未發生、屬假設情況「即使我們輸」，用 even if。even though 引導既成事實不合；no matter 須接 wh-；as long as 表條件語意相反。

d3.stem: The plants in the garden will die ___ you water them regularly.
d3.explanation: 「除非你定期澆水，否則植物會枯死」需用含否定的 unless。as long as／provided that 會變成「只要澆水就會死」語意矛盾；in case 不通。

d3.stem: There was ___ little water left in the bottle that we had to share every drop.
d3.explanation: little 是形容詞，so + little + 不可數名詞 + that 表結果。such 之後直接接名詞片語不搭 little 這種數量形容詞；such a 用於單數可數名詞。

d3.stem: ___ hard the problem was, she never gave up trying.
d3.explanation: However + 形容詞 + 主詞 + 動詞，表「無論多麼…」。No matter 須寫成 no matter how 才完整；whatever 接名詞；although 後不接這種形容詞前置結構。

d3.stem: By the time you ___ this letter, I will have already left the country.
d3.explanation: 時間副詞子句以現在式代替未來式，故用 read；主要子句用未來完成式 will have left。will read 違反規則，have read／had read 時態不合此語境。

d3.stem: Not until the sun set ___ the temperature drop.
d3.explanation: 否定詞 Not until 置句首，主要子句須部分倒裝為助動詞 + 主詞 + 原形動詞：did the temperature drop。全句為過去時間，故用 did 而非 does／had／was。

d3.stem: You can stay out until midnight tonight ___ you keep your phone on and text me where you are.
d3.explanation: 表「只要你保持開機並回報位置」的條件，用 as long as。in case（以防）、even though（雖然）、no matter（須接 wh-）皆不符此條件語意。

d3.stem: ___ happens tomorrow, promise me you will never forget where you came from.
d3.explanation: happens 前缺主詞，需用 no matter what（無論發生什麼事）當主詞。no matter how 修飾程度；whatever how 為錯誤搭配；however 後須接形容詞或副詞。

d3.stem: It was ___ difficult a decision that she needed a whole week to think it over.
d3.explanation: 此語序為 so + 形容詞 + a + 單數名詞（so difficult a decision）。such 的語序須是 such a difficult decision，與題目語序不符；very 不引導 that 結果子句。

d3.stem: ___ exhausted, the marathon runners kept pushing toward the finish line.
d3.explanation: 這是讓步子句的省略：Although (they were) exhausted，although 後可直接接形容詞。因前後為「雖然筋疲力盡卻仍堅持」的轉折，because（原因）、so that、as long as 皆不合。

topic.label: 準關代與複合關代
topic.scope: as/but/than 當準關係代名詞(such...as, the same...as)、what 引導(關係代名詞含先行詞)、複合關代 whatever/whoever/whichever、no matter who=whoever

note.title: 準關代與複合關代
note.summary: 當先行詞出現 such、the same、as、so、more/-er、no 這類特殊字時，關係代名詞不再用 who/which/that，而改用 as、but、than，這就是「準關係代名詞」。此外，what 本身就等於「the thing(s) that」含有先行詞；whatever/whoever/whichever 這類「複合關代」則等於「anything that / anyone who」，既能引導名詞子句，也能表達「無論…」的讓步語氣，與 no matter who/what 相通。本單元帶你分辨這四類的用法與陷阱。

sections.h: 準關代 as：such / the same / as / so 的好朋友
sections.body: 當先行詞前有 such、the same、as、so 時，關係代名詞用 as。as 可代替人或物，在子句中當主詞或受詞。此外 as 還能代表「整個主要子句」，形成 As is often the case（一如往常）這類固定用法，可放句首或句尾。要特別注意：the same...as 表示「同一類型」，the same...that 才是「同一個實體」。
examples: Such students as work hard will succeed. 像那些用功的學生會成功。
This is the same bag as I lost last week. 這和我上週弄丟的是同款包包。
As we all know, health is more important than wealth. 眾所周知，健康比財富更重要。

sections.h: 準關代 but 與 than
sections.body: but 當準關代時等於「that...not」，只用在否定先行詞（no、never、nobody）之後，形成雙重否定表肯定：There is no rule but has exceptions.（沒有規則不含例外＝每條規則都有例外）。than 則用在比較級 more/-er 之後，引導的子句省略了主詞或受詞：Don't spend more money than is necessary.（別花超過必要的錢）。
examples: There is no one but admires her courage. 沒有人不佩服她的勇氣。
He earned more money than was expected. 他賺的錢比預期的多。
There was no student but passed the exam. 沒有一個學生沒通過考試。

sections.h: 關係代名詞 what：自帶先行詞
sections.body: what＝the thing(s) that / that which，本身已含先行詞，前面不可再有名詞。what 引導名詞子句，可當主詞、受詞或補語，動詞通常視為單數。常見片語：what is called／what we call（所謂的）、what is more（更有甚者）、what is worse（更糟的是），以及 A is to B what C is to D（A 之於 B，猶如 C 之於 D）。
examples: What matters most is your attitude. 最重要的是你的態度。
This is exactly what I wanted to say. 這正是我想說的。
Reading is to the mind what food is to the body. 閱讀之於心靈，猶如食物之於身體。

sections.h: 複合關代 whatever/whoever/whichever 與 no matter
sections.body: whatever＝anything that，whoever＝anyone who，whichever＝any one that（用於範圍內擇一）。它們有兩種身分：一是引導名詞子句（You may take whatever you like.）；二是表讓步，等於 no matter what/who/which（Whatever happens, I'll stay.＝No matter what happens...）。關鍵差異：whoever 可當名詞子句直接充當句子主詞；no matter who 只能當副詞子句，後面主句必須另有主詞（No matter who wins, he will be respected.）。
examples: Whoever comes to the party is welcome. 無論誰來參加派對都歡迎。
You can choose whichever seat you prefer. 你可以選你喜歡的任一個座位。
No matter what you say, I won't change my mind. 無論你說什麼，我都不會改變心意。

sections.h: 一眼判斷該用哪一個
sections.body: 看先行詞就能決定：先行詞為 such/the same/as/so → 用 as；先行詞含否定（no/never）→ 用 but；先行詞前有比較級 more/-er → 用 than；句中根本沒有先行詞、需要一個含先行詞的關代 → 用 what；若要表達「無論…」的名詞或讓步子句 → 用 whatever/whoever/whichever 或 no matter 系列。
examples: Such a chance as this seldom comes twice. 像這樣的機會很少會有第二次。
What she needs is not money but courage. 她需要的不是錢而是勇氣。

mistakes: 誤：This is the same watch which I lost. → 正：This is the same watch as I lost.（the same 後接準關代 as，不用 which）
誤：What I said were all true. → 正：What I said was all true.（what 子句視為單數，動詞用 was）
誤：No matter who wins the game will get a prize. → 正：Whoever wins the game will get a prize.（要當句子主詞須用名詞子句 whoever；no matter who 只能當副詞子句）
誤：There is no one but have faults. → 正：There is no one but has faults.（but＝that...not，動詞須與 one 一致用 has）
note.tip: 口訣：such/same/as 配 as，否定先行詞配 but，比較級 more/-er 配 than，沒先行詞就用 what。

d1.stem: This is the same bike ___ I lost last week.
d1.explanation: 先行詞前有 the same，準關係代名詞須用 as，不可用 which/what。

d1.stem: ___ he said in the meeting is not true.
d1.explanation: 句首需要一個含先行詞的關代當主詞，用 what（＝the thing that）。that/which 前需有先行詞，故不可。

d1.stem: There is no rule ___ has exceptions; every rule can be broken.
d1.explanation: 先行詞含否定 no，準關代用 but（＝that...not），no rule but has exceptions 意為「每條規則都有例外」。

d1.stem: You may take ___ you like from the basket.
d1.explanation: take 的受詞是「你喜歡的任何東西」，用複合關代 whatever（＝anything that）。

d1.stem: ___ comes first will get the best seat.
d1.explanation: 空格是子句主詞（comes 的主詞），指「任何先到的人」，用 whoever。

d1.stem: She earned much more money ___ she had expected.
d1.explanation: 先行詞前有比較級 more，準關代用 than，引導的子句省略了受詞。

d1.stem: Such a kind person ___ Amy will surely help you.
d1.explanation: 先行詞前有 such，準關代用 as。

d1.stem: ___ happens tomorrow, I will always support you.
d1.explanation: 表「無論發生什麼事」，用 whatever happens（＝no matter what happens）。

d1.stem: ___ I want most for my birthday is a new phone.
d1.explanation: 名詞子句當主詞、且含先行詞，用 what（＝the thing that）。

d1.stem: Teamwork is ___ makes our class special.
d1.explanation: be 動詞後的補語子句沒有先行詞，用 what；that/which 需要先行詞。

d1.stem: He is not such a fool ___ to believe that rumor.
d1.explanation: such...as to 表「如此…以致於」，準關代用 as。

d2.stem: Look! This is the very same watch ___ I lost yesterday—someone found it for me.
d2.explanation: 強調「同一個」實體用 the same...that；若用 the same...as 則指「同一款式」。由「someone found it for me」可知是失而復得的那一支，故用 that。

d2.stem: ___ is often the case, Tom forgot to hand in his homework again.
d2.explanation: as 可代表整個主要子句，固定用法 As is often the case（一如往常）。

d2.stem: Please give the first prize to ___ deserves it most.
d2.explanation: 空格是子句中 deserves 的主詞，用主格 whoever，不因前面介系詞 to 而改用受格。

d2.stem: ___ surprised everyone was that the shy boy won the speech contest.
d2.explanation: 名詞子句當主詞、含先行詞且是 surprised 的主詞，用 what。

d2.stem: Don't lend him more money ___ is really necessary.
d2.explanation: 比較級 more 之後的準關代用 than，子句省略主詞。

d2.stem: Reading is to the mind ___ exercise is to the body.
d2.explanation: 固定句型 A is to B what C is to D（A 之於 B 猶如 C 之於 D），用 what。

d2.stem: ___ difficulties you may meet on the way, never give up your dream.
d2.explanation: 後接名詞 difficulties，用 whatever（＝no matter what）修飾；However 後須接形容詞/副詞。

d2.stem: Here are two paths; take ___ leads to the train station.
