d2.explanation: 在既定的兩條路中擇一，用 whichever（範圍內擇一）；whatever 指範圍不限。

d2.stem: A wise traveler is not so careless ___ to trust a total stranger.
d2.explanation: so...as to 表「如此…以致於」，準關代用 as。

d2.stem: Mr. Lin is ___ we call a walking dictionary.
d2.explanation: 固定片語 what we call（所謂的），用 what。

d2.stem: ___ breaks the school rule, he must be punished fairly.
d2.explanation: 主句已有主詞 he，空格帶的是讓步副詞子句，須用 No matter who；若用 Whoever 則不需再有 he。

d3.stem: What impressed the judges most ___ her calm confidence on stage.
d3.explanation: what 引導的名詞子句視為單數，主要動詞用單數 was。

d3.stem: There is no mother ___ loves her own children; parental love is universal.
d3.explanation: 先行詞含否定 no mother，準關代用 but（＝that...not），全句雙重否定表「每位母親都愛孩子」；but 後動詞與 mother 一致用 loves。

d3.stem: ___ was widely expected, the typhoon struck the island hard overnight.
d3.explanation: as 代表整個主句、放句首，形成 As was expected（如所預料）。what 會使子句缺少先行詞而語意不合。

d3.stem: The organizer handed out free tickets to ___ showed up before nine, regardless of age.
d3.explanation: 空格是 showed up 的主詞，用主格 whoever；介系詞 to 支配的是整個名詞子句，而非單一代名詞。

d3.stem: "Is this your umbrella?" "No, but it's exactly the same ___ mine."
d3.explanation: the same...as 為固定用法，此處為省略結構（無子句），只能用 as。

d3.stem: Language is to a nation ___ blood is to a human body.
d3.explanation: A is to B what C is to D 句型，用 what 表比擬。

d3.stem: Such accidents ___ arise from carelessness can usually be prevented.
d3.explanation: 先行詞前有 such，準關代用 as，as 在子句中當 arise 的主詞。

d3.stem: ___ the real reason may be, we simply cannot excuse his rudeness to the teacher.
d3.explanation: 後接名詞 the real reason，用 Whatever（＝no matter what）引導讓步子句；However/No matter how 後須接形容詞或副詞。

d3.stem: I promise I'll buy you ___ you want for graduation, no matter how expensive it is.
d3.explanation: 由 no matter how expensive 可知範圍不限，用 whatever（anything that）強調「任何東西」；whichever 指有限範圍擇一，語氣不合。

d3.stem: ___ the committee finally elects will serve as class president for one year.
d3.explanation: 空格是子句中 elects 的受詞，正式文法用受格 Whomever；整個名詞子句再充當全句主詞。

d3.stem: He missed the last bus, and ___ was worse, it began to pour.
d3.explanation: 固定插入語 what is/was worse（更糟的是），用 what。

d3.stem: ___ you may say to persuade me, I have already made up my mind to leave.
d3.explanation: say 的受詞是「無論你說什麼」，用 Whatever（＝no matter what）引導讓步子句。

topic.label: 假設語氣
topic.scope: 與現在/過去/未來事實相反的三種句型、混合假設、if 省略倒裝(Were I.../Had I known)、I wish/if only、as if/as though、It's (high) time + 過去式、would rather + 主詞 + 過去式、without/but for

note.title: 假設語氣
note.summary: 假設語氣(subjunctive)用來描述「與事實相反」或「純屬想像」的情況，是學測英文的高頻重點。核心觀念在於「動詞時態往回退一格」：談現在就用過去式，談過去就用過去完成式。本單元從三種基本與事實相反句型出發，延伸到混合假設、if 省略後的倒裝(Were I.../Had I known/Should you)、I wish 與 if only 的懊悔、as if/as though 的比擬，以及 It's high time、would rather、without/but for 等常考特殊句型。只要掌握「時態後退」與各句型的固定公式，就能在填空與語法判斷題中穩定得分。

sections.h: 三種與事實相反的基本句型
sections.body: 與『現在』事實相反：If + 主詞 + 過去式(be 用 were)，主詞 + would/could/might + 原形。與『過去』事實相反：If + 主詞 + had + p.p.，主詞 + would/could/might + have + p.p.。與『未來』純想像：If + 主詞 + were to/should + 原形，主詞 + would + 原形。訣竅是動詞時態一律比真實時間往後退一格。
examples: If I had a time machine, I would go back to the exam and choose the right answer. 如果我有時光機，我會回到考試那天選對答案。
If you had woken up earlier, you would not have missed the bus. 如果你早點起床，就不會錯過公車了。
If the sun were to rise in the west, I would believe your excuse. 如果太陽打西邊出來，我才會相信你的藉口。

sections.h: 混合假設(Mixed Conditionals)
sections.body: 當『條件』和『結果』發生的時間不同時，兩個子句要各自用對應的時態。最常見為：過去的條件造成現在的結果——If + had + p.p.，主詞 + would + 原形(現在)。反過來，持續至今的個性或狀態(現在條件)造成過去的結果，則 If 子句用過去式，主要子句用 would have + p.p.。判斷關鍵是找出 now / today / last year 等時間副詞。
examples: If I had learned to swim as a child, I would not be so afraid of water now. 如果我小時候學過游泳，現在就不會這麼怕水。
If she were more careful, she would not have failed the driving test last week. 如果她個性再細心點，上週就不會考不過駕照。
If they had saved money earlier, they would be able to travel abroad this summer. 如果他們早點存錢，今年夏天就能出國旅行了。

sections.h: if 省略後的倒裝句
sections.body: 在正式或書面英文中，if 子句含有 were、had、should 時，可省略 if 並把這些字移到主詞前形成倒裝：If I were → Were I；If I had known → Had I known；If you should need → Should you need。倒裝後語氣更正式，是學測閱讀與填空的常客。否定不縮寫，寫成 Were it not for.../Had he not....
examples: Were I in your position, I would talk to the teacher honestly. 如果我是你，我會誠實地和老師談。
Had I known the test was today, I would have studied all night. 早知道今天考試，我就會念一整晚。
Should you have any questions about the assignment, please email me. 萬一你對作業有任何疑問，請寄電子郵件給我。

sections.h: I wish / If only 表達懊悔或願望
sections.body: I wish 與語氣更強的 If only 用來表達與事實相反的願望或懊悔。對『現在』不滿用過去式(be 用 were)；對『過去』後悔用 had + p.p.；希望他人改變未來行為用 would + 原形。三種時態各對應不同的遺憾焦點，務必看清句中時間線索。
examples: I wish I were a little braver when speaking English in class. 真希望我在課堂上說英文時能勇敢一點。
If only I had reviewed the vocabulary before the quiz! 要是我考前有複習單字就好了！
I wish my little brother would stop borrowing my things without asking. 真希望我弟弟別再不問一聲就拿我的東西。

sections.h: as if / as though 的比擬用法
sections.body: as if 與 as though 意為『彷彿、好像』。若比擬的內容與事實相反，動詞要用假設語氣：與現在相反用過去式(were)，與更早的過去相反用 had + p.p.。注意：若說話者認為內容可能為真，動詞則用一般直說法，需依語意判斷。
examples: He orders everyone around as if he were the class president. 他對每個人頤指氣使，彷彿他是班長似的。
She looked pale, as though she had not eaten anything all day. 她臉色蒼白，好像一整天都沒吃東西。
The old photos make me feel as if I were back in elementary school. 這些舊照片讓我彷彿回到了國小時光。

sections.h: 其他常考特殊句型
sections.body: 三個必背句型:(1) It's (high/about) time + 主詞 + 過去式，意為『早該…卻還沒』。(2) would rather + 主詞 + 過去式，表達對他人的希望；同主詞則用 would rather + 原形，對過去懊悔用 would rather have + p.p.。(3) without / but for = 『要不是；如果沒有』，等同 if 條件句，主要子句依現在或過去選 would + 原形或 would have + p.p.。
examples: It's high time we started saving the environment instead of just talking. 我們早該開始拯救環境，而不只是空談。
I would rather you didn't post that embarrassing photo of me online. 我寧願你別把我那張糗照片貼到網路上。
But for the streetlights, the whole neighborhood would have been in total darkness. 要不是有路燈，整個社區早就一片漆黑了。

mistakes: 誤：If I was you, I would study abroad. → 正：If I were you, I would study abroad.（與現在相反，be 動詞一律用 were）
誤：If she had studied harder, she would pass the exam. → 正：If she had studied harder, she would have passed the exam.（與過去相反，主要子句要用 would have + p.p.）
誤：It's high time we start reviewing for the test. → 正：It's high time we started reviewing for the test.（It's high time 後主詞要接過去式）
誤：I would rather you tell me the truth. → 正：I would rather you told me the truth.（would rather 接不同主詞時用過去式）
note.tip: 一句話記住核心：談現在退一格(過去式)、談過去退兩格(had + p.p.)，句型看到 wish / if only / as if / It's time / would rather / without 就立刻啟動『時態後退』開關。

d1.stem: If I ___ you, I would apologize to Mr. Lin for being late.
d1.explanation: 與現在事實相反的假設，if 子句動詞用過去式，be 動詞一律用 were，故選 were。

d1.stem: If she had reviewed her notes harder, she ___ the midterm exam.
d1.explanation: 與過去事實相反：if 子句用 had + p.p.，主要子句用 would have + p.p.，故選 would have passed。

d1.stem: If it ___ tomorrow, our class would cancel the outdoor sports day.
d1.explanation: 主要子句為 would cancel，屬與現在／未來事實相反句型，if 子句須用過去式 rained（rains 只能搭配 will cancel），故選 rained。

d1.stem: I wish I ___ taller so that I could join the school basketball team.
d1.explanation: I wish 表達與現在事實相反的願望，be 動詞用 were，故選 were。

d1.stem: If only I ___ harder last semester, my grades would be much better now.
d1.explanation: If only 對過去的懊悔（last semester），用 had + p.p.，故選 had studied。

d1.stem: He talks as if he ___ everything about the college entrance exam.
d1.explanation: as if 表達與現在事實相反（其實他並不全懂），動詞用過去式 knew，故選 knew。

d1.stem: It's high time we ___ preparing for the final exams.
d1.explanation: It's high time + 主詞 + 過去式，表示早該做卻還沒做，故選 started。

d1.stem: I would rather you ___ me the truth about the broken window right now.
d1.explanation: would rather + 主詞（不同人）+ 過去式，表示對現在的希望，故選 told。

d1.stem: If I had known you were in the hospital, I ___ you last weekend.
d1.explanation: 與過去事實相反，主要子句用 would have + p.p.，故選 would have visited。

d1.stem: Without water, no living creature on the earth ___ survive.
d1.explanation: Without = If there were no，屬與現在事實相反，主要子句用 could + 原形，故選 could。

d1.stem: ___ I known the correct answer, I would have raised my hand in class.
d1.explanation: 與過去相反的 if 省略時倒裝，把 had 移到句首：Had I known...，故選 Had。

d1.stem: If I were you, I ___ give up my dream of studying abroad so easily.
d1.explanation: 與現在事實相反，主要子句用 would/wouldn't + 原形，故選 wouldn't。

d2.stem: If I had taken your advice last year, I ___ a much more confident student now.
d2.explanation: 混合假設：條件在過去(had taken)，結果在現在(now)，主要子句用 would + 原形，故選 would be。

d2.stem: ___ you need any help with your science project, feel free to call me anytime.
d2.explanation: 未來不確定的假設 If you should need... 省略 if 倒裝成 Should you need...，故選 Should。

d2.stem: But for your timely help, we ___ the submission deadline yesterday.
d2.explanation: But for = If it had not been for，此處指過去(yesterday)，主要子句用 would have + p.p.，故選 would have missed。

d2.stem: The rookie played so calmly, as if he ___ in many championship finals before.
d2.explanation: as if 指比主要動詞更早的過去經驗（其實他沒打過），用 had + p.p.，故選 had played。

d2.stem: I wish I ___ the concert tickets earlier; now they are completely sold out.
d2.explanation: I wish 對過去的懊悔用 had + p.p.，故選 had bought。

d2.stem: The rules are so outdated; it's about time the school ___ the dress code.
d2.explanation: It's (about) time + 主詞 + 過去式，表示早該改卻還沒改，故選 changed。

d2.stem: My parents would rather I ___ medicine than art when I go to college.
d2.explanation: would rather + 主詞（不同人）+ 過去式，表達希望，故選 studied。

d2.stem: If the earth ___ to stop spinning suddenly, catastrophic disasters would follow.
d2.explanation: were to + 原形用於假設純屬想像、幾乎不可能的未來，be 用 were，故選 were。

d2.stem: Had she not missed the school bus, she ___ late for the entrance interview.
d2.explanation: Had she not missed 是與過去相反的倒裝條件，主要子句用 would(n't) have + p.p.，故選 wouldn't have been。

d2.stem: If our group ___ a little more time, we could finish the report properly.
d2.explanation: 與現在事實相反，if 子句用過去式 had，故選 had。

d2.stem: You look pale and tired, as though you ___ for two whole nights.
d2.explanation: as though 描述與過去事實相反且時間更早的狀態，用 had + p.p.，故選 hadn't slept。

d2.stem: I wish you ___ interrupting me every time I try to give my presentation.
d2.explanation: I wish + 主詞 + would 表示對他人行為的不滿並希望改變（未來），故選 would stop。

d3.stem: ___ he listened to his coach back then, he would be a national champion today.
d3.explanation: 混合假設倒裝：過去條件(back then)用 Had he listened，現在結果(today)用 would be，省略 if 後倒裝為 Had，故選 Had。

d3.stem: The campus is a mess after the festival; it's high time the student council ___ something about it.
d3.explanation: 語意陷阱：It's high time 雖談的是現在該做的事，動詞仍必須用過去式 did，故選 did。

d3.stem: But for his quick reaction just now, the whole chemistry experiment ___ ruined at this moment.
d3.explanation: But for 表假設，句尾 at this moment 指現在結果，主要子句用 would + 原形 be，故選 would be。

d3.stem: I would rather ___ at home reviewing than attend that pointless three-hour lecture.
d3.explanation: 陷阱：would rather 後接同一主詞時用原形動詞(stay)；只有接不同主詞的子句才用過去式，故選 stay。

d3.stem: Looking at his failing report card, he now wishes he ___ so much time on video games.
d3.explanation: wish 對過去行為的懊悔用 had + p.p.，否定為 hadn't spent，故選 hadn't spent。

d3.stem: ___ it not for the full scholarship, she ___ able to study at such a top university now.
d3.explanation: 與現在相反的倒裝 Were it not for（= If it were not for），現在結果用 wouldn't be，故選 Were / wouldn't be。

d3.stem: He answered every question instantly, as if he ___ the exam paper in advance.
d3.explanation: as if 表與事實相反且發生在更早時間（其實沒看過考卷），用 had + p.p.，故選 had seen。

d3.stem: ___ any student wish to appeal the final grade, he or she must submit a written request.
d3.explanation: If any student should wish... 省略 if 倒裝為 Should any student wish...，表未來較不確定的情況，故選 Should。

d3.stem: If she weren't so painfully shy, she ___ the class leadership award last year.
d3.explanation: 混合假設：條件是持續至今的個性(weren't)，結果在過去(last year)，用 would have + p.p.，故選 would have won。

d3.stem: The quarrel destroyed our friendship. If only I ___ my temper that afternoon.
d3.explanation: If only 對過去的深深懊悔，用 had + p.p.，故選 had controlled。

d3.stem: The guilt is unbearable; I would rather ___ the truth than lied to my parents.
d3.explanation: would rather 同主詞表對過去的懊悔時，用 would rather have + p.p.，與 than 後的 lied 對比，故選 have told。

d3.stem: There is no more time to hesitate; it's time for us ___ a final decision about the trip.
d3.explanation: 陷阱：此處是 It's time for + 受詞 + to V 結構，非 It's time + 主詞 + 過去式，故選 to make。

topic.label: 倒裝句
topic.scope: 否定副詞置句首(Never/Hardly...when/Not until/No sooner...than/Little)、only+副詞片語置句首、地方副詞倒裝(Here comes)、so/such...that 倒裝、so/neither/nor 附和句倒裝

note.title: 倒裝句
note.summary: 倒裝句(Inversion)是把助動詞或be動詞移到主詞前面,形成「疑問句語序」的特殊句型,常用來加強語氣或符合正式書面英語的規範。學測最常考的五大類為:(1)否定副詞置句首(Never/Hardly...when/Not until/No sooner...than/Little/Seldom/Not only),(2)only+副詞片語置句首,(3)地方或方向副詞置句首的全動詞倒裝(Here comes...),(4)so/such...that 的補語倒裝,(5)so/neither/nor 的附和句倒裝。掌握關鍵:先判斷「句首放了什麼」,再決定倒裝的形式與範圍。

sections.h: 一、否定副詞置句首:助動詞倒裝
sections.body: 當 Never, Hardly, Scarcely, Seldom, Rarely, Little, Not until, No sooner, Not only, Under no circumstances 等含否定意味的副詞(片語)放在句首時,主要子句要「部分倒裝」:把助動詞(do/does/did、have/has/had、will/can/should 等)移到主詞前面,像疑問句一樣。若原句沒有助動詞,就依時態補上 do/does/did。注意搭配:Hardly/Scarcely...when、No sooner...than。
examples: Never have I seen such a spectacular fireworks display. 我從未看過如此壯觀的煙火。
Not until the bell rang did the students stop writing. 直到鐘響,學生們才停筆。
No sooner had she opened the door than the cat rushed out. 她一打開門,貓就衝了出去。

sections.h: 二、Only + 副詞(片語/子句)置句首
sections.body: 當 only 修飾一個時間、地方、方式或條件的副詞片語或副詞子句並置於句首時,「主要子句」要部分倒裝(注意:倒裝的是主要子句,不是 only 引導的那一段)。常見:Only then, Only after..., Only when..., Only by..., Only in this way。
examples: Only after finishing the exam could we finally relax. 只有在考完試之後,我們才終於能放鬆。
Only when you fail do you truly understand the value of effort. 唯有失敗時,你才真正明白努力的價值。
Only by working together can we solve this problem. 唯有合作,我們才能解決這個問題。

sections.h: 三、地方/方向副詞置句首:全動詞倒裝
sections.body: 當表地方或方向的副詞(Here, There, Down, Up, In, Out, On the hill 等)放在句首,且動詞是不及物的 come/go/stand/lie/run 等時,採「全倒裝」:整個動詞(不用助動詞)移到主詞前。但關鍵陷阱:若主詞是「代名詞」則不倒裝!Here comes the bus.(名詞→倒裝) vs. Here it comes.(代名詞→不倒裝)。
examples: Here comes the school bus; hurry up! 校車來了,快點!
On the top of the mountain stands an ancient temple. 山頂上矗立著一座古廟。
There she goes, running toward the finish line. 她跑走了,朝終點線奔去。(代名詞不倒裝)

sections.h: 四、so / such ... that 的補語倒裝
sections.body: 把 so + 形容詞/副詞 或 such + 名詞片語 移到句首以加強語氣時,主要子句要倒裝。公式:So + adj./adv. + 助動詞/be + 主詞 + that...;Such + be動詞 + 主詞 + that...。注意 so 後面接形容詞或副詞,such 後面接名詞。
examples: So difficult was the test that half of the class failed. 那考試如此困難,以致全班一半的人不及格。
So heavily did it rain that the game was canceled. 雨下得如此大,以致比賽被取消。
Such was his determination that nothing could stop him. 他的決心如此堅定,沒有什麼能阻擋他。

sections.h: 五、so / neither / nor 附和句倒裝
sections.body: 要表達「某人也是這樣」時用倒裝附和句。肯定附和用 So + 助動詞/be + 主詞(某人也是);否定附和用 Neither/Nor + 助動詞/be + 主詞(某人也不是)。關鍵:助動詞的種類與時態要跟前句一致(前句 can 就用 can,前句過去式就用 did)。別跟 So + 主詞 + do(表「的確如此」)搞混。
examples: "I love K-pop." "So do I." 「我愛韓國流行樂。」「我也是。」
"She can't come to the party." "Neither can I." 「她不能來派對。」「我也不能。」
Tom passed the exam, and so did his best friend. 湯姆通過了考試,他的死黨也是。

mistakes: 誤:Never I have seen such a mess. → 正:Never have I seen such a mess.(否定副詞置句首,助動詞 have 要移到主詞 I 前面)
誤:No sooner had he arrived when the meeting started. → 正:No sooner had he arrived than the meeting started.(No sooner 固定搭配 than,不是 when;Hardly/Scarcely 才搭配 when)
誤:Here the bus comes. → 正:Here comes the bus.(地方副詞置句首且主詞為名詞時要全倒裝;但若主詞是代名詞 it 則為 Here it comes.)
誤:"I don't like coffee." "So do I." → 正:"I don't like coffee." "Neither do I."(否定句的附和要用 Neither/Nor,不能用 So)
note.tip: 看到句首是「否定副詞或 only 片語」就把主詞後的助動詞搬到主詞前(部分倒裝);看到句首是「地方副詞+不及物動詞」且主詞是名詞就整個動詞往前搬(全倒裝),但主詞是代名詞就不動!

d1.stem: Never ___ so tired in my whole life until I ran the marathon.
d1.explanation: 否定副詞 Never 置於句首,主要子句要部分倒裝,把助動詞 have 移到主詞 I 前面,故選 have I felt。

d1.stem: Here ___! Let's get on quickly before it leaves.
d1.explanation: 地方副詞 Here 置句首,動詞為不及物 come 且主詞為名詞 the bus,採全倒裝:Here comes the bus。

d1.stem: Not until midnight ___ his homework.
d1.explanation: Not until 引導的時間片語置句首,主要子句要倒裝並依過去式補助動詞 did:did he finish。

d1.stem: "I love watching basketball games." "___"
d1.explanation: 肯定句的附和用 So+助動詞+主詞;原句是一般動詞現在式,故用 So do I(我也是)。

d1.stem: "I haven't finished the report yet." "___"
d1.explanation: 否定句的附和用 Neither/Nor+助動詞+主詞;原句用現在完成式 haven't,故用 Neither have I。

d1.stem: Hardly ___ home when it suddenly began to rain.
d1.explanation: Hardly 置句首要倒裝,且 Hardly...when 慣用過去完成式,故為 had I arrived。

d1.stem: Little ___ that a surprise party was waiting for her at home.
d1.explanation: 否定副詞 Little(幾乎不)置句首,主要子句倒裝,依過去式補 did:did she know。

d1.stem: No sooner had the teacher left the room ___ the students started chatting.
d1.explanation: No sooner 固定搭配 than,不可用 when 或 then,故選 than。

d1.stem: Only after the final exam ___ finally relax.
d1.explanation: Only+時間片語置句首,主要子句倒裝,把助動詞 could 移到主詞前:could the students。

d1.stem: So ___ that we couldn't stop laughing all night.
d1.explanation: So+形容詞置句首要倒裝,be 動詞移到主詞前:So funny was the show that...。

d1.stem: Such ___ his devotion to the team that everyone respected him.
d1.explanation: Such+名詞置句首倒裝用 be 動詞;主詞 his devotion 為單數,故用 was:Such was his devotion。

d1.stem: "My sister enjoys hiking on weekends." "So ___ I."
d1.explanation: 附和「我也是」,原句為一般動詞現在式,主詞 I 用 do:So do I。

d2.stem: ___ had I closed my eyes than my phone buzzed with an urgent message.
d2.explanation: 空格後搭配 than,只有 No sooner...than 這個組合成立(Hardly/Scarcely 搭配 when),故選 No sooner。

d2.stem: Scarcely had the concert begun ___ the excited fans started screaming.
d2.explanation: Scarcely/Hardly 固定搭配 when,表「一…就…」,故選 when。

d2.stem: Not only ___ the first prize in the contest, but she also won a scholarship.
d2.explanation: Not only 置句首,主要子句要部分倒裝,依過去式補 did:did she win。

d2.stem: Only when we lost the championship ___ how important teamwork really was.
d2.explanation: Only when 引導副詞子句置句首,主要子句倒裝:did we realize。倒裝的是主句而非 only 子句。

d2.stem: "I didn't enjoy that horror movie at all." "___ It was too scary."
d2.explanation: 否定附和用 Neither+助動詞+主詞,語序不可顛倒;原句過去式故 Neither did I。

d2.stem: So severe ___ that all outdoor activities were canceled that day.
d2.explanation: So+形容詞 severe 置句首,主句倒裝,be 動詞移到主詞前:So severe was the storm that...。

d2.stem: Under no circumstances ___ your password with strangers online.
d2.explanation: Under no circumstances 為否定副詞片語置句首,主句部分倒裝,助動詞 should 移到主詞前:should you share。

d2.stem: Seldom ___ such a well-organized science fair at our high school.
d2.explanation: 否定副詞 Seldom 置句首要倒裝,現在完成式助動詞 have 移到主詞前:have we seen。

d2.stem: "Tom can solve this tricky math problem in seconds." "___ Mary; she's a genius too."
d2.explanation: 肯定附和「瑪莉也可以」,前句用 can,附和句助動詞須一致:So can Mary。

d2.stem: Not until she read the letter a second time ___ its true meaning.
d2.explanation: Not until 引導的時間片語置句首,主句倒裝並依過去式補 did:did she understand。

d2.stem: Such was his ___ that no one in the room dared to argue with him.
d2.explanation: Such+名詞的結構,his 後需接名詞,故用名詞 confidence(自信),confident 是形容詞、confidently 是副詞。

d2.stem: On the top of the green hill ___ an old wooden temple surrounded by pine trees.
d2.explanation: 地方副詞片語置句首,不及物動詞 stand 且主詞為名詞,採全倒裝:...stands an old temple。

d3.stem: Not until I got home from the restaurant ___ that I had left my wallet on the table.
d3.explanation: Not until 引導的時間片語置句首,主句倒裝;主句動作為過去簡單式,補助動詞 did:did I realize。

d3.stem: Rarely ___ a student who excels in both sports and academics, so we should treasure her talent.
d3.explanation: 否定副詞 Rarely 置句首,主句部分倒裝,現在式補 do:do we see。

d3.stem: ___ had the referee blown the whistle when the whole stadium erupted in cheers.
d3.explanation: 空格與 had...when 搭配,Scarcely/Hardly...when 才成立;No sooner 須配 than,Not until/Only 不接 when 子句,故選 Scarcely。

d3.stem: So absorbed ___ in the online game that they completely forgot about the project deadline.
d3.explanation: So+過去分詞當形容詞 absorbed 置句首,主句倒裝,be 動詞移前:were the players。

d3.stem: "I have never been abroad in my life." "___, but I'm saving money for a trip to Europe."
d3.explanation: 否定附和且前句用現在完成式 have never been,故用 Neither have I,助動詞須與前句一致。

d3.stem: Only by staying calm and thinking clearly ___ the emergency successfully.
d3.explanation: Only by+動名詞片語置句首,主句倒裝,助動詞 could 移到主詞前:could we handle。

d3.stem: Little did the coach know that his few encouraging words ___ the young athlete's life forever.
d3.explanation: Little did...know 為倒裝;that 子句敘述當時尚未發生、之後才發生的事,用過去未來式 would change 表「將會改變」。

d3.stem: Not a single word ___ during the entire ceremony; the hall remained completely silent.
d3.explanation: 含否定的 Not a single word 置句首,主句部分倒裝,過去式補 did:did he say。

d3.stem: Hardly had the new school policy been announced ___ complaints began to pour in from every class.
d3.explanation: Hardly 搭配 when,表「才剛…就…」;此處為被動完成式 had been announced,連接詞仍用 when。

d3.stem: So heavily ___ last night that this morning's football match had to be postponed.
d3.explanation: So+副詞 heavily 置句首,主句倒裝;原句無助動詞,依過去式補 did:did it rain。注意此為部分倒裝,主詞 it 後接原形 rain。

d3.stem: ___ does the charm of the old town lie in its ancient buildings, but also in the warmth of its people.
d3.explanation: 後半有 but also,構成 Not only...but also 句型;Not only 置句首,主句倒裝(does...lie),故選 Not only。

d3.stem: Down the runway ___, waving confidently to the audience as camera flashes lit up the hall.
d3.explanation: 方向副詞 Down 置句首,不及物動詞 walk 且主詞為名詞 the model,採全倒裝:walked the model。若主詞是代名詞則不倒裝。

topic.label: 分詞與分詞構句
topic.scope: 現在分詞/過去分詞當形容詞、分詞構句簡化副詞子句、獨立分詞構句(不同主詞)、with + O + 分詞、情緒動詞現在分詞vs過去分詞(exciting/excited)、Having p.p.

note.title: 分詞與分詞構句
note.summary: 分詞是動詞的變身,分成現在分詞(V-ing,表主動/進行)與過去分詞(V-p.p.,表被動/完成)。它最常做兩件事:一是當形容詞修飾名詞(a crying baby、a broken window),二是把副詞子句(表時間、原因、條件、讓步、連續動作)簡化成更精簡的分詞構句。本單元一次掌握六大重點:分詞當形容詞的主被動判斷、分詞構句的簡化步驟、情緒動詞 exciting/excited 的用法、主詞不同時的獨立分詞構句、with + O + 分詞的附帶狀態,以及強調先後的完成分詞 Having p.p.。學會後,你的句子會更精煉,也能看懂學測閱讀測驗裡大量出現的分詞結構。

sections.h: 重點一:現在分詞 vs 過去分詞當形容詞
sections.body: 現在分詞(V-ing)表「主動、正在進行」,被修飾的名詞是動作的發出者;過去分詞(V-p.p.)表「被動、已完成」,被修飾的名詞是動作的承受者。例如 running water(流動的水,水主動流)相對於 boiled water(煮沸過的水,水被煮)。分詞可放在名詞前(a sleeping cat),若帶有其他字詞則放名詞後(the boy standing at the gate)。判斷口訣:名詞會自己做這動作 → V-ing;名詞是被這動作處理 → p.p.。
examples: The barking dog scared the mail carrier. 那隻吠叫的狗嚇到了郵差。
The letter written in French was really hard to read. 那封用法文寫的信真的很難讀。
Look at the sleeping cat lying on the sofa. 看看沙發上那隻睡著的貓。

sections.h: 重點二:用分詞構句簡化副詞子句
sections.body: 當副詞子句(表時間、原因、條件、讓步或連續動作)和主要子句的主詞相同時,可簡化成分詞構句,讓句子更精煉。三步驟:(1)去掉連接詞(because, when, as, after...);(2)兩句主詞相同,去掉副詞子句的主詞;(3)把動詞改成分詞——原本主動用 V-ing,原本被動用 p.p.(being 通常省略)。注意:分詞的邏輯主詞一定要和主要子句主詞一致,否則就是錯誤的「垂懸分詞」。
examples: Because she felt sick, she stayed home. → Feeling sick, she stayed home. 因為覺得不舒服,她待在家。
When it is heated, ice turns into water. → Heated, ice turns into water. 冰一被加熱就變成水。
He walked into the room and turned on the light. → Walking into the room, he turned on the light. 他走進房間,把燈打開。

sections.h: 重點三:情緒動詞 -ing 令人 vs -ed 感受
sections.body: 情緒動詞(interest, excite, bore, surprise, tire, satisfy, disappoint, shock, frighten...)本意是「使人產生某種情緒」。用 V-ing 表「令人感到…的」,通常修飾事物或原因;用 V-p.p. 表「(人)感到…的」,通常修飾人。關鍵在於:主詞是『引發』情緒(用 -ing),還是『感受』情緒(用 -ed)。這是學測與段考最愛考的陷阱。
examples: The game was exciting, so all the players were excited. 比賽很刺激,所以所有球員都很興奮。
I was disappointed with the disappointing final score. 我對那令人失望的最終比分感到失望。
The boring lecturer made the whole class bored. 那位無聊的講者讓全班都覺得無聊。

sections.h: 重點四:獨立分詞構句 與 with + O + 分詞
sections.body: 若副詞子句和主要子句『主詞不同』,分詞前必須保留自己的主詞,形成『獨立分詞構句』,如 The sun having set, we went home(太陽下山了,我們就回家)。常見慣用形式有:There being...(因為有/沒有…)、Weather permitting(天氣許可的話)、All things considered(整體考量)、Generally speaking(一般而言)。此外,『with + 受詞 + 分詞』用來描述『附帶狀態』:受詞主動做動作用 V-ing,受詞被動承受用 p.p.。
examples: The rain having stopped, the children ran out to play. 雨停了,孩子們跑出去玩。
She fell asleep with the TV still playing. 她睡著了,電視還開著(playing,電視主動播放)。
He listened to the teacher with his arms folded. 他抱著雙臂聽老師講(folded,手臂被交疊)。

sections.h: 重點五:完成分詞 Having p.p. 表先後
sections.body: 當分詞的動作『明顯發生在主要子句動作之前』,用『Having + p.p.』(完成主動)或『Having been + p.p.』(完成被動)來強調先後順序;若兩動作幾乎同時發生,用一般分詞即可。否定則把 not/never 放在分詞之前,如 Not having finished...、Never having seen...。
examples: Having finished her homework, she went out for a walk. 寫完功課後,她才出門散步。
Having been warned several times, he still broke the rule. 被警告過好幾次,他還是犯規。
Not having slept the night before, she could hardly stay awake in class. 前一晚沒睡,她上課幾乎撐不住。

mistakes: 誤:I am so boring in this math class. → 正:I am so bored in this math class.(人『感受』無聊要用 -ed;用 boring 會變成『我這個人很無聊』)
誤:Seeing from the airplane, the rice fields look like a chessboard. → 正:Seen from the airplane, the rice fields look like a chessboard.(稻田是『被看』,主詞被動須用過去分詞 Seen)
誤:Finished the exam, she felt relieved. → 正:Having finished the exam, she felt relieved.(她『主動』考完試,動作又先發生,應用完成分詞 Having finished 或 Finishing,不可用光禿禿的被動 Finished)
誤:Walking to school, the heavy rain started to fall. → 正:Walking to school, I was caught in the heavy rain.(垂懸分詞:分詞主詞須與主要子句一致,雨不會自己走路上學)
note.tip: 一句話記牢:主動進行用 -ing、被動完成用 p.p.;人感受用 -ed、事物令人用 -ing;動作先發生就加 Having。

d1.stem: The little girl was frightened by the ___ dog next door.
d1.explanation: 狗是主動發出吠叫的動作，用現在分詞 barking 當形容詞修飾 dog（會吠的狗）。

d1.stem: I found the three-hour movie really ___ and almost fell asleep.
d1.explanation: 電影是『令人厭煩的』，屬引發情緒的事物，用現在分詞 boring。若用 bored 則變成電影自己有感受，不合理。

d1.stem: Be careful of the ___ leaves on the ground; they are quite slippery.
d1.explanation: 葉子已經落在地上，是完成的狀態，用過去分詞 fallen（已落下的）。falling 是『正在飄落』，與 on the ground 不合。

d1.stem: ___ very tired after the exam, she went to bed right away.
d1.explanation: 分詞構句還原為 Because she felt tired，she 主動感到累，用現在分詞 Feeling。

d1.stem: All the students were ___ about the coming summer vacation.
d1.explanation: 主詞是 students（人），表示『感到興奮的』，用過去分詞 excited。exciting 是用來形容令人興奮的事物。

d1.stem: ___ in simple English, this novel is suitable for beginners.
d1.explanation: 小說是『被寫』的，主詞 novel 為被動，分詞構句用過去分詞 Written。

d1.stem: He leaned back in the chair with his legs ___.
d1.explanation: with + O + 分詞表附帶狀態；雙腿是『被交疊』的（被動），用過去分詞 crossed。

d1.stem: The result of the election was truly ___ to almost everyone.
d1.explanation: 選舉結果是『令人震驚的』事物，用現在分詞 shocking。shocked 用來形容感到震驚的人。

d1.stem: ___ down the street, I ran into an old classmate from junior high.
d1.explanation: 分詞構句還原為 While I was walking，主詞 I 主動走路，用現在分詞 Walking。

d1.stem: Please sweep up the ___ glass before someone gets hurt.
d1.explanation: 玻璃是『被打破』的，已完成且被動，用過去分詞 broken（破掉的玻璃）。

d1.stem: The baby is ___ in the next room, so please keep your voice down.
d1.explanation: 寶寶正在睡覺，主動且進行中，be + 現在分詞 sleeping 表進行式。

d1.stem: I was really ___ in the documentary about deep-sea creatures.
d1.explanation: 主詞是 I（人），表示『感到有興趣的』，用過去分詞 interested；固定搭配 be interested in。

d2.stem: ___ no bus in sight, we had no choice but to walk home in the rain.
d2.explanation: 這是獨立分詞構句，由 There was no bus 簡化而來，保留 there 並將 be 改為分詞，故用 There being 表原因。

d2.stem: The professor spoke far too fast, ___ most of the freshmen totally confused.
d2.explanation: 分詞構句表『連續／結果』動作，主詞（professor）主動造成結果，用現在分詞 leaving（= and left...）。

d2.stem: ___ by the sudden storm, the beach volleyball match was called off.
d2.explanation: 比賽是『被』暴風雨影響，主詞 match 為被動，用過去分詞 Affected。

d2.stem: With the final deadline fast ___, the whole team stayed up all night.
d2.explanation: with + O + 分詞；deadline 主動『逼近』，用現在分詞 approaching 表進行中的附帶狀態。

d2.stem: ___ what to do next, the nervous new intern just stood there awkwardly.
d2.explanation: 分詞構句的否定要把 not 放在分詞之前；intern 主動『不知道』，用 Not knowing。

d2.stem: The coach found the team's recent progress ___ and decided to raise the goal.
d2.explanation: progress（進步）是『令人滿意的』事物，用現在分詞 satisfying。satisfied 用來形容感到滿意的人。

d2.stem: ___ the same careless error twice, Kevin was determined to double-check everything.
d2.explanation: 犯錯發生在『下定決心』之前，強調先後，用完成分詞 Having made。

d2.stem: The fans watched the deciding set, with their eyes ___ on the ball the whole time.
d2.explanation: with + O + 分詞；eyes 是『被固定／緊盯』（be fixed on），為被動，用過去分詞 fixed。

d2.stem: ___ from the observation deck, the whole city looks like a glittering circuit board.
d2.explanation: 主要子句主詞是 city，城市是『被看』的，故用過去分詞 Seen。若用 Seeing 會變成城市自己看，是垂懸分詞錯誤。

d2.stem: All things ___, studying abroad right after high school may not suit everyone.
d2.explanation: All things considered（整體考量）是慣用的獨立分詞構句；things 是『被考量』，用過去分詞 considered。

d2.stem: The evening lecture was so ___ that several students dozed off in the back row.
d2.explanation: lecture 是『令人厭煩的』事物，用現在分詞 boring；dozed off 正說明它使人想睡。

d2.stem: The weather ___ unexpectedly fine, we decided to hike up to the waterfall.
d2.explanation: 主要子句主詞是 we，與 weather 不同，屬獨立分詞構句，保留主詞 weather 並將 be 改為分詞 being。

d3.stem: ___ by the disaster but not defeated, the villagers began to rebuild their ruined homes.
d3.explanation: 村民是『被』災難震撼（被動），且與後面的 not defeated 形成平行的過去分詞結構，故用 Shaken。

d3.stem: Generally ___ the toughest exam in Taiwan, the GSAT demands months of hard preparation.
d3.explanation: GSAT 是『被視為』最難的考試，還原為 which is generally considered...，分詞構句用被動的過去分詞 considered（being 省略）。

d3.stem: ___ that she had never lived abroad, her almost native accent truly amazed the judges.
d3.explanation: Given that... 是慣用的分詞介系詞，意為『考慮到、有鑑於』，固定用過去分詞 Given。

d3.stem: There ___ no objection from the board members, the merger proposal was passed at once.
d3.explanation: 獨立分詞構句 There being no objection（= As there was no objection）表原因，保留 there 並用分詞 being。

d3.stem: The outdoor graduation ceremony will be held next Friday, weather ___.
d3.explanation: weather permitting（天氣許可的話）是慣用獨立分詞構句；weather 主動『允許』，用現在分詞 permitting。

d3.stem: ___ so humiliated in her entire life, she wished the floor would open up and swallow her.
d3.explanation: 表達『這輩子從未有過』的先前經驗狀態（含 in her entire life），須用完成分詞並把 never 置前，故為 Never having felt。

d3.stem: ___ properly, these leftovers can give you serious food poisoning, so refrigerate them at once.
d3.explanation: 還原為 If they are not stored properly；leftovers 是『被』儲存（被動）且否定，用 Not stored。

d3.stem: The ancient temple, ___ countless earthquakes over the centuries, still stands proudly today.
d3.explanation: 歷經多次地震是延續到現在、先於『still stands』的完成動作（over the centuries），用完成主動分詞 having survived 最貼切。

d3.stem: With the final exams finally ___, the seniors could at last relax and plan their summer trips.
d3.explanation: with + O + 分詞；配合 finally 表『考試已結束』的完成狀態，exams 為被完成，用過去分詞 finished。

d3.stem: ___ from a distance, the messy wall paint actually forms a stunning portrait of a famous poet.
d3.explanation: 主要子句主詞是 the wall paint，牆面是『被觀看』的，用過去分詞 Viewed；用 Viewing 會變成牆自己看，屬垂懸分詞。

d3.stem: ___ twice by the very same online scam, Leo finally learned to verify every link before clicking.
d3.explanation: Leo 是『被』詐騙（被動），且動作先於『learned』發生，須用完成被動分詞 Having been fooled。

topic.label: 比較句進階
topic.scope: 倍數比較(twice/three times as...as, three times the size of)、the 比較級...the 比較級、比較級 and 比較級(越來越)、no more...than/not so much...as、比較級表最高級(比誰都)、less...than、the+比較級 of the two

note.title: 比較句進階
note.summary: 比較句除了基本的「A 比 B 更…」之外，還有許多學測常考的進階句型。本單元帶你掌握七大重點：倍數比較(twice/three times as…as、three times the size of)、「the 比較級…the 比較級」(越…就越…)、「比較級 and 比較級」(越來越…)、no more…than 與 not so much…as、用比較級或否定詞表達最高級(比誰都…)、less…than(較不…)，以及 the+比較級 of the two(兩者中較…的)。這些句型看似零散，核心都是『在比較的架構上加上倍數、程度變化或語意轉折』。熟練後，無論克漏字、文意選填還是作文，你都能寫出更精準、更道地的英文。

sections.h: 1. 倍數比較：as…as 與 the+名詞 of
sections.body: 表達『幾倍』有兩種常見寫法。第一種是『倍數 + as + 原級形容詞/副詞 + as』，例如 twice as tall as(兩倍高)、three times as many as(三倍多)。注意倍數(twice、three times)放在最前面，中間永遠用『原級』不是比較級。第二種是『倍數 + the + 名詞(size/height/length/weight/number)+ of』，如 three times the size of(三倍大)。也可改寫成『倍數 + 比較級 + than』，例如 three times bigger than。要特別留意可數用 as many、不可數用 as much。
examples: The new gym is three times as large as the old one. 新體育館是舊的三倍大。
Their house is twice the size of ours. 他們的房子是我們家的兩倍大。
I spent twice as much money on books as my roommate did. 我花在書上的錢是室友的兩倍。

sections.h: 2. the 比較級…the 比較級：越…就越…
sections.body: 句型為『The + 比較級 + 主詞 + 動詞, the + 比較級 + 主詞 + 動詞』，前半為條件、後半為結果，中間用逗號隔開。兩個比較級都必須把 the 放在最前面，且比較級要提到子句開頭(語序調動)。若形容詞是多音節，要用 the more + 原級。常見於勵志、學習、健康等學測主題。
examples: The harder you work, the luckier you get. 你越努力，運氣就越好。
The more we practice speaking, the more fluent we become. 我們越常開口練習，就越流利。
The older he grew, the wiser he became. 他年紀越大，就越有智慧。

sections.h: 3. 比較級 and 比較級：越來越…
sections.body: 表達『某狀態持續加深』用『比較級 + and + 比較級』，如 hotter and hotter(越來越熱)、bigger and bigger。多音節形容詞則用『more and more + 原級』，如 more and more difficult(越來越難)，不可寫成 difficult and difficult。常搭配 become、get、grow 等變化動詞使用。
examples: The days are getting longer and longer as summer approaches. 隨著夏天接近，白天變得越來越長。
The exam questions are becoming more and more challenging every year. 考題一年比一年更有挑戰性。
She felt more and more confident as the interview went on. 面試進行中，她感到越來越有自信。

sections.h: 4. no more…than 與 not so much…as
sections.body: 『A is no more X than B is (X)』表示『A 不是 X，正如 B 不是 X 一樣』，兩者一律否定，語氣強烈。例如 A whale is no more a fish than a horse is.(鯨魚不是魚，就像馬不是魚)。『not so much A as B』意為『與其說是 A 不如說是 B』，重點在後者 B。這兩個句型是學測進階常見的語意陷阱，關鍵在看懂『兩者皆否定』與『重心在後』。
examples: A computer is no more alive than a rock is. 電腦不是活的，就像石頭不是活的一樣。
He is not so much a scholar as a dreamer. 與其說他是學者，不如說他是個夢想家。
Our failure was not so much bad luck as poor planning. 我們的失敗與其說是運氣差，不如說是規劃不當。

sections.h: 5. 用比較級/否定詞表達最高級：比誰都…
sections.body: 不用 the most 也能表達『最…』。方法一：『否定主詞(Nothing/No one/No other)+ 比較級 + than』，如 Nothing is more precious than time.(時間最寶貴)。方法二：『比較級 + than any other + 單數名詞』，如 Taipei is larger than any other city…。方法三：『比較級 + than anyone else』。這是把『第一名』用『打敗其他所有人』的邏輯呈現，語意即最高級。
examples: No other student in our class studies harder than May. 班上沒有人比 May 更用功。(May 最用功)
Nothing makes me happier than a good night's sleep. 沒什麼比一夜好眠更讓我開心。
Mount Jade is higher than any other mountain in Taiwan. 玉山比台灣其他任何一座山都高。

sections.h: 6. less…than 與 the+比較級 of the two
sections.body: 『less + 原級 + than』表示『較不…』，是 more…than 的反義，常用於不可數概念或委婉語氣，如 less interesting than(沒那麼有趣)。可數東西減少要用 fewer。另外，當比較對象只有『兩者』時，較…的那一方要加 the：『the + 比較級 + of the two』，如 Of the two, Amy is the taller.(兩人中 Amy 較高)。這個 the 不可省略，是學測易失分處。
examples: This method is less complicated than the one we tried before. 這方法沒有我們之前試的那麼複雜。
Of the two proposals, this one is the more practical. 兩個提案中，這個比較實際。
There was less traffic today than yesterday. 今天的車流量比昨天少。

mistakes: 誤：This box is three times heavier as that one. → 正：This box is three times as heavy as that one.(用 as…as，中間放原級，倍數擺最前面)
誤：The exam is getting difficult and difficult. → 正：The exam is getting more and more difficult.(多音節形容詞的『越來越』要用 more and more，不可重複原級)
誤：Of the two sisters, Anna is taller. → 正：Of the two sisters, Anna is the taller.(限定兩者中較…的一方，比較級前必須加 the)
誤：No one runs fast than Tom in our team.(想表達 Tom 跑最快) → 正：No one runs faster than Tom in our team.(用否定主詞表最高級時，than 前面務必是比較級 faster)
note.tip: 看到倍數或『兩者取一』就先想 the：倍數比較夾在 as…as 的原級中間，而『the+比較級 of the two』的 the 千萬別漏。

d1.stem: My backpack is twice as ___ as yours.
d1.explanation: 倍數比較用『倍數 + as + 原級 + as』，twice(兩倍)後面接原級 heavy，不可用比較級 heavier 或最高級 heaviest。

d1.stem: The more you read, the ___ you know.
d1.explanation: 『the 比較級…the 比較級』句型，前面用 the more，後面也要對應用 the more，表示『你讀得越多，知道得越多』。

d1.stem: It's getting ___ outside, so we'd better turn on the lights.
d1.explanation: 單音節形容詞 dark 的『越來越…』用『比較級 and 比較級』，即 darker and darker，不用 more and more。

d1.stem: This building is three times ___ high as that one.
d1.explanation: 倍數比較的固定結構是『倍數 + as + 原級 + as』，第一個空格要用 as，構成 three times as high as。

d1.stem: To me, math is ___ interesting than science, so I prefer science.
d1.explanation: 表達『較不…』用『less + 原級 + than』，less interesting than 意為『沒那麼有趣』。least 是最高級、little 不與 than 連用。

d1.stem: Of the two movies, this one is ___ exciting.
d1.explanation: 比較對象只有兩者(of the two)時，較…的一方比較級前要加 the，故用 the more exciting。

d1.stem: The weather is becoming ___ these days.
d1.explanation: warm 為單音節，『越來越暖』用 warmer and warmer；不可用 more and more warm，也不可只變一邊。

d1.stem: ___ is more important than health; without it, we can do nothing.
d1.explanation: 用『否定主詞 + 比較級 + than』表達最高級，Nothing is more important than health 意為『健康最重要』。

d1.stem: Their new house is twice ___ size of their old one.
d1.explanation: 另一種倍數說法是『倍數 + the + 名詞 + of』，twice the size of 表示『兩倍大』，size 前用 the。

d1.stem: The ___ friends you have, the happier you feel.
d1.explanation: 『the 比較級…the 比較級』句型，friends 為可數複數，其比較級用 the more(不是 many/much)。

d1.stem: I have three times as ___ books as my brother does.
d1.explanation: books 為可數複數，as…as 中間要用 many；不可數才用 much。倍數比較用原級 as many。

d1.stem: As the marathon went on, the runners grew more and ___ tired.
d1.explanation: tired 為多音節，『越來越累』用 more and more tired，故空格填 more。

d2.stem: A dolphin is ___ a fish than a bat is a bird.
d2.explanation: 『A is no more X than B is』表示『A 不是 X，正如 B 不是 X』，兩者皆否定。海豚不是魚，就像蝙蝠不是鳥。

d2.stem: His achievement was not so much a matter of talent ___ of hard work.
d2.explanation: 『not so much A as B』意為『與其說是 A 不如說是 B』，固定搭配是 not so much…as，重心在 B(hard work)。

d2.stem: Taipei 101 is taller than ___ building in the city.
d2.explanation: 用比較級表最高級時，須用『比較級 + than any other + 單數名詞』，any other building 排除自己以外的所有建築。

d2.stem: We spent twice as ___ money on the trip as we had originally planned.
d2.explanation: money 為不可數，as…as 中間要用 much；倍數比較一律用原級，故 twice as much money as。

d2.stem: ___ it gets, the more slippery the mountain roads become.
d2.explanation: 『the 比較級…the 比較級』句型，前半子句的比較級 colder 要把 the 提到句首，構成 The colder it gets。

d2.stem: This year's typhoon caused ___ damage than last year's did.
d2.explanation: damage(損害)為不可數名詞，較少要用 less；fewer 只能修飾可數名詞。

d2.stem: Ann and Bella are both bright, but Bella is ___ of the two.
d2.explanation: 比較對象僅『兩人』(of the two)，較聰明者比較級前須加 the，用 the smarter，而非最高級 smartest。

d2.stem: She is ___ talented than her twin sister; they are equally gifted.
d2.explanation: 『no less…than』表示『不亞於、同樣地…』。句意為兩人同樣有才華，故用 no less talented than。

d2.stem: The less you worry about making mistakes, the ___ you will speak.
d2.explanation: 『the 比較級…the 比較級』後半要用比較級，freely 為副詞，其比較級為 more freely。

d2.stem: The observation tower is about three times ___ of the old temple beside it.
d2.explanation: 『倍數 + the + 名詞 + of』的用法，three times the height of 表示『三倍高』；若用 as…as 則需寫成 three times as high as。

d2.stem: With each passing week of practice, she grew ___ in giving English speeches.
d2.explanation: confident 為多音節，『越來越有自信』用 more and more confident，不可重複原級。

d2.stem: The real problem lies not so much in the plan itself ___ in the way it is carried out.
d2.explanation: 固定句型『not so much A as B』，與 A(in the plan)相對的 B 前面用 as，表示『與其說在計畫本身，不如說在執行方式』。

d3.stem: A house without love is ___ a home than a cage is a nest.
d3.explanation: 『A is no more X than B is』兩者皆否定：沒有愛的房子算不上家，就像籠子算不上鳥巢一樣。

d3.stem: The greater the challenge we face, ___.
d3.explanation: 『the 比較級…the 比較級』後半可省略 be 動詞，形成 the greater the reward(=the reward is greater)，前後結構須對稱。

d3.stem: Never in my life have I read a ___ novel than this one; it kept me up all night.
d3.explanation: 句中有 than，屬比較級結構，故用 more gripping；搭配 Never…have I 的倒裝與 than 比較，語意上等於『最扣人心弦』。

d3.stem: What truly upset her was not so much his harsh words ___ the cold tone in which he said them.
d3.explanation: 『not so much A as B』固定搭配，B(the cold tone)前用 as，強調傷人的與其說是話語，不如說是語氣。

d3.stem: Only two runners remain in the final, and Kevin is by far ___ experienced of the two.
d3.explanation: 限定在『兩人之中』(of the two)，較…的一方比較級前加 the，用 the more experienced；by far 用來加強比較級。

d3.stem: Honestly, I felt ___ annoyed than amused by his ridiculous excuse.
d3.explanation: 『less A than B』意為『與其說 A 不如說 B』，less annoyed than amused 表示『與其說生氣，不如說覺得好笑』，符合『荒謬的藉口』語境。

d3.stem: The city's population has grown to nearly ___ what it was two decades ago.
d3.explanation: 倍數用 three times(three 為複數用 times)，接名詞子句 what it was，表示『成長到約三倍』。three time、triple time 均為錯誤形式。

d3.stem: No other beach on the island draws ___ visitors in summer than this one does.
d3.explanation: 用『否定主詞 + 比較級 + than』表最高級，visitors 為可數複數，其比較級為 more(不是 many/most)，意為『這個海灘夏天遊客最多』。

d3.stem: The harder the wind blew against the sail, ___ the little boat moved forward.
d3.explanation: 『the 比較級…the 比較級』後半須用比較級並把 the 提前，副詞 slowly 的比較級為 more slowly，故用 the more slowly。

d3.stem: To everyone's relief, the final exam was ___ difficult as we had feared, and most of us passed.
d3.explanation: 否定的同級比較用『not so + 原級 + as』，not so difficult as 意為『沒有…那麼難』，比 not as 更正式，符合語境。

d3.stem: ___ than 500 volunteers signed up for the beach cleanup — far beyond what the organizers had expected.
d3.explanation: volunteers 為可數，『多達』要用 no fewer than(強調數量之多)；no less than 多用於不可數或金額，是常見誤用陷阱。

d3.stem: Frankly, John knows ___ about repairing cars than I do — which means neither of us knows a thing.
d3.explanation: 『A knows no more…than B』表示兩者都不懂：John 對修車的了解不比我多，也就是兩人都一竅不通，符合後半句語意。

topic.label: 強調句與分裂句
topic.scope: It is/was...that/who 分裂句(強調主詞、受詞、時間、地方副詞)、do/does/did + 原形強調動詞、the very + 名詞、in the world/on earth 加強疑問詞、not...until 的分裂句

note.title: 強調句與分裂句
note.summary: 當我們想讓句子中某個成分「特別突出」時，英文有一整套強調（emphasis）與分裂句（cleft sentence）的手法。最核心的是「It is/was ... that/who ...」分裂句，可以把主詞、受詞、時間或地方副詞抽出來重點強調；此外還有用助動詞 do/does/did + 原形動詞來加強語氣的動詞、the very + 名詞表示「正是這個」、在疑問詞後加 in the world / on earth 表達驚訝或不耐，以及 not...until 搭配分裂句或倒裝的用法。這些句型是學測、指考閱讀與寫作常見的加分利器，掌握結構與『that 通吃』的規則就能靈活運用。

sections.h: It is/was ... that/who 分裂句
sections.body: 把一個普通句子拆成兩半，用 It is/was 放在句首，緊接想強調的成分，其餘的話放進 that 子句，就是「分裂句」。可以強調主詞、受詞、時間副詞或地方副詞。最重要的規則是：不論強調的是人、事、時間還是地點，連接詞幾乎都用 that；只有強調『人』時可以用 who 代替 that。時態由原句決定：原句過去式，It was；原句現在式，It is。虛主詞 It 永遠用單數，就算後面接複數也用 was，不能用 were。
examples: It was Kevin who broke the school record last month. 上個月打破校紀錄的正是 Kevin。（強調主詞，人用 who）
It was in the gym that we held the farewell party. 我們是在體育館舉辦歡送會的。（強調地方，仍用 that）
It was last Friday that I handed in my application. 我是上週五交出申請表的。（強調時間，用 that）

sections.h: do/does/did + 原形動詞：強調動詞
sections.body: 想加強語氣、表達「真的、確實」時，可在動詞前加助動詞 do/does/did，後面的主要動詞一律回到原形。主詞第三人稱單數用 does，過去式用 did，其餘用 do。因為助動詞已經承擔了時態與人稱，主要動詞絕不能再加 s、ed 或改成過去式。這種強調常用於反駁、對比或再三保證的語氣。
examples: I do believe you can pass the exam. 我真的相信你能通過考試。
She does care about her classmates, though she looks cold. 她其實很在乎同學，只是看起來冷淡。
We did finish the whole report before the deadline. 我們確實在期限前完成了整份報告。

sections.h: the very + 名詞：正是這個
sections.body: very 除了當「非常」的副詞，還能放在 the 與名詞之間當形容詞，表示「正是、就是這個」，用來精準指出所講的人事物。結構固定為 the very + 名詞，強調獨一無二、不多不少剛好就是它。後面常再接關係子句補充說明。
examples: This is the very book I have been searching for all week. 這正是我找了一整週的那本書。
You are the very person I wanted to talk to. 你就是我想找的那個人。
That café was the very place where they first met. 那家咖啡廳正是他們初次相遇的地方。

sections.h: in the world / on earth：加強疑問詞
sections.body: 在 what、who、where、when、why、how 等疑問詞之後，加上 on earth 或 in the world，可強化語氣，表達強烈的驚訝、疑惑或不耐煩，中文近似「到底、究竟」。兩者可互換，但介系詞要記牢：是 on earth（在地球上）與 in the world（在世界上），千萬不要說成 in earth。這個片語放在疑問詞正後方。
examples: What on earth are you doing on the roof? 你到底在屋頂上做什麼？
Why in the world did he quit such a good job? 他究竟為什麼要辭掉這麼好的工作？
How on earth did you solve that difficult problem? 你到底是怎麼解出那道難題的？

sections.h: not ... until 的分裂句與倒裝
sections.body: not...until 表示「直到...才」。要強調『直到某時間點才發生』時，有兩種進階寫法：一是分裂句『It is/was not until + 時間 + that + 主要子句』；二是把 Not until 移到句首形成倒裝，主要子句的助動詞要提到主詞前。兩種都在強調『之前一直沒發生，到那一刻才發生』。倒裝時記得只倒裝主要子句，until 引導的子句維持正常語序。
examples: It was not until midnight that he finished his homework. 他直到半夜才寫完功課。
Not until the teacher explained it again did I understand the rule. 直到老師再解釋一次，我才懂那條規則。
It was not until she left that I realized how much I missed her. 直到她離開，我才明白我有多想念她。

mistakes: 誤：It was in the park where we first met. → 正：It was in the park that we first met.（分裂句一律用 that，即使強調地方也不用 where）
誤：I do finished my homework already. → 正：I did finish my homework already.（do/does/did 之後動詞要用原形，不可再加 ed）
誤：What in earth are you talking about? → 正：What on earth are you talking about?（加強疑問詞是 on earth 或 in the world，沒有 in earth）
note.tip: 分裂句的萬用口訣：「It is/was 夾出重點，中間用 that 全包辦，只有強調人才換 who」；強調動詞則記「do/does/did 之後動詞永遠光溜溜（原形）」。

d1.stem: It ___ Amy who won the English speech contest last spring.
d1.explanation: 分裂句「It is/was...who...」用來強調主詞。時間 last spring 為過去，故用 was；虛主詞 It 一律用單數。

d1.stem: It was in the school library ___ I first met my best friend.
d1.explanation: 分裂句不論強調什麼成分，連接詞都用 that，即使強調地方也不用 where。

d1.stem: Believe me, I ___ lock the door before we left this morning.
d1.explanation: 強調動詞用助動詞 + 原形。動作發生在 this morning（過去），主詞 I，故用 did lock。

d1.stem: This is the ___ pen my grandfather gave me before he passed away.
d1.explanation: the very + 名詞 表示「正是這一支」，強調獨一無二的那個東西。

d1.stem: Where ___ earth did I leave my umbrella?
d1.explanation: 加強疑問詞用 on earth 或 in the world。介系詞是 on earth，不可說 in earth。

d1.stem: It was my coach ___ believed in me when no one else did.
d1.explanation: 分裂句強調的是「人」（coach），關係詞用 who（也可用 that）。

d1.stem: My little sister ___ love drawing cartoons in her free time.
d1.explanation: 強調現在的習慣，主詞為第三人稱單數，用 does + 原形動詞 love。

d1.stem: It was not until the bell rang ___ the students stopped writing.
d1.explanation: 「It was not until...that...」是固定的分裂句結構，直到...才，連接詞用 that。

d1.stem: What ___ the world are you talking about?
d1.explanation: 加強疑問詞的片語為 in the world 或 on earth，此處搭配 in 為 in the world。

d1.stem: It was on a rainy Friday ___ we moved into our new house.
d1.explanation: 強調時間副詞的分裂句仍用 that，不可用 when 或 which。

d1.stem: He really ___ try his best in yesterday's final exam.
d1.explanation: 強調過去的動作，用 did + 原形動詞 try。

d1.stem: It ___ my parents who supported my decision to join the band.
d1.explanation: 分裂句以 It was 開頭，虛主詞用單數 was，即使後面接複數 parents 也不用 were。動作為過去。

d2.stem: It was because she practiced every day ___ she won the piano competition.
d2.explanation: 分裂句強調 because 引導的原因子句，其後連接詞仍用 that，不可再用 because 或 why。

d2.stem: ___ was the heavy traffic that made us late for the ceremony.
d2.explanation: 分裂句以虛主詞 It 開頭，「It was...that...」，不可用 There/This/That。

d2.stem: It was not until he lost his health ___ he realized the value of exercise.
d2.explanation: 「It was not until...that...」為固定分裂句結構，直到失去健康才明白，連接詞用 that。

d2.stem: Not until midnight ___ that all the guests had already left.
d2.explanation: Not until 置於句首，主要子句須倒裝，助動詞提前：did he notice。

d2.stem: It ___ the students' hard work and teamwork that impressed the judges.
d2.explanation: 分裂句虛主詞用單數，且動作為過去，故用 was；不因後面並列名詞而改為 were。

d2.stem: It is honesty ___ matters most in a true friendship.
d2.explanation: 分裂句用 that；若要用 what 需改寫成「What matters most is honesty」。honesty 非人，不用 who。

d2.stem: You've made the ___ mistake that cost us the game last time!
d2.explanation: the very + 名詞 表示「正是同一個」，強調就是上次那個失誤。

d2.stem: How ___ earth did you finish the whole project overnight?
d2.explanation: 加強疑問詞用 on earth（或 in the world）。此處介系詞為 on。

d2.stem: It was the old photograph ___ brought back all her childhood memories.
d2.explanation: 強調的是事物（photograph），分裂句連接詞用 that；非人不用 who，此處也不用 what/when。

d2.stem: It was to his grandmother ___ he wrote the very first letter of his life.
d2.explanation: 強調的是介系詞片語 to his grandmother，分裂句連接詞用 that，不可用 who/whom/which。

d2.stem: She seldom cooks, but last night she ___ prepare a wonderful dinner for us.
d2.explanation: 用助動詞強調對比語氣「確實」，last night 為過去，用 did + 原形 prepare。

d2.stem: It was only after the exam ___ I remembered the correct answer.
d2.explanation: 「It was only after...that...」為分裂句結構，強調時間點，連接詞用 that。

d3.stem: It was the confidence, not the talent, ___ finally carried her to victory.
d3.explanation: 儘管中間插入 not the talent，分裂句強調的仍是主詞 the confidence，連接詞用 that。confidence 非人不用 who。

d3.stem: I may not speak French fluently, but I ___ understand most of what natives say.
d3.explanation: 用助動詞強調「確實聽得懂」的對比語氣，主詞 I、現在式，用 do + 原形 understand。

d3.stem: It will not be until she returns from abroad ___ we can hold the family reunion.
d3.explanation: 分裂句「It will not be until...that...」是未來式版本，直到她回國才能聚會，連接詞仍用 that。

d3.stem: Not until the storm had completely passed ___ to leave the shelter.
d3.explanation: Not until 引導的片語置於句首，主要子句須倒裝，助動詞提前為 dared we（leave）。until 子句本身維持正常語序。

d3.stem: Standing before us was the very ___ we had traveled thousands of miles to see.
d3.explanation: the very + 名詞 直接接名詞即可，後面接省略關係詞的子句 we had traveled...；其餘選項多了多餘或錯誤的關係詞。

d3.stem: It was during the summer of 2020, ___ the whole world stayed home, that we learned to value freedom.
d3.explanation: 外層分裂句已用 that；此空格是修飾 summer 的關係子句，指時間，用 when。要能分辨『分裂句的 that』與『關係子句的 when』。

d3.stem: Rarely does he lose his temper, but when the referee cheated, he ___ raise his voice in protest.
d3.explanation: 用助動詞強調「確實發聲抗議」的對比。事件發生在過去（cheated），用 did + 原形 raise。

d3.stem: I really wonder how ___ earth she managed to stay so calm during the whole crisis.
d3.explanation: 加強疑問詞的 on earth 即使放在間接問句中也照用，介系詞為 on。

d3.stem: ___ was not until I read the letter twice that its true meaning became clear to me.
d3.explanation: 這是「It was not until...that...」的分裂句框架，句首必須用虛主詞 It。

d3.stem: It was the volunteers ___ the villagers thanked most for rebuilding their homes.
d3.explanation: 強調受詞 the volunteers（村民感謝的對象），分裂句用 that；非物不用 which，語意也不合 what/whose。

d3.stem: If you ___ happen to run into Mr. Lin, please do tell him the good news right away.
d3.explanation: do happen 是強調語氣「萬一真的碰到」，主詞 you、現在式，用 do + 原形；後半 do tell 亦為祈使句的強調。

d3.stem: Look closely — it is not the price ___ makes this gift special, but the thought behind it.
d3.explanation: 「it is not X that... but Y」的對比分裂句，連接詞用 that；the price 非人不用 who，也不用 what。

topic.label: 對等連接與平行結構
topic.scope: both...and/either...or/neither...nor/not only...but also 的對稱、就近一致(動詞隨較近主詞)、平行結構(V-ing 對 V-ing、to V 對 to V)、rather than/as well as/not...but

note.title: 對等連接與平行結構
note.summary: 對等連接詞（correlative conjunctions）如 both...and、either...or、neither...nor、not only...but also，必須「成對出現」並連接「文法地位相同」的成分。這牽涉兩大重點：一是主詞動詞一致（both...and 一律用複數，其餘採「就近一致」，動詞隨較近的主詞變化），二是平行結構（V-ing 對 V-ing、to V 對 to V、名詞對名詞、形容詞對形容詞）。此外 rather than、as well as、not...but 也常在學測考驗你對形式對稱與動詞判斷的敏感度。掌握這一單元，不只文法選擇題得分，寫作句構也會更精準漂亮。

sections.h: 對等連接詞要「成對且對稱」
sections.body: correlative conjunctions 一定成對出現：both A and B、either A or B、neither A nor B、not only A but also B。最常見的陷阱是配錯搭檔（both...or、neither...or、not only...and 都是錯的），以及 A、B 兩邊的詞性／結構不對稱。記住：連接詞前後放的東西必須「同一種類」，是名詞就都名詞，是動詞片語就都動詞片語。
examples: She is both intelligent and hardworking. 她既聰明又勤奮。
You can either stay here or come with us. 你可以留在這裡，或者跟我們走。
Not only the design but also the price attracts customers. 不只設計，連價格也吸引顧客。

sections.h: 主詞動詞一致：both...and 用複數，其餘就近一致
sections.body: both A and B 視為兩者，動詞一律用複數。但 either...or、neither...nor、not only...but also 連接兩個主詞時，採「就近原則」（proximity rule）——動詞跟「離它最近的那個主詞」一致。所以較近主詞是單數就用單數動詞，是複數就用複數動詞。這是學測最愛考的判斷點。
examples: Both the teacher and the students are ready. 老師和學生都準備好了。
Neither the players nor the coach was satisfied. 球員和教練都不滿意（coach 較近，用單數）。
Not only the coach but also the players were angry. 不只教練，連球員也很生氣（players 較近，用複數）。

sections.h: 平行結構：詞性與形式要一致
sections.body: 用 and、or、but 或對等連接詞把幾個成分並列時，這些成分的「形式」要一致：動名詞配動名詞（V-ing / V-ing）、不定詞配不定詞（to V / to V，第二個 to 常可省略但動詞維持原形）、名詞配名詞、形容詞配形容詞。列舉三項以上時（A, B, and C）三者也要平行。這是英文寫作是否成熟的重要指標。
examples: I enjoy swimming, hiking, and cycling. 我喜歡游泳、健行和騎腳踏車。
My goal is to study hard and (to) get into a good college. 我的目標是用功讀書並考上好大學。
The speech was clear, short, and powerful. 那場演說清楚、簡短又有力。

sections.h: rather than / as well as / not...but 的接法
sections.body: rather than（而不是）與 not...but（不是...而是）連接的兩邊要對稱，常配名詞、動名詞或原形動詞。as well as（以及、還有）連接兩個主詞時，動詞跟「前面的主詞」一致（因為 as well as B 是附加訊息，真正的主詞是 A）。這點和 both...and 剛好相反，要特別分清楚。
examples: He chose to walk rather than take a taxi. 他選擇走路而不是搭計程車。
What matters is not the result but the effort. 重要的不是結果，而是努力。
The teacher, as well as her students, is going on the trip. 老師和她的學生都要去旅行（主詞是 teacher，用單數）。

mistakes: 誤：Both Tom and Mary is my friend. → 正：Both Tom and Mary are my friends.（both...and 必用複數動詞）
誤：Neither the students nor the teacher were happy. → 正：Neither the students nor the teacher was happy.（就近一致，較近的 teacher 是單數）
誤：I like reading books and to watch movies. → 正：I like reading books and watching movies.（平行結構，V-ing 要對 V-ing）
誤：Not only he passed the exam but also won a prize. → 正：Not only did he pass the exam, but he also won a prize.（Not only 置句首要倒裝）
誤：She is smart as well as her sister are pretty. → 正：She, as well as her sister, is smart.（as well as 的動詞跟前面主詞一致）
note.tip: 口訣：「both 一定複數，其餘看最近；連接詞兩邊，形式要對稱。」

d1.stem: Both Tom and Jerry ___ good at playing basketball.
d1.explanation: both A and B 表示「兩者都」，主詞視為複數，動詞須用 are。

d1.stem: Neither the teacher nor the students ___ late for the morning exam.
d1.explanation: neither...nor 採就近一致，較近的主詞是複數 the students，動詞用 were。

d1.stem: Either you or he ___ responsible for cleaning the classroom today.
d1.explanation: either...or 就近一致，較近的主詞是單數 he，動詞用 is。

d1.stem: Not only the players but also the coach ___ excited about the championship.
d1.explanation: not only...but also 就近一致，較近的主詞是單數 the coach，動詞用 is。

d1.stem: She likes swimming in the morning and ___ in the evening.
d1.explanation: 平行結構：and 連接兩個動名詞，前面是 swimming，後面須用 jogging。

d1.stem: My plan is to review my notes and ___ good grades in the final exam.
d1.explanation: 平行結構：連接兩個不定詞，前面是 to review，後面應對應 to get。

d1.stem: The new student is smart, friendly, and ___.
d1.explanation: 平行結構：列舉的是形容詞 smart、friendly，第三項也須用形容詞 honest。

d1.stem: Both my parents and my brother ___ coming to my graduation ceremony.
d1.explanation: both A and B 主詞為複數，動詞用 are。

d1.stem: You can either take the school bus ___ ride a bike to school.
d1.explanation: either 必須搭配 or，構成 either...or「二者擇一」。

d1.stem: ___ Anna nor Mary passed the difficult math test.
d1.explanation: 與 nor 成對出現的是 Neither，構成 neither...nor「兩者都不」。

d1.stem: The homemade soup tastes not only delicious ___ also healthy.
d1.explanation: not only 固定搭配 but also，構成 not only...but also「不但...而且」。

d1.stem: We prefer studying together rather than ___ video games all afternoon.
d1.explanation: rather than 兩邊要對稱，前面是動名詞 studying，後面須用 playing。

d2.stem: Not only the students but also the homeroom teacher ___ surprised by the sudden fire drill during class.
d2.explanation: not only...but also 就近一致，較近的主詞是單數 the homeroom teacher，用 was。誘答 were 是誤把主詞當複數。

d2.stem: Either the manager or the assistants ___ going to attend the international conference next week.
d2.explanation: either...or 就近一致，較近主詞為複數 the assistants，動詞用 are。

d2.stem: Neither the taste of the food nor the prices ___ satisfactory at that newly opened restaurant.
d2.explanation: neither...nor 就近一致，較近的主詞是複數 the prices，用 are。前面的 the taste 是干擾。

d2.stem: The teacher advised us to read more books, ___ less TV, and exercise regularly.
d2.explanation: 平行結構：三個動作共用一個 to（to read... watch... exercise），第二項須用原形 watch。

d2.stem: He is interested not only in playing the guitar but also in ___ new songs by himself.
d2.explanation: 平行結構：not only in + V-ing / but also in + V-ing，前面是 playing，後面對應 making。

d2.stem: Both learning a foreign language and ___ a musical instrument require years of patience.
d2.explanation: both A and B 要對稱，前面是動名詞 learning，後面須用 playing；主詞複數故動詞 require 也正確。

d2.stem: On such a cold night, she would rather stay at home ___ go to the crowded party.
d2.explanation: would rather A than B 固定句型，選 than。誘答 then 是拼字相近的常見錯誤。

d2.stem: ___ the captain nor the sailors were fully prepared for the approaching storm.
d2.explanation: 與 nor 成對的是 Neither；且動詞 were 隨較近的複數 the sailors，符合就近一致。

d2.stem: Reading widely helps students not only broaden their horizons ___ sharpen their critical thinking.
d2.explanation: not only 固定搭配 but also，兩個原形動詞 broaden、sharpen 也平行對稱。

d2.stem: Our team's success comes from working hard together rather than ___ purely on good luck.
d2.explanation: rather than 兩邊要對稱，前面是動名詞 working，後面須用 relying。

d2.stem: Neither of the two proposals ___ perfect, but we still have to pick one before Friday.
d2.explanation: neither of + 複數名詞，視為單數，動詞用 is。誘答 are 是被 proposals 誤導。

d2.stem: During the field trip, the volunteers spent the day cleaning the beach, planting trees, and ___ recycling to visitors.
d2.explanation: 平行結構：三個動名詞 cleaning、planting、promoting 並列，須用 promoting。

d3.stem: Not only ___ to finish the tough assignment on time, but he also helped two classmates with theirs.
d3.explanation: Not only 置於句首，主句須「倒裝」：把助動詞提前，成 did he manage。這是學測進階倒裝考點。

d3.stem: The committee praised the report for being clear, concise, and ___.
d3.explanation: 平行結構：與形容詞 clear、concise 並列，第三項也須為形容詞性質的 well organized。

d3.stem: A capable leader should know not only how to make wise decisions but also how ___ responsibility for the outcomes.
d3.explanation: 平行結構：not only how to make... but also how to take...，兩個 how to + 原形須對稱，選 to take。

d3.stem: Either the students misunderstood the directions, or the teacher ___ them clearly enough in the first place.
d3.explanation: 就近一致，較近主詞是單數 the teacher；且與前半 misunderstood 同為過去式，用 didn't explain。

d3.stem: What truly impresses everyone about her is not her family's wealth but ___ toward strangers.
d3.explanation: not A but B 要對稱，A 是名詞片語 her family's wealth，B 也須為名詞 her kindness。

d3.stem: The success of the whole team lies not in the talent of one star player but in the ___ of every member.
d3.explanation: not in A but in B，A 是名詞 talent，B 也須用名詞 cooperation 以維持平行。

d3.stem: Both the sleek design of the app and its user-friendly interface ___ highly praised by professional reviewers.
d3.explanation: both A and B 主詞為複數，被動語態動詞用 were praised。誘答 was/is 忽略了複數。

d3.stem: So talented was she that not only ___ the debate contest but also convinced every judge on the panel.
d3.explanation: not only 引導的部分置於句中作強調，仍須倒裝為 did she win，與後半 convinced 平行。

d3.stem: Neither his outstanding talent nor his powerful connections ___ able to save the failing family business.
d3.explanation: neither...nor 就近一致，較近主詞是複數 his powerful connections，用 were。前面 talent 是干擾。

d3.stem: During the earthquake drill, we were told to stay calm, follow the exit signs, and ___ the elevators at all costs.
d3.explanation: 平行結構：to stay... follow... avoid 三個原形動詞共用一個 to，第三項用原形 avoid。

d3.stem: It was her sheer determination, rather than her natural ability, ___ finally made her the national champion.
d3.explanation: 這是強調句 It was...that...，被強調的先行詞是單數 determination，用 that made（動詞單數過去式）。

d3.stem: The teacher kept reminding us that reading extensively is just as important ___ memorizing vocabulary lists.
d3.explanation: as...as 原級比較句型，as important 之後須用 as，表「和...一樣重要」。

topic.label: 主詞動詞一致
topic.scope: each/every/either/neither + 單數動詞、a number of(複數) vs the number of(單數)、集合名詞、部分詞 most/some/half/the rest of + N 動詞隨 N、there be 就近一致、學科/國名/金額當單數

note.title: 主詞動詞一致
note.summary: 主詞動詞一致就是動詞的單複數要跟著「真正的主詞」走。學測難點在於主詞常被修飾語、of 片語、片語連接詞或倒裝結構「偽裝」，讓你誤判核心主詞。本單元整理六大常考陷阱：each/every/either/neither 一律單數、a number of 與 the number of 的差別、集合名詞、部分詞 most/some/half/the rest of + N 的動詞跟著 N 走、there be 的就近一致，以及學科/國名/金額/時間距離當單數。抓住「先找出真正決定動詞的核心名詞」這個原則，就能破解大部分陷阱題。

sections.h: each / every / either / neither + 單數動詞
sections.body: 這四個字都把主詞視為「單一個體」，即使後面接 of + 複數名詞，動詞仍用單數。every 所修飾的名詞（即使是 A and B 並列）也視為單數。這是最常被考、也最常被忽略的規則。
examples: Each of the players has a number on the shirt. 每位球員的球衣上都有號碼。
Every student and teacher was invited to the party. 每位學生和老師都受邀參加派對。
Neither of the plans works well in practice. 兩個計畫實際上都行不通。

sections.h: a number of(複數) vs the number of(單數)
sections.body: a number of 等於 many，重點在後面的複數名詞，所以動詞用複數；the number of 指「數目」本身，是一個抽象單一概念，所以動詞用單數。看到 the number of 就想成一個數字，看到 a number of 就想成一群人。
examples: A number of volunteers are helping clean the beach. 許多志工正在幫忙清理海灘。
The number of volunteers is increasing every year. 志工的數目逐年增加。
A number of endangered species have disappeared. 許多瀕危物種已經消失。

sections.h: 集合名詞(team / family / committee / audience)
sections.body: team, family, class, committee, audience 等集合名詞當「整體」看時用單數動詞；強調「個別成員各自行動」時用複數動詞（英式英語較常見）。台灣考試多以「整體單數」為主要答案，除非句意明顯強調成員個別。
examples: Our team is the strongest in the whole league. 我們隊是全聯盟最強的。（視為整體）
The audience were arguing among themselves. 觀眾彼此爭論不休。（強調個別成員）
The committee has made its final decision. 委員會已做出最終決定。

sections.h: 部分詞 most / some / half / the rest of + N
sections.body: most, some, half, part, the rest, a lot of, 分數與百分比 + of + N，動詞完全跟著 of 後面的名詞走：名詞是可數複數就用複數動詞，是不可數就用單數動詞。關鍵不在 most/half 本身，而在 of 後面那個名詞。
examples: Most of the students are from Taiwan. 大部分學生來自台灣。（可數複數）
Most of the homework is already finished. 大部分作業已經完成。（不可數）
Half of the cake was eaten by the kids. 一半的蛋糕被孩子吃掉了。

sections.h: there be 就近一致
sections.body: there be 句型的動詞要與「最靠近的那個主詞」一致。列舉多個名詞時，看緊接在動詞後的第一個名詞：單數就用 is/was，複數就用 are/were。調換名詞順序，動詞也要跟著換。
examples: There is a notebook and three pens in the bag. 袋子裡有一本筆記本和三支筆。（靠近 a notebook）
There are three pens and a notebook in the bag. 袋子裡有三支筆和一本筆記本。（靠近 three pens）
There were many problems and one big challenge. 有許多問題和一個大挑戰。

sections.h: 學科 / 國名 / 金額 / 時間距離當單數
sections.body: 字尾有 -s 但表示「單一概念」的名詞用單數動詞：學科(physics, mathematics, economics, statistics 作學科時)、國名(the United States, the Philippines)、以及金額、時間、距離、重量當「一個整體量」時。注意 statistics 作「數據」解時則是複數。
examples: Economics is a very popular major. 經濟學是很熱門的主修。
Five thousand dollars is a lot of money for a student. 五千元對學生來說是一大筆錢。
The United States has fifty states. 美國有五十個州。

mistakes: 誤：Each of the students have a book. → 正：Each of the students has a book.（each of 後動詞用單數）
誤：A number of people is waiting outside. → 正：A number of people are waiting outside.（a number of = many，用複數）
誤：The number of tourists are increasing. → 正：The number of tourists is increasing.（the number of 指數目，用單數）
誤：Physics are my favorite subject. → 正：Physics is my favorite subject.（學科名雖有 -s 仍是單數）
誤：There are a pen and two books on the desk. → 正：There is a pen and two books on the desk.（there be 就近一致，靠近 a pen）
note.tip: 口訣：the number of 看『數目』用單數，a number of 等於 many 用複數；most/half/rest of 後面看『of 的名詞』決定動詞。

d1.stem: Each of the students ___ a laptop for the online class.
d1.explanation: each of + 複數名詞時，主詞視為單一個體，動詞用單數，故選 has。

d1.stem: Every boy and girl in the club ___ excited about the field trip.
d1.explanation: every 所修飾的 boy and girl 視為單數，且句意為現在，故用 is。

d1.stem: Neither of the answers ___ correct, so we lost the point.
d1.explanation: neither of + 複數名詞，正式用法動詞用單數，故選 is。

d1.stem: A number of students ___ absent because of the flu today.
d1.explanation: a number of 等於 many，動詞依複數名詞 students，用 are。

d1.stem: The number of foreign tourists ___ increasing steadily.
d1.explanation: the number of 指「數目」本身，為單數概念，故用 is。

d1.stem: Physics ___ my favorite subject at school.
d1.explanation: physics 是學科名，雖字尾有 -s 仍視為單數，故用 is。

d1.stem: Two hundred dollars ___ enough to buy the concert ticket.
d1.explanation: 金額當一個整體量看待，用單數動詞 is。

d1.stem: Either Tom or his brothers ___ going to help us move.
d1.explanation: either...or 採就近一致，靠近動詞的 brothers 為複數，故用 are。

d1.stem: The United States ___ one of the largest countries in the world.
d1.explanation: 國名雖字尾有 -s，仍視為單一國家，故用 is。

d1.stem: Half of the cake ___ already gone.
d1.explanation: half of + 不可數名詞 cake，動詞跟著 cake，用單數 is。

d1.stem: There ___ a dictionary and two magazines on the shelf.
d1.explanation: there be 就近一致，最靠近的 a dictionary 為單數，故用 is。

d1.stem: Everyone in our class ___ ready for the final exam.
d1.explanation: everyone 是單數不定代名詞，介系詞片語不影響動詞，故用 is。

d2.stem: Most of the furniture in the new office ___ imported from Italy.
d2.explanation: furniture 是不可數名詞，most of + 不可數，動詞跟著它用單數 is。

d2.stem: Half of the apples in the basket ___ already rotten.
d2.explanation: half of + 複數名詞 apples，動詞跟著 apples 用複數 are。

d2.stem: Neither of my parents ___ willing to change their decision.
d2.explanation: neither of + 複數，正式用法動詞用單數，故用 is。

d2.stem: A number of tourists from Korea ___ visiting the night market tonight.
d2.explanation: a number of = many，動詞依複數名詞 tourists，用 are。

d2.stem: The number of people who exercise regularly ___ growing.
d2.explanation: 主詞核心是 the number of，指數目本身，who 子句不影響，故用單數 is。

d2.stem: Mathematics, along with English, ___ required for graduation.
d2.explanation: 主詞 mathematics 為單數學科名；along with 片語不改變主詞，故用 is。

d2.stem: Ten kilometers ___ too long a distance for a beginner to run.
d2.explanation: 距離當一個整體量看待，用單數動詞 is。

d2.stem: Either the manager or the assistants ___ responsible for the error.
d2.explanation: either...or 就近一致，靠近的 assistants 為複數，故用 are。

d2.stem: The rest of the milk ___ gone bad, so throw it away.
d2.explanation: the rest of + 不可數名詞 milk，動詞跟著 milk 用單數 has。

d2.stem: There ___ several good reasons for changing the plan.
d2.explanation: there be 就近一致，靠近的 several reasons 為複數，故用 are。

d2.stem: Every one of the volunteers ___ given a free T-shirt after the event.
d2.explanation: every one of + 複數，主詞視為單數；被動語態用 was given，故用 was。

d2.stem: The Philippines ___ made up of more than seven thousand islands.
d2.explanation: 國名雖字尾有 -s，仍視為單一國家，故用 is。

d3.stem: Not only the players but also the coach ___ disappointed with the final result.
d3.explanation: not only...but also 採就近一致，靠近動詞的 the coach 為單數，故用 was。

d3.stem: On the classroom wall ___ several old photographs of past graduates.
d3.explanation: 地方副詞置句首形成倒裝，真正主詞是複數 photographs，故用 are。

d3.stem: The number of students who fail the entrance exam ___ decreasing, which pleases the teachers.
d3.explanation: 主詞核心是 the number of，指數目為單數；who 子句與插入語不影響，故用 is。

d3.stem: Bread and butter ___ what most British children have for breakfast.
d3.explanation: bread and butter 視為一道「單一餐點」，為單數概念，故用 is。

d3.stem: Although they look objective, statistics ___ sometimes used to mislead the public.
d3.explanation: statistics 作「數據」解時為複數（前面用 they 呼應），故用 are；作「統計學」時才單數。

d3.stem: Neither the students nor the teacher ___ aware that the microphone was still on.
d3.explanation: neither...nor 就近一致，靠近動詞的 the teacher 為單數，故用 was。

d3.stem: Here ___ the exam results that you have been anxiously waiting for.
d3.explanation: here 引導倒裝句，真正主詞 results 為複數，故用 are。

d3.stem: More than one passenger ___ complained about the two-hour delay.
d3.explanation: more than one + 單數名詞，雖語意上不只一人，動詞仍用單數，故用 has。

d3.stem: In modern society, the rich ___ not always happier than the poor.
d3.explanation: the + 形容詞（the rich）指「一群人」，視為複數，故用 are。

d3.stem: Twenty years ___ a long time to wait for a sincere apology.
d3.explanation: 一段時間視為單一整體量，搭配 be 動詞時用單數，故用 is。

d3.stem: A number of endangered species ___ disappeared due to rapid climate change.
d3.explanation: a number of = many，動詞用複數；此處完成式，故用 have disappeared。species 單複數同形，此處為複數。

d3.stem: Each of the countries that signed the treaty ___ its own reasons for doing so.
d3.explanation: each of + 複數，主詞視為單數；that 子句不影響，又搭配單數 its，故用 has。

topic.label: 介系詞與慣用搭配
topic.scope: 時間介系詞(by/until/for/since/during/within)、形容詞+介系詞(be good at/afraid of/famous for)、動詞+介系詞(depend on/consist of)、片語介系詞(due to/instead of/in spite of/regardless of/thanks to)

note.title: 介系詞與慣用搭配
note.summary: 介系詞看似小字，卻是學測選擇題與克漏字最愛設陷阱的地方。它不像單字有明確中文對應，而是靠「固定搭配(collocation)」死背活用。本單元分四大區塊：時間介系詞(by/until/for/since/during/within)、形容詞+介系詞、動詞+介系詞、以及片語介系詞(due to/instead of/in spite of/regardless of/thanks to)。掌握的關鍵不是翻譯，而是記住『哪個字後面固定接哪個介系詞』，以及『後面該接名詞還是子句』。

sections.h: 一、時間介系詞:by / until / for / since / during / within
sections.body: by 表示『在某時間點之前(期限、最晚)』,動作只發生一次;until(till)表示『持續到某時間點才停止』,強調一段狀態的延續。for 後接『一段時間長度』(for three years);since 後接『時間起點』(since 2010),且句子常搭配完成式。during 後接『名詞』表某段期間內發生(During the exam);within 表示『在…之內、不超過某範圍』。
examples: You must hand in the form by Friday. 你必須在星期五前交表格。
The library stays open until 10 p.m. 圖書館開到晚上十點。
I have studied French for five years since 2019. 我從2019年起已學法文五年。

sections.h: 二、形容詞 + 介系詞的固定搭配
sections.body: 許多形容詞後面接的介系詞是固定的,必須整組記憶。常見:be good at(擅長)、be afraid/scared of(害怕)、be famous/known for(以…聞名)、be interested in(對…有興趣)、be proud of(以…自豪)、be worried about(擔心)、be married to(與…結婚)、be responsible for(負責)。注意 be good at 後接動作時用動名詞(V-ing)。
examples: She is good at solving math problems. 她擅長解數學題。
Our town is famous for its hot springs. 我們鎮上以溫泉聞名。
He is afraid of speaking in public. 他害怕在眾人面前說話。

sections.h: 三、動詞 + 介系詞的固定搭配
sections.body: 動詞片語也有固定介系詞:depend/rely on(依賴)、consist of(由…組成)、insist on(堅持)、result in(導致結果)、result from(起因於)、accuse sb of(指控)、reply to(回覆)、apologize to sb for sth(為…向某人道歉)。特別注意 result in 與 result from 方向相反:in 接『結果』,from 接『原因』。
examples: Success depends on hard work, not luck. 成功取決於努力,而非運氣。
Water consists of hydrogen and oxygen. 水由氫和氧組成。
Careless driving may result in serious accidents. 粗心駕駛可能導致嚴重事故。

sections.h: 四、片語介系詞:due to / instead of / in spite of / regardless of / thanks to
sections.body: 這些是由多字組成、後面『接名詞或動名詞』的介系詞片語。due to / because of / owing to =因為(表原因);in spite of / despite =儘管(讓步,despite 後不加 of);instead of =而不是、取代;regardless of =不管、無論;thanks to =多虧、幸虧(常含正面語氣)。關鍵陷阱:despite 直接接名詞,although / though 才接完整子句。
examples: The match was canceled due to the storm. 因為暴風雨,比賽取消了。
In spite of the rain, they kept playing. 儘管下雨,他們仍繼續打球。
Thanks to your help, I passed the exam. 多虧你的幫忙,我通過了考試。

sections.h: 五、易混點深入:by vs until 與 for vs since
sections.body: by 用於『一次性動作的期限』(必須在此之前完成);until 用於『持續性動作或狀態的終點』(一直…到)。判斷訣竅:句中動詞若是 finish, submit, return, arrive 等一次完成的動作,用 by;若是 wait, stay, sleep, work 等可延續的動作,用 until。for 後面是『多久』(for a week),since 後面是『從何時起』(since Monday),且 since 幾乎一定配現在完成式。
examples: Please return the book by next Monday. 請最晚在下週一前還書。
I waited until the shop closed. 我一直等到商店打烊。
We have known each other since junior high. 我們從國中起就認識彼此。

mistakes: 誤:I have lived here for 2010. → 正:I have lived here since 2010.(2010是時間起點,用 since,不用 for)
誤:Despite of the traffic, we arrived on time. → 正:Despite the traffic, we arrived on time.(despite 後面直接接名詞,不加 of;要加 of 請用 in spite of)
誤:She is good at play the piano. → 正:She is good at playing the piano.(介系詞 at 後面接動作要用動名詞 V-ing)
誤:You must finish it until Friday. → 正:You must finish it by Friday.(finish 是一次完成的動作,期限用 by,不用 until)
誤:The accident resulted from many injuries. → 正:The accident resulted in many injuries.(結果用 result in;起因才用 result from)
note.tip: 背介系詞別用翻譯,要用『整組聲音』記:good at、afraid of、depend on、famous for 一次唸出來,考試時嘴巴會自動接對那個介系詞。

d1.stem: My little sister is good ___ drawing cartoon characters.
d1.explanation: be good at 為固定搭配，表示『擅長』，後接動作時用動名詞 drawing。

d1.stem: You have to finish your science report ___ Friday, or you will lose points.
d1.explanation: finish 是一次完成的動作，期限『在…之前』用 by；until 用於持續動作的終點。

d1.stem: Many students are afraid ___ making mistakes when they speak English.
d1.explanation: be afraid of 為固定搭配，表示『害怕』，後接動名詞 making。

d1.stem: Tainan is famous ___ its traditional snacks and old temples.
d1.explanation: be famous for 表示『以…聞名』，for 後接聞名的原因。

d1.stem: Whether we win the contest depends ___ how hard we practice.
d1.explanation: depend on 為固定搭配，表示『取決於、依賴』。

d1.stem: A water molecule consists ___ two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.
d1.explanation: consist of 表示『由…組成』，為固定搭配，無被動用法。

d1.stem: ___ the summer vacation, our family went hiking in the mountains.
d1.explanation: during 後接名詞(the summer vacation)表某段期間；while 後面須接完整子句。

d1.stem: My grandparents have lived in this old house ___ 1985.
d1.explanation: 1985 是時間起點，用 since，並搭配現在完成式 have lived。

d1.stem: He has been learning the violin ___ almost eight years.
d1.explanation: almost eight years 是一段時間長度，用 for；since 後面要接起始時間點。

d1.stem: ___ the heavy rain, the outdoor concert was canceled.
d1.explanation: Due to 後接名詞片語表原因；Because 與 Since 表原因時後面須接完整子句。

d1.stem: Instead ___ watching TV all night, why not read a good book?
d1.explanation: instead of 為固定片語，表示『而不是』，後接名詞或動名詞。

d1.stem: I will reply ___ your email as soon as I get home.
d1.explanation: reply to somebody/something 為固定搭配，表示『回覆』。

d2.stem: Remember to submit your application ___ Friday; no late forms will be accepted after that.
d2.explanation: submit 是一次完成的動作，強調『最晚在…之前』的期限，用 by；until 用於持續動作的終點。

d2.stem: I stood at the bus stop and waited ___ the last bus finally arrived.
d2.explanation: wait 是可持續的動作，『一直等到…才』用 until，表狀態延續到某時間點。

d2.stem: Don't worry—the new medicine usually starts working ___ thirty minutes.
d2.explanation: within 表示『在…之內、不超過』某段時間範圍，強調時間長度內完成。

d2.stem: Ever since he was a child, David has been deeply interested ___ astronomy.
d2.explanation: be interested in 為固定搭配，表示『對…有興趣』。

d2.stem: Cutting down too many trees will eventually result ___ serious flooding.
d2.explanation: result in 表示『導致(結果)』，in 後接結果；result from 才是『起因於』。

d2.stem: Her outstanding performance resulted ___ years of disciplined training.
d2.explanation: result from 表示『起因於、源自』，from 後接原因；此句強調成果的來源。

d2.stem: ___ his young age, the boy handled the emergency with surprising calm.
d2.explanation: Despite 後接名詞片語(his young age)表讓步；Although 須接完整子句，故不合。

d2.stem: ___ of taking the crowded subway, we decided to ride our bikes to school.
d2.explanation: instead of 表示『而不是、取代』；aside 要用 aside from，rather 要用 rather than。

d2.stem: The morning flight was delayed for hours ___ the thick fog over the airport.
d2.explanation: because of 後接名詞片語表原因；because 後面須接完整子句。

d2.stem: This scholarship is open to everyone, ___ of nationality or family income.
d2.explanation: regardless of 表示『不管、無論』，後接名詞；此處指無論國籍或收入皆可申請。

d2.stem: My aunt is a nurse, and she is married ___ a firefighter.
d2.explanation: be married to somebody 為固定搭配，表示『與…結婚』，不用 with。

d3.stem: The research grant must be approved ___ the end of this month, or the whole project will be shut down.
d3.explanation: approve 是一次完成的動作，句意強調『最晚在月底前』這個期限，用 by；until 表持續、within 表範圍內，皆不合語意。

d3.stem: ___ being the youngest and least experienced player, she outshone everyone on the court.
d3.explanation: Despite 後可接動名詞片語(being…)表讓步；Although 須接完整子句，Even 須配 though，Because of 語意相反。

d3.stem: The witness's story simply does not accord ___ the evidence the police collected.
d3.explanation: accord with 為固定搭配，表示『與…一致、相符』。

d3.stem: ___ her quick reaction and cool head, a serious accident was successfully avoided.
d3.explanation: thanks to 表示『多虧、幸虧』，後接名詞且常帶正面語氣；thanks for 用於直接向人道謝(Thanks for helping)。

d3.stem: Not until the final scores were posted ___ how much his hard work had paid off.
d3.explanation: 以否定詞 Not until 置於句首時，主要子句須倒裝為『助動詞+主詞+原形動詞』，故用 did he realize。

d3.stem: The new attendance rule applies to every member of the club, ___ of their age or rank.
d3.explanation: regardless of 表示『不論、無論』，後接名詞；其餘選項語意或搭配皆不符。

d3.stem: Although she was clearly exhausted, she still insisted ___ walking the guests all the way to the door.
d3.explanation: insist on 為固定搭配，表示『堅持(做)』，後接動名詞 walking。

d3.stem: The whole neighborhood has changed almost beyond recognition ___ I last came back three years ago.
d3.explanation: since 引導表『自從…以來』的時間點子句，並搭配主句的現在完成式 has changed。

d3.stem: The team's championship was due not to a single lucky moment ___ to months of relentless practice.
d3.explanation: 此為 not…but… 對比句型：『不是因為X，而是因為Y』，故填 but，並保持 to 平行。

d3.stem: Two senior students were accused ___ leaking the exam questions, but both firmly denied it.
d3.explanation: accuse somebody of something 為固定搭配，表示『指控某人做某事』，of 後接動名詞。

d3.stem: If you don't hear back from the admissions office ___ two weeks, feel free to call and check.
d3.explanation: within two weeks 表示『在兩週之內』的時間範圍；until 表持續終點、by 表單一期限，皆不符『之內』語意。

d3.stem: ___ of endlessly complaining about the broken system, why don't you propose a real solution?
d3.explanation: instead of 後接動名詞，表示『與其…不如、而不是』；Rather 須配 than，Aside 須配 from，皆不合。

topic.label: 構詞與詞類變化
topic.scope: 名詞化字尾(-tion/-ment/-ness/-ity)、形容詞字尾(-ful/-ous/-al)與副詞-ly、前綴(un-/dis-/re-/in-)、依句法判斷空格詞類(填名詞/形容詞/副詞)、複合字與轉品

note.title: 構詞與詞類變化
note.summary: 英文單字常靠「字根＋字首＋字尾」組合而成。字尾決定詞類（名詞、形容詞或副詞），字首多半改變語意（如否定、重複、相反）。學測文法題最愛考「依句子結構判斷空格該填哪一種詞類」：看空格前後是冠詞、be動詞、還是被修飾的動詞，就能推出答案。本單元帶你熟悉常見名詞化字尾（-tion/-ment/-ness/-ity）、形容詞字尾（-ful/-ous/-al）、副詞 -ly、四大前綴（un-/dis-/re-/in-），以及複合字與「轉品」現象，讓你不必背整句也能靠構詞規則選對答案。

sections.h: 名詞化字尾：-tion / -ment / -ness / -ity
sections.body: 這四個字尾會把動詞或形容詞變成名詞。-tion／-sion 多接在動詞後（decide→decision）；-ment 也接動詞（develop→development）；-ness 接形容詞（kind→kindness）；-ity 接形容詞（active→activity、pure→purity）。判斷技巧：空格前若有 a／an／the／this／形容詞，或空格當主詞、受詞，通常就要填名詞。
examples: The government made an important decision about education. 政府對教育做出了重要的決定。
Her kindness to strangers moved everyone in the classroom. 她對陌生人的善良感動了教室裡的每個人。
The diversity of cultures makes this city attractive. 文化的多樣性讓這座城市充滿吸引力。

sections.h: 形容詞字尾 -ful / -ous / -al 與副詞字尾 -ly
sections.body: 形容詞字尾把名詞變成形容詞：help→helpful（有幫助的）、danger→dangerous（危險的）、nature→natural（自然的）、environment→environmental（環境的）。副詞多半是「形容詞＋ly」：quick→quickly、careful→carefully。形容詞用來修飾名詞或接在 be／look／sound 等連綴動詞後；副詞用來修飾一般動詞、形容詞或整句。
examples: The volunteers were very helpful to the earthquake victims. 志工們對地震災民非常有幫助。
The students listened quietly to the teacher's instructions. 學生們安靜地聽老師的指示。
Climbing that mountain alone can be extremely dangerous. 獨自爬那座山可能極度危險。

sections.h: 四大前綴：un- / dis- / re- / in-
sections.body: 字首不改變詞類，只改變語意。un-（不、非）：happy→unhappy、safe→unsafe；dis-（相反、否定）：agree→disagree、appear→disappear；re-（再一次、重新）：write→rewrite、build→rebuild；in-（不，另有 im-/il-/ir- 變體）：correct→incorrect、possible→impossible、legal→illegal、regular→irregular。注意 in- 在 b／m／p 前變 im-（impolite），在 l 前變 il-，在 r 前變 ir-。
examples: It is unsafe to leave the front door open at night. 晚上把大門開著是不安全的。
After the earthquake, the city promised to rebuild the broken bridges. 地震後，市府承諾重建損毀的橋樑。
The information turned out to be incorrect, so we made a wrong choice. 那資訊結果是錯的，所以我們做了錯誤的選擇。

sections.h: 依句法判斷空格詞類
sections.body: 學測題常不告訴你詞義，而要你「看結構選詞類」。口訣：冠詞／形容詞後→填名詞；be、look、sound、seem 後→填形容詞；一般動詞前後修飾語→填副詞；形容詞或整句前的修飾語→填副詞。例如 an important ___（填名詞 decision），drives ___（填副詞 carefully），sounds ___（填形容詞 successful）。先鎖定詞類，再從選項找對應字尾，往往不必懂整句。
examples: The plan sounded successful, so the team decided to try it. 這計畫聽起來會成功，所以團隊決定試試看。
She solved the difficult puzzle surprisingly quickly. 她出乎意料地快就解開了那道難題。
The improvement in air quality pleased the whole town. 空氣品質的改善讓整個小鎮很開心。

sections.h: 複合字與轉品（同形不同詞類）
sections.body: 複合字是兩個字合成一個新字：blackboard（黑板）、well-prepared（準備充分的）、five-year-old（五歲的）。轉品（conversion）則是同一個字不改字形就換詞類：water 可當名詞（水）也可當動詞（澆水）；google 從名詞變動詞（上網搜尋）；upgrade 既是名詞也是動詞。判斷時仍靠句法：句中缺動詞就把它當動詞，缺名詞就當名詞。
examples: Remember to water the plants before you leave for school. 上學前記得幫植物澆水。
She gave a well-prepared speech that moved the whole audience. 她發表了一場準備充分的演說，感動了全場觀眾。
Many students now google new words instead of using a paper dictionary. 許多學生現在上網查新單字，而不用紙本字典。

mistakes: 誤：He made an important decide. → 正：He made an important decision.（冠詞＋形容詞後要用名詞，不能用動詞。）
誤：She drives very careful. → 正：She drives very carefully.（修飾動詞 drives 要用副詞，不是形容詞。）
誤：The plan sounds successfully. → 正：The plan sounds successful.（連綴動詞 sound 後接形容詞當主詞補語，不用副詞。）
誤：a friendly girl 寫成副詞用（She smiled friendly.）→ 正：She smiled in a friendly way.（friendly 是形容詞，字尾 -ly 不代表副詞。）
誤：inpossible／uncorrect → 正：impossible／incorrect（in- 在 p 前變 im-，correct 的否定用 in- 而非 un-。）
note.tip: 看空格前後就懂了：冠詞後填名詞、be／sound 後填形容詞、動詞旁填副詞——先鎖詞類，再挑字尾。

d1.stem: The teacher gave us a clear ___ of the new grammar rule.
d1.explanation: 空格前有冠詞 a 和形容詞 clear，需要名詞。動詞 explain 加 -ation 變名詞 explanation（解釋）。

d1.stem: My grandmother always drives very ___ on the highway.
d1.explanation: 空格修飾動詞 drives，要用副詞。careful 加 -ly 變副詞 carefully（小心地）。careless 意思相反且仍是形容詞。

d1.stem: Her ___ to strangers made everyone in the class feel warm.
d1.explanation: 空格當主詞需要名詞，形容詞 kind 加 -ness 變名詞 kindness（善良）。

d1.stem: The ___ of cultures in this city attracts many tourists.
d1.explanation: 空格前有 The、後有 of，需要名詞。形容詞 diverse 加 -ity 變名詞 diversity（多樣性）。diversify 是動詞。

d1.stem: The volunteers were very ___ to the earthquake victims.
d1.explanation: be 動詞 were 後接形容詞當主詞補語，help 加 -ful 變 helpful（有幫助的），並與 to 搭配。helpless（無助的）語意不合，且不與 to 搭配。

d1.stem: Climbing that mountain alone at night is really ___.
d1.explanation: be 動詞 is 後接形容詞。名詞 danger 加 -ous 變形容詞 dangerous（危險的）。

d1.stem: We must work together to solve this ___ problem.
d1.explanation: 空格修飾名詞 problem，要用形容詞。environment 加 -al 變形容詞 environmental（環境的）。

d1.stem: The whole class listened ___ while the principal was speaking.
d1.explanation: 空格修飾動詞 listened，要用副詞。quiet 加 -ly 變 quietly（安靜地）。

d1.stem: It is ___ to leave the front door open when you go to bed.
d1.explanation: 就寢時把大門敞開顯然「不安全」，safe 加否定前綴 un- 變 unsafe。句型 It is + 形容詞，故不用副詞 safely 或名詞 safety。

d1.stem: Your essay has many mistakes, so please ___ it before Friday.
d1.explanation: 因為有很多錯誤要「重寫」，write 加前綴 re-（再一次）變 rewrite。祈使句用原形動詞。

d1.stem: The company announced the ___ of a brand-new smartphone.
d1.explanation: 空格前有 the、後有 of，需要名詞。動詞 develop 加 -ment 變名詞 development（開發、研發）。

d2.stem: Although the plan sounded ___ at first, it later failed completely.
d2.explanation: 連綴動詞 sound 後接形容詞當補語。success（名詞）、succeed（動詞）、successfully（副詞）皆不合，應用形容詞 successful。

d2.stem: The scientist explained her theory so ___ that even beginners understood it.
d2.explanation: 空格修飾動詞 explained，要用副詞 clearly（清楚地）。so ... that 中間放副詞修飾動作。
