d2.stem: His ___ behavior in class annoyed the teacher, who told him to grow up.
d2.explanation: 依「惹惱老師、叫他成熟點」的負面語氣，用 childish（幼稚的，貶義）。childlike 是「純真的」褒義，語意不合。

d2.stem: The information she gave was ___, so we ended up going to the wrong place.
d2.explanation: 因為去錯地方，資訊應是「錯的」。correct 的否定用前綴 in- 變 incorrect，而非 un-。be 動詞後用形容詞，故不選副詞 correctly。

d2.stem: The ___ of this year's exam surprised many students who expected easy questions.
d2.explanation: 空格前有 The、後有 of，需要名詞當主詞。此處指「考試的難度」，為不可數用法的 difficulty（難度）；difficulties 意為「困難、麻煩」語意不貼切，difficultly 非常用副詞。

d2.stem: There has been a great ___ in air quality since the old factory closed down.
d2.explanation: a great ___ in 需要名詞。動詞 improve 加 -ment 變名詞 improvement（改善）。動名詞 improving 前不加冠詞 a。

d2.stem: The twin brothers look ___ similar, so people often mistake one for the other.
d2.explanation: 空格修飾形容詞 similar，要用副詞 surprisingly（出乎意料地）。名詞、分詞形式皆不能修飾形容詞。

d2.stem: The magician suddenly ___ from the stage, and the whole audience gasped.
d2.explanation: disappear from the stage（從舞台上消失）為自然搭配，觀眾因此驚呼。appear 加前綴 dis- 變 disappear，且句子需要過去式動詞，appearance 是名詞。

d2.stem: The old man is well known for his ___ donations to poor children every year.
d2.explanation: 空格修飾名詞 donations，要用形容詞 generous（慷慨的）。generosity 是名詞、generously 是副詞、generate 是動詞，皆不合。

d2.stem: Being ___ of the coming storm, the hikers decided to turn back at once.
d2.explanation: be aware of（察覺、知道）為固定用法。因為「察覺到暴風雨」才立刻折返，故用 aware；unaware 語意矛盾，awareness 是名詞。

d2.stem: She is a ___ person who always greets her neighbors with a big smile.
d2.explanation: 空格修飾名詞 person，要用形容詞。friendly（友善的）雖以 -ly 結尾，卻是形容詞不是副詞，這是常見陷阱。

d2.stem: After the flood, the government promised to ___ all the damaged houses.
d2.explanation: 洪水後受損房屋要「重建」，build 加前綴 re-（重新）變 rebuild。to 後接原形動詞，故不選名詞 building 或 builder。

d2.stem: Everyone praised the ___ of the young pianist after her amazing performance.
d2.explanation: the ___ of 需要名詞。talent（天賦）為名詞；talented 是形容詞、talentedly 為副詞。此處指「她的才華」用單數不可數的 talent 最恰當。

d3.stem: Not until she received the ___ letter did she believe she had won the scholarship.
d3.explanation: 空格修飾名詞 letter，需要具名詞性的字構成「錄取信」，用名詞 acceptance（acceptance letter 錄取通知）。句首 Not until 引導倒裝，助動詞 did 提前，屬進階句型。

d3.stem: A good doctor must be ___ to patients' feelings, listening patiently to every worry.
d3.explanation: 依「顧及病人感受」語意用 sensitive（敏感體貼的），且 be sensitive to 為固定搭配。sensible 意為「明智的」，是易混淆陷阱；be 動詞後用形容詞而非副詞 sensitively。

d3.stem: Only by working together can we ___ our shared dream of a cleaner planet.
d3.explanation: Only by... 置句首引起倒裝（can we + 原形動詞）。句中缺動詞，用 realize（實現）。real 是形容詞、really 副詞、reality 名詞皆不能當動詞。

d3.stem: Buying in bulk is a far more ___ choice for a large family trying to save money.
d3.explanation: 依「省錢」語意用 economical（節省的、划算的）。economic 意為「經濟的、經濟學的」，是最常見的辨析陷阱；空格修飾名詞 choice 故用形容詞。

d3.stem: Shakespeare made great ___ contributions that still influence writers around the world today.
d3.explanation: 修飾名詞 contributions 且語意為「文學的」，用 literary。literal 是「字面的」、literate 是「識字的」、literally 是副詞，皆為易混形近字。

d3.stem: The harder the task is, the greater the ___ we feel when we finally finish it.
d3.explanation: the + 比較級 ... the + 比較級句型中，the greater the ___ 需要名詞當受詞。動詞 satisfy 的名詞是 satisfaction（滿足感）。

d3.stem: The novelist is highly ___, creating fantasy worlds that no one has ever imagined before.
d3.explanation: 形容「作家富有想像力」用 imaginative。imaginary 是「虛構的」（形容事物）、imaginable 是「可想像的」、imagination 是名詞，三者為經典辨析組。

d3.stem: It was very ___ of you to prepare a small gift for every single guest at the party.
d3.explanation: It is + 形容詞 + of somebody 表人的特質，依「體貼」語意用 considerate（體貼的）。considerable 意為「相當大的」，是常見混淆點。

d3.stem: After the joint meeting ended, the two managers returned to their ___ offices to write reports.
d3.explanation: 依「各自的辦公室」語意用 respective（各自的）。respectful 是「恭敬的」、respectable 是「體面的」、respectively 是副詞，四字極易混淆。

d3.stem: The ___ with which she solved the tricky puzzle amazed everyone in the room.
d3.explanation: 介系詞 with 後需名詞，the ease with which...（她解題的輕鬆程度）為進階關係子句結構。ease 是常用名詞；easiness 罕用，easy/easily 詞類不合。

d3.stem: The instructions were so confusing that many users ___ the new machine and broke it on the first day.
d3.explanation: 因「說明混亂」導致「用錯」而弄壞，前綴 mis-（錯誤地）最貼合語意，misuse（誤用）。overuse 是過度使用、reuse 是重複使用，皆不符因果。

d3.stem: Her ___ remarks during the debate hurt several classmates, even though she claimed she was only joking.
d3.explanation: 空格修飾名詞 remarks，需要形容詞 offensive（冒犯的）。offend 是動詞、offense 名詞、offensively 副詞，皆不能直接修飾名詞。

spec.topic: 校園生活與社團活動介紹

questions.stem: What is the main idea of this passage?
options: Clubs at Sunny High School help students learn and make friends.
Students at Sunny High School do not like joining clubs.
The Music Club is the only club at the school.
Club meetings are held only once a year.
questions.explanation: 全文介紹陽光高中的各種社團如何幫助學生學習新技能並結交朋友，第一句與最後一句都點出主旨。其他選項與內文不符：學生喜歡社團、社團有多個、每週聚會。

questions.stem: How often do the clubs meet?
options: Once a week on Wednesday afternoons
questions.explanation: 文中明確提到「The clubs meet once a week, usually on Wednesday afternoons.」，屬細節題，故答案為每週三下午聚會一次。

questions.stem: What does the Music Club do?
options: It plants trees around the town.
It teaches students to cook meals.
It practices songs and plays at the school festival.
It helps students with their homework.
questions.explanation: 文中說音樂社「practice songs together and play at the school festival」，練習歌曲並在校慶演出。種樹是環保社、其他選項未提及。

questions.stem: In the passage, the word "skills" is closest in meaning to ___.
questions.explanation: 字義題。skills(技能)指透過學習而具備的能力，與 abilities(能力)意思最接近。mistakes(錯誤)、problems(問題)、rules(規則)皆不符。

reading.title: Club Life at Sunny High School
reading.text: School is not only about books and tests. At Sunny High School, students also join clubs to learn new skills and make good friends. There are many clubs to choose from. In the Music Club, students practice songs together and play at the school festival. In the Art Club, they draw pictures and paint colorful walls. Students who love nature can join the Green Club, which plants trees around the town. The clubs meet once a week, usually on Wednesday afternoons. During the meetings, students share ideas, help each other, and have a lot of fun. Many students say that club time is the best part of the week. They feel happy because they can do the things they really like. Joining a club also helps shy students talk to more people. Teachers believe that clubs make school life richer and more exciting. So when you start high school, do not be afraid. Find a club you love, and enjoy every moment of it!

spec.topic: 一種常見動物的有趣習性

reading.title: Why Do Cats Knead?
reading.text:
Have you ever watched a cat push its front paws up and down on a soft blanket or on your lap? This funny action is called "kneading." Many cat owners also call it "making biscuits" because it looks like the cat is making bread with its paws.

Why do cats do this? Scientists believe the habit starts when cats are very young. Baby cats, or kittens, press on their mother's body while drinking her milk. This pressing helps the milk come out. So kneading gives young cats a feeling of warmth and safety.

Even after cats grow up, they keep this habit. When a cat kneads on your legs, it may feel happy and relaxed. Some cats also knead to mark a place as their own, because there are special scent glands in their paws.

Next time your cat makes biscuits on you, remember it is a sign of love and comfort. Your cat feels safe with you!

questions.stem: What is the main idea of this passage?
options: How to teach a cat to make real bread
The reasons why cats knead with their paws
Why kittens need their mother's milk
How to stop a cat from scratching people
questions.explanation: 全文圍繞「貓咪為什麼會用腳掌揉按（kneading）」這個習性，並解釋其來源與意義，因此主旨是選項(B)。其他選項只是文中的細節或未提及的內容。

questions.stem: According to the passage, why do kittens press on their mother's body?
options: To keep themselves warm in winter
To help the milk come out
To mark the mother as their own
questions.explanation: 文中明確指出小貓在喝奶時按壓母貓的身體「helps the milk come out（幫助奶水流出）」，故答案為(B)。

questions.stem: The word "kneading" in the passage most likely means ___.
options: pushing paws up and down on something soft
questions.explanation: 由第一段的描述「push its front paws up and down on a soft blanket」可知 kneading 就是用前腳掌在柔軟的東西上上下推按，故選(B)。

questions.stem: What can we infer about a cat that kneads on your lap?
options: It is feeling sick and needs a doctor
It is angry and wants to leave
It feels safe and comfortable with you
It is hungry and wants food
questions.explanation: 文末提到揉按是「a sign of love and comfort」且「Your cat feels safe with you」，可推論貓咪在你腿上揉按代表牠感到安全與舒適，故選(C)。

spec.topic: 學生與老師討論報告安排

questions.stem: What is the student mainly worried about at the beginning of the conversation?
options: She cannot find a topic for her report.
She may not finish her report by Friday.
She wants to change her group members.
She does not understand the reading material.
questions.explanation: 學生一開始說 "I'm afraid I can't finish my report by Friday."（我恐怕無法在星期五前完成報告），可知她擔心的是趕不上截止日,故選(B)。其他選項文中未提及。

questions.stem: Why does the student need more time to finish her work?
options: She was sick and stayed in the hospital.
She has to take part in a swimming contest.
She joined a science project this week.
She lost her notes on the bus.
questions.explanation: 學生說 "I have a swimming contest this week, so I have little time to study."（本週有游泳比賽,幾乎沒時間讀書），因此需要更多時間,答案為(B)。

questions.stem: What does the teacher finally decide to do?
options: Give the student a lower grade.
Let the student hand in the report next Monday.
Ask another student to help her.
Cancel the report for the whole class.
questions.explanation: 老師最後說 "You may hand it in next Monday instead."（你可以改成下週一交），表示同意延到下週一,故選(B)。

questions.stem: What can we infer about the teacher from the dialogue?
options: He is strict and never changes the rules.
He is kind and willing to understand students.
He does not care about the report at all.
He is angry about the student's excuse.
questions.explanation: 老師願意聆聽學生的困難並延後截止日,還提醒她之後要安排好時間,可推論他是個體貼、願意理解學生的老師,故選(B)。(A)(C)(D)與老師的態度不符。

reading.text:
(Amy knocks on the office door.)

Amy: Excuse me, Mr. Lin. Do you have a minute?

Mr. Lin: Sure, Amy. Come in. What's the matter?

Amy: I'm afraid I can't finish my report by Friday. I have a swimming contest this week, so I have little time to study.

Mr. Lin: I see. When is your contest?

Amy: It's on Thursday afternoon. After that, I still need two more days to write everything down.

Mr. Lin: That does sound busy. How much have you finished so far?

Amy: About half of it. I've read all the books, but I haven't written the ending yet.

Mr. Lin: All right. You may hand it in next Monday instead. But please try to plan your time better next time.

Amy: Thank you so much, Mr. Lin! I promise I'll do my best.

Mr. Lin: Good. And good luck with your contest, too.

Amy: Thanks! I won't let you down.
reading.title: A Talk About the Report
Sunrise High School English Speech Contest
reading.text:
Do you love speaking English? Come and join the Sunrise High School English Speech Contest! This year's theme is "A Person Who Changed My Life." All students in Grades 10 to 12 are welcome to sign up.

The contest will be held in the school auditorium on Friday, October 17, from 2:00 p.m. to 4:30 p.m. Each speaker will have three to four minutes to give a speech. Notes are allowed, but please do not simply read from your paper. Three teachers will judge every speech based on pronunciation, content, and confidence.

The first-place winner will receive a prize of NT$3,000 and a certificate. The second and third winners will also get book coupons.

To sign up, please give your name and class to Ms. Chen in the English Office before October 10. Places are limited to twenty speakers, so do not wait too long!

Believe in yourself, step onto the stage, and let your voice be heard!

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this poster?
options: To announce a school music festival
To invite students to join an English speech contest
To sell books in the English Office
questions.explanation: 整篇海報主旨是邀請學生報名參加 English Speech Contest(英語演講比賽),開頭「Come and join the Sunrise High School English Speech Contest!」即點明主旨,故選 (B)。其他選項皆非海報內容。

questions.stem: When and where will the contest take place?
options: In the English Office on October 17
questions.explanation: 文中明確寫道比賽在「the school auditorium(學校禮堂)」舉行,時間是「Friday, October 17」。October 10 是報名截止日,English Office 是報名地點,故正確答案為 (C)。

questions.stem: According to the poster, what will the first-place winner get?
questions.explanation: 文中說「The first-place winner will receive a prize of NT$3,000 and a certificate.」第一名可得三千元獎金與一張獎狀。書券(book coupons)是給第二、三名的,故選 (B)。

questions.stem: What can we infer from the sentence "Places are limited to twenty speakers, so do not wait too long"?
options: Students should sign up early because spots are few
Anyone can join at any time
The contest will last twenty hours
Only twenty teachers can watch the contest
questions.explanation: 由「名額限二十人,不要等太久」可推論出報名名額有限,應盡早報名,故選 (A)。其餘選項與「places are limited(名額有限)」的推論不符。

questions.stem: What is the main idea of this passage?
options: Plastic products are cheap and convenient for daily life
Reducing plastic use is an important way to protect the environment
Sea animals prefer to eat plastic bags
Recycling factories make a lot of money
questions.explanation: 全文圍繞塑膠垃圾對環境的傷害以及如何減少塑膠使用，最後呼籲小改變能保護地球，故主旨為「減少塑膠使用是保護環境的重要方式」。選項(B)最貼近主旨。其餘選項僅為文中細節或與文意不符。

questions.stem: According to the passage, why is plastic harmful to the ocean?
options: It makes the sea water warmer
It changes the color of the sea
It can take hundreds of years to break down and harms sea animals
It helps fish find more food
questions.explanation: 文中提到塑膠可能需要數百年才能分解(break down)，且海洋動物常誤把塑膠當食物而受害，故正確答案為(C)。其他選項在文中未提及或與事實不符。

questions.stem: In the passage, the word "reusable" is closest in meaning to ___.
options: something that can only be used once
something that can be used many times
something that is made of metal
questions.explanation: 字義題。"reusable" 由 re-(再) + use(使用) + -able(能夠) 組成，意為「可重複使用的」，與"used many times"意思最接近，故選(B)。文中以可重複使用的購物袋和水瓶對比一次性塑膠。

questions.stem: What can we infer from the passage?
options: Everyone must stop using plastic completely today
Small daily actions by individuals can make a difference
Only governments can solve the plastic problem
Plastic pollution is impossible to reduce
questions.explanation: 推論題。文末鼓勵讀者從自帶購物袋、水瓶等小事做起，暗示個人日常的小改變也能帶來影響，故可推論(B)。文章語氣正面且務實，並未主張立刻完全停用塑膠或只靠政府，(A)(C)(D)皆與文意不符。

reading.text:
Every year, people around the world throw away millions of tons of plastic. Plastic bags, bottles, and straws are cheap and easy to use, but they cause serious problems for our planet. Much of this waste ends up in rivers and oceans. Because plastic can take hundreds of years to break down, it stays in the water for a very long time. Sea animals often mistake small pieces of plastic for food, and eating them can make the animals sick or even kill them.

The good news is that we can all help. Reducing plastic is not as hard as it sounds. Instead of using plastic bags at the store, we can bring our own reusable bags. Instead of buying bottled water, we can carry a reusable water bottle. We can also say no to plastic straws and choose products with less packaging.

These may seem like small actions, but if millions of people make these changes, together we can protect the environment and keep our oceans clean for the future.
reading.title: Small Changes, Cleaner Oceans: Reducing Plastic Waste
Why Teenagers Need More Sleep
reading.text:
Many teenagers stay up late and wake up early for school, so they often feel tired during the day. Scientists say that teenagers actually need about nine hours of sleep each night. However, most of them get far less than that.

One reason is biology. During the teenage years, the body's inner clock changes. This makes teenagers feel sleepy later at night. As a result, going to bed early becomes difficult, even when they want to.

Losing sleep can cause serious problems. Tired students find it hard to pay attention in class, and their grades may drop. Poor sleep is also linked to sadness and stress. Some studies show that sleepy teenagers are more likely to have accidents, too.

Doctors suggest a few simple habits. Teenagers should turn off their phones before bed, because bright screens keep the brain awake. Going to sleep at the same time every night also helps. With enough rest, teenagers can feel happier, think more clearly, and stay healthier.

questions.stem: What is the main idea of this passage?
options: Teenagers should study harder at night.
Sleep is important for teenagers' health and learning.
Phones are the best tool for teenagers.
Doctors know little about sleep problems.
questions.explanation: 全文圍繞青少年需要充足睡眠、缺乏睡眠的問題及改善方法，主旨是睡眠對青少年健康與學習很重要，故選 (B)。其他選項只是片段或與文意不符。

questions.stem: According to the passage, why is it hard for teenagers to go to bed early?
options: Their inner body clock makes them feel sleepy later.
questions.explanation: 第二段指出青少年時期身體的『inner clock』改變，使他們較晚才想睡，因此難以早睡，故選 (B)。文中並未提到作業太多或工作等原因。

questions.stem: What does the word "linked" in the third paragraph most likely mean?
questions.explanation: 『Poor sleep is also linked to sadness and stress』意指睡眠不足與悲傷、壓力有關聯，linked 最接近 connected（有關聯、相連），故選 (A)。

questions.stem: What can we infer from the passage?
options: Using phones before bed helps teenagers sleep better.
Getting enough sleep can improve teenagers' mood and thinking.
Teenagers need less sleep than adults.
questions.explanation: 最後一段提到有足夠休息後青少年會更快樂、思考更清楚、更健康，可推論充足睡眠能改善情緒與思考，故選 (B)。(A) 與文中『螢幕使大腦清醒』相反；(C)(D) 文中皆未提及。

spec.topic: 一次難忘的旅行或志工經驗

questions.stem: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: A trip to the beach with the writer's family
How a volunteer experience changed the writer's view of giving
The difficulty of building houses in a village
The importance of learning English in school
questions.explanation: 全文以作者在偏鄉當志工的經歷為主軸,結尾點出「付出比得到更快樂」的體悟,故主旨是志工經驗如何改變作者對「給予」的看法。選項A、C、D皆只是文中細節或未提及。

questions.stem: Why did the writer feel nervous at first?
options: He had never taught anyone before
He did not know how to build a house
He got lost on the way to the village
He could not speak the local language
questions.explanation: 細節題。文中提到作者原本擔心自己教不好,是因為他從未教過任何人 ("I had never taught anyone"),所以感到緊張。其他選項文中並未提及。

questions.stem: What can we infer about the children in the village?
options: They were eager to learn despite few resources
They had many teachers to help them
questions.explanation: 推論題。文中描述孩子們雖然只有破舊的課本,眼中卻閃著渴望,且緊跟著作者的每一句話,可推論他們在資源匱乏下仍十分渴望學習。故選C。

questions.stem: What does the word "reward" in the last paragraph most likely mean?
options: A sum of money for hard work
A feeling of satisfaction from helping others
A prize given by the teacher
A long rest after a busy week
questions.explanation: 字義題。由上下文「付出比得到更快樂」及看到孩子微笑而感到值得可知,此處 reward 指的是幫助他人所獲得的內心滿足感,而非金錢或獎品,故選B。

reading.text:
Last summer, I joined a volunteer program and spent two weeks in a small mountain village. My job was to teach English to the local children. At first, I felt nervous because I had never taught anyone, and I was afraid I would do it badly.

On the first morning, I walked into a tiny classroom. The children had only old, torn textbooks, but their eyes were bright with hope. They followed every word I said and repeated the sentences again and again. Their eagerness surprised me. Even without computers or fancy tools, they never stopped trying.

By the end of the two weeks, the children could sing songs and tell simple stories in English. On my last day, a shy girl handed me a drawing of the two of us under a big tree. I almost cried.

I went there to give, but I received far more. Their smiles were the greatest reward I could imagine. That trip taught me a simple truth: giving makes us happier than getting.
reading.title: The Summer That Changed Me

spec.topic: 手機使用時間統計圖表判讀

reading.title: Reading a Screen-Time Chart
reading.text:
Last month, our class carried out a survey to find out how much time students spent on their phones each day. We collected the data and turned it into a bar chart. The chart showed four activities: social media, gaming, watching videos, and studying.

The results surprised many of us. On average, students spent 3.5 hours a day on social media, making it the top activity. Watching videos came second at 2 hours, while gaming took up 1.5 hours. Studying with the phone, however, received only 45 minutes—the lowest of all.

Interestingly, the chart also revealed a difference between weekdays and weekends. On weekends, total screen time nearly doubled, mostly because of gaming and videos. Our teacher pointed out that being aware of these numbers is the first step toward managing our habits. After seeing the chart, several classmates decided to set daily limits on their apps.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this passage?
options: To warn students about the dangers of gaming
To describe a survey about students' daily phone use
To teach students how to make a bar chart
To compare different brands of smartphones
questions.explanation: 全文圍繞班上進行的手機使用時間調查與圖表結果展開，主旨是描述這項調查，故選(B)。(A)遊戲危險、(C)如何製圖、(D)品牌比較皆非重點。

questions.stem: According to the chart, which activity took up the most time on average?
questions.explanation: 文中明確指出社群媒體平均每天3.5小時，是最高的(the top activity)，故選(C)社群媒體。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students' phone use on weekends?
options: They used their phones less than on weekdays
They spent much more time on their phones
They only used phones for studying
They stopped using social media completely
questions.explanation: 文中提到週末總螢幕時間幾乎加倍(nearly doubled)，可推論週末手機使用時間大幅增加，故選(B)。

questions.stem: What does the word "aware" mostly likely mean in the passage?
questions.explanation: aware of these numbers指「知道、意識到這些數字」，是了解狀況的第一步，故字義最接近(A)knowing about something。

spec.topic: 寫給筆友介紹台灣文化的信

reading.title: A Letter to My Pen Pal: Discovering Taiwan
reading.text:
Dear Emma,

Thank you for your last letter about life in London. Now let me tell you about Taiwan, my home!

Taiwan is a small island, but its culture is rich and colorful. One thing you must experience is our night markets. In the evening, streets fill with the smell of stinky tofu, bubble tea, and grilled squid. Locals and tourists walk shoulder to shoulder, playing games and enjoying cheap, tasty snacks until midnight.

Festivals are also special here. During the Lantern Festival, thousands of glowing lanterns rise into the sky, each carrying a wish. At the Dragon Boat Festival, teams race long, colorful boats while families eat sticky rice dumplings.

What I love most is the warmth of our people. Strangers often help you find your way, and shop owners give you a friendly smile. If you ever visit, I promise to show you these wonders myself.

Please write back soon and tell me more about your country!

Your friend,
Mei-ling

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this letter?
options: To invite Emma to move to Taiwan permanently
To introduce aspects of Taiwanese culture to a pen pal
To describe the history of Taiwan's festivals
To compare food in Taiwan and London
questions.explanation: 全信圍繞台灣的夜市、節慶與人情味等文化面向，向筆友 Emma 介紹台灣文化，故選 (B)。(A) 只是邀請她來訪，非搬去定居；(C)(D) 皆為細節而非主旨。

questions.stem: According to the letter, what can people do at Taiwan's night markets?
options: Release glowing lanterns into the sky
Eat cheap snacks and play games until midnight
questions.explanation: 文中提到夜市裡人們「playing games and enjoying cheap, tasty snacks until midnight」，故選 (C)。(A) 屬端午節、(B) 屬元宵節，皆非夜市活動。

questions.stem: What does the phrase "walk shoulder to shoulder" most likely mean?
options: Walk very close together in a crowd
Carry heavy bags on their shoulders
Walk while arguing with each other
questions.explanation: shoulder to shoulder 字面為「肩並肩」，描述夜市人潮擁擠、彼此靠得很近，故選 (A)。其他選項與擁擠人潮的語境不符。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the writer's feelings toward Taiwan?
options: She feels bored with her hometown
She is proud of and fond of her home
She wishes she could live in London instead
She thinks Taiwan is too crowded and noisy
questions.explanation: 作者以「my home!」「What I love most」及主動承諾親自導覽等語氣，流露對家鄉的驕傲與喜愛，可推論選 (B)。文中無厭倦、想搬去倫敦或嫌吵的訊息。

spec.topic: 人工智慧對未來工作的影響

questions.stem: What is the main idea of this passage?
options: Artificial intelligence will soon replace every human worker in the world.
AI is reshaping the job market, eliminating some roles while demanding new human skills.
Factory workers are the only people affected by artificial intelligence.
Governments should ban the use of AI to protect existing jobs.
questions.explanation: 全文主旨在說明 AI 正在「重塑」就業市場：一方面取代部分工作，另一方面創造需求並要求勞工培養新技能。選項(B)最能涵蓋整篇的核心。(A)過度誇大「取代所有人」，(C)只提工廠工人太狹隘，(D)文中並未主張禁用 AI。

questions.stem: According to the passage, which kind of jobs are MOST likely to be automated?
options: Jobs that require creativity and emotional understanding
Jobs that involve routine and repetitive tasks
Jobs that demand complex ethical judgment
Jobs that rely on human collaboration
questions.explanation: 文中明確指出重複性、例行性(routine and repetitive)的工作最容易被自動化取代，屬細節題。其餘選項(創造力、情感、倫理判斷、人際合作)在文中被視為 AI 難以取代、反而更受重視的人類特質。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about workers who want to stay competitive in the future?
options: They should avoid learning about technology altogether.
They ought to keep learning and adapt to new tools.
They must move to factories for stable employment.
They need to compete directly with machines on speed.
questions.explanation: 推論題。文章結尾強調終身學習(lifelong learning)與適應力(adaptability)的重要，可推知勞工需持續學習、適應新工具才能保持競爭力。(A)與文意相反,(C)工廠工作正被取代,(D)在速度上與機器競爭並非文章建議。

questions.stem: The word "displace" in the passage is closest in meaning to ___.
questions.explanation: 字義題。displace 意為「取代、使離開原位」，與上下文「AI 取代部分勞工」相符，故最接近 replace(取代)。encourage(鼓勵)、train(訓練)、reward(獎勵)皆與詞義不符。

reading.text:
Artificial intelligence is transforming the workplace faster than many experts once predicted. Machines can now analyze data, translate languages, and even write basic reports in seconds. As a result, jobs that involve routine and repetitive tasks are the most likely to be automated, and some workers may be displaced by increasingly capable software.

However, the story is not entirely gloomy. While AI eliminates certain positions, it also creates demand for new ones, such as data analysts, robot supervisors, and engineers who design smarter systems. Moreover, qualities that machines struggle to imitate—creativity, empathy, and ethical judgment—are becoming more valuable than ever.

Experts therefore argue that the key to surviving this shift is adaptability. Instead of fearing technology, workers are encouraged to embrace lifelong learning and to develop skills that complement, rather than compete with, machines. Those who keep learning will not merely endure the age of AI; they may well thrive in it.
reading.title: AI and the Future of Work

spec.topic: 社群媒體與人際關係的兩面

reading.title: The Double Edge of Social Media
reading.text:
Social media has reshaped the way people build and maintain relationships. On one hand, platforms like Instagram and LINE allow us to stay connected with distant relatives and old classmates who might otherwise fade from our lives. A single message can bridge thousands of kilometers in seconds, and shared photos let us feel present at moments we cannot physically attend.

On the other hand, this convenience comes at a cost. Studies suggest that the more time teenagers spend scrolling through carefully edited posts, the more likely they are to feel lonely or inadequate. Constantly comparing our ordinary daily lives to others' highlight reels can quietly erode our self-esteem. Moreover, a text message, however clever, can never fully replace the warmth of a face-to-face conversation.

The key, therefore, lies not in rejecting technology but in using it wisely. When social media supplements real interaction rather than substituting for it, it becomes a bridge instead of a barrier. Ultimately, the quality of our relationships depends on how, not how often, we connect.

questions.stem: What is the main idea of this passage?
options: Social media should be completely avoided by teenagers.
Social media has both benefits and drawbacks, so it should be used wisely.
Face-to-face conversations are no longer necessary in modern life.
Instagram and LINE are the best tools for making new friends.
questions.explanation: 全文採「一方面…另一方面…」的對比結構，先談社群媒體的好處（維繫遠距關係），再談壞處（孤獨、自尊受損），最後結論在於「明智使用」。因此主旨為社群媒體有利有弊、應善用，選項(B)正確。(A)過度絕對，(C)(D)僅為細節或與文意相反。

questions.stem: According to the passage, why might teenagers feel lonely or inadequate?
options: Because they cannot afford expensive smartphones.
Because they compare their ordinary lives to others' edited, idealized posts.
Because their relatives refuse to reply to their messages.
Because face-to-face conversations make them nervous.
questions.explanation: 第二段明確指出：青少年花越多時間瀏覽「carefully edited posts」，越容易感到孤獨或不如人，因為把平凡日常拿去和別人的「highlight reels（精華片段）」比較會侵蝕自尊。故選(B)，屬細節理解題。

questions.stem: What does the word "erode" most likely mean in the passage?
questions.explanation: erode 原意為「侵蝕、逐漸削弱」。由上下文「不斷比較會 quietly erode our self-esteem（悄悄地__自尊）」可推知是負面、逐漸減損的意思，故選(A) to gradually weaken or destroy。此為字義題。

questions.stem: What can be inferred from the last paragraph?
options: Technology will eventually replace all human relationships.
People who use social media more often always have better friendships.
How we use social media matters more than how much we use it.
Rejecting all forms of technology is the only way to be happy.
questions.explanation: 末段強調關鍵不在拒絕科技，而在明智使用；當社群媒體「補充」而非「取代」真實互動時便成為橋梁，並以「depends on how, not how often」作結。可推論「使用方式比使用頻率更重要」，故選(C)。(A)(D)過度絕對，(B)與文意相反。

spec.topic: 一位科學家或發明家的貢獻

questions.stem: What is the main idea of this passage?
options: mRNA vaccines were invented overnight during the pandemic
Katalin Karikó's persistence turned a doubted idea into life-saving vaccines
Nobel Prizes are usually given to famous scientists
Universities should never cut research funding
questions.explanation: 全文以卡里科數十年的堅持為主軸,說明她不被看好的 mRNA 研究最終促成救命疫苗並獲諾貝爾獎,故 (B) 為主旨。(A) 與文中「數十年」矛盾;(C)(D) 僅為枝節或未提及。

questions.stem: According to the passage, what problem did Karikó and Weissman solve?
options: mRNA could not be produced in large amounts
mRNA triggered a harmful immune reaction in the body
mRNA was too expensive for hospitals to buy
mRNA could only be stored at room temperature
questions.explanation: 文中明確指出他們發現改造 mRNA 的成分可避免引發身體危險的免疫反應,故選 (B)。其餘選項文中皆未提及。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about Karikó's career before her success?
options: She was always well funded and highly respected
She faced repeated rejection and professional setbacks
She quickly won awards for her early research
She gave up science and changed her profession
questions.explanation: 文中提到她被降職、經費被拒,可推論她成功前歷經一再的挫折,故 (B)。(A)(C) 與內文相反;她始終沒放棄,故 (D) 錯。

questions.stem: In the passage, the word "skeptical" is closest in meaning to ___.
questions.explanation: skeptical 意為「懷疑的、不相信的」,與 doubtful(存疑的)最接近,故選 (A)。excited 興奮、grateful 感激、curious 好奇皆不符。

reading.text: For years, few people believed in Katalin Karikó's work. A Hungarian-born biochemist, she was convinced that a fragile molecule called messenger RNA, or mRNA, could one day instruct human cells to fight disease. Yet grant committees remained skeptical, funding was repeatedly refused, and she was even demoted at her university. Rather than abandon her vision, Karikó pressed on. Together with immunologist Drew Weissman, she discovered that slightly modifying mRNA's building blocks prevented it from triggering a dangerous immune reaction in the body. At the time, this breakthrough attracted little attention. Decades later, however, it became the foundation for the mRNA vaccines that protected millions of lives during the COVID-19 pandemic. In 2023, Karikó and Weissman were awarded the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine. Her story is a powerful reminder that scientific progress often depends not only on brilliant ideas, but on the courage to endure years of doubt.
reading.title: The Scientist Who Refused to Give Up
